basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Nisāʾ — The Women — is a great Medinan sūra of 176 verses, laying the foundations of justice in human bonds: inheritance, marriage, the protection of orphans and the vulnerable, equity among all. Yet beneath the letter of the law Qushayrī hears a summons to a justice of the heart: whoever guards against wronging others first learns not to wrong his own soul by forgetting his Lord. The sūra opens on the unity of origin — a single soul from which God brought forth all humankind — and unfolds taqwā as inner vigilance: God is al-Raqīb, the Watchful, who numbers your breaths and brings forth your every movement; whoever knows this cannot but feel shame before Him. Among the legal verses rise the great spiritual themes: obedience to God and His Messenger, the unmasking of hypocrisy, the call to reconciliation, and the defense of ʿĪsā against those who denied him.
themes: the unity of human origin (a single soul)
justice toward orphans and the weak
the law of inheritance and marriage
taqwā as vigilance of the heart
obedience to God and the Messenger
the rebuttal of denials about ʿĪsā

versets.ar: يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ نَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ وَخَلَقَ مِنْها زَوْجَها وَبَثَّ مِنْهُما رِجالاً كَثِيراً وَنِساءً وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ الَّذِي تَسائَلُونَ بِهِ وَالْأَرْحامَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ عَلَيْكُمْ رَقِيباً
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu ttaqū rabbakumu lladhī khalaqakum min nafsin wāḥidatin wa-khalaqa minhā zawjahā wa-batha minhumā rijālan kathīran wa-nisāʾan wa-ttaqū llāha lladhī tasāʾalūna bihi wa-l-arḥāma inna llāha kāna ʿalaykum raqīban
versets.trad: O mankind! Be mindful of your Lord, who created you from a single soul. From it He created its mate, and from the two of them He scattered abroad a multitude of men and women. Be mindful of God, in whose name you appeal to one another, and honour the ties of kinship. God, assuredly, watches over you attentively.
versets.commentaire: From the word "mankind" (al-nās) Qushayrī draws a play on forgetting (nisyān): you were named "man" because of your forgetfulness; if you forget Me, nothing is more abject than you; yet whoever forgets Me, his suffering is without end, and whoever forgets the creation rises without limit. To the sinners He says: "O you who have abandoned My covenant and overstepped My bound, it is time to return to My door." To the knowers: "O you who have forgotten your own share in Us, your claim upon Us has become immense." Mindfulness (taqwā) embraces every obedience: its beginning is the abandonment of associating partners with God, its end the abandonment of every "other" than God — and the first of those "others" is your own self. "He watches over you": He numbers your breaths, sees your senses, prompts your movements and your rest; whoever knows this, it befits him to feel shame before Him.

mots.racine: ن و س / أ ن س
mots.def: an address to humanity; Qushayrī links it to intimacy (uns) and to forgetting (nisyān).

mots.racine: و ق ي / ر ب ب
mots.def: taqwā: reverential awe that guards against every "other."

mots.racine: ن ف س / و ح د
mots.def: the one soul: Adam, from whom the whole species proceeds.

mots.racine: ر ح م
mots.def: the wombs, the bonds of relationship that must not be severed.

mots.racine: ر ق ب
mots.def: the vigilant Witness who numbers the breaths.

versets.ar: وَآتُوا الْيَتامى أَمْوالَهُمْ وَلا تَتَبَدَّلُوا الْخَبِيثَ بِالطَّيِّبِ وَلا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوالَهُمْ إِلى أَمْوالِكُمْ إِنَّهُ كانَ حُوباً كَبِيراً
versets.tr: wa-ātū l-yatāmā amwālahum wa-lā tatabaddalū l-khabītha bi-ṭ-ṭayyibi wa-lā taʾkulū amwālahum ilā amwālikum innahu kāna ḥūban kabīran
versets.trad: Give the orphans the property that belongs to them. Do not exchange what is good in their estate for what is bad, and do not consume their wealth by adding it to your own: that would truly be a grievous sin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever has been set in a position of protection and then abused the one in his charge, it is his Lord who becomes his adversary — for He, glorified be He, avenges His servants as He does not avenge for His own sake. The guardian of the orphan: if he is just and beneficent, his reward falls to God; if he is unjust and transgresses, it is God who will bring the case against him. The orphan's property is a trust that no human eye defends: God alone stands surety for it.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ي ت م
mots.def: to restore to the orphans their property.

mots.racine: خ ب ث / ط ي ب
mots.def: to substitute a worthless asset for an excellent one in the orphan's estate.

mots.racine: ح و ب / ك ب ر
mots.def: ḥūb: a heavy fault, a major transgression.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلاَّ تُقْسِطُوا فِي الْيَتامى فَانْكِحُوا ما طابَ لَكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ مَثْنى وَثُلاثَ وَرُباعَ فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلاَّ تَعْدِلُوا فَواحِدَةً أَوْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ ذلِكَ أَدْنى أَلاَّ تَعُولُوا
versets.tr: wa-in khiftum allā tuqsiṭū fī l-yatāmā fa-nkiḥū mā ṭāba lakum mina n-nisāʾi mathnā wa-thulātha wa-rubāʿa fa-in khiftum allā taʿdilū fa-wāḥidatan aw mā malakat aymānukum dhālika adnā allā taʿūlū
versets.trad: If you fear that you will not deal fairly with the orphans, then marry such women as seem good to you, two, three or four. But if you fear that you will not be equitable [towards them], then only one, or take a wife from among those whom your right hand possesses. That is more likely to keep you from doing injustice.
versets.commentaire: God permitted free men to marry up to four wives, but He made equity between them obligatory. Qushayrī draws from this a rule of the inner life: the servant must first consider what is obligatory; if he knows himself able to fulfil its due, let him then make use of the permitted; but if he senses he will fall short of the duty, let him abstain from the permitted, for of the obligatory he will be questioned. Thus a licence is worth nothing unless borne by faithfulness to duty.

mots.racine: ق س ط
mots.sens: that you will not be fair

mots.racine: ث ن ي / ث ل ث / ر ب ع
mots.def: distributive numbers setting a limit to polygamy.

mots.racine: ع و ل
mots.def: taʿūlū: to incline, to deviate from justice.

versets.ar: وَآتُوا النِّساءَ صَدُقاتِهِنَّ نِحْلَةً فَإِنْ طِبْنَ لَكُمْ عَنْ شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ نَفْساً فَكُلُوهُ هَنِيئاً مَرِيئاً
versets.tr: wa-ātū n-nisāʾa ṣaduqātihinna niḥlatan fa-in ṭibna lakum ʿan shayʾin minhu nafsan fa-kulūhu hanīʾan marīʾan
versets.trad: Give women their dowries as a gift; but if of their own goodwill they remit to you some part of it, then enjoy it with ease and serenity.
versets.commentaire: The verse teaches that what is given wholeheartedly becomes wholesome. Qushayrī: the food of the noble and the generous is sound, for they give only from a contented soul; the food of the miserly is tainted, for they give only under constraint, without goodwill. And he cites the Prophet — peace be upon him: "the food of the generous is a remedy, the food of the miser is a sickness." The dowry offered without reluctance carries the blessing of liberality.

mots.racine: ص د ق
mots.def: ṣadāq / ṣaduqa: the nuptial gift due to the wife.

mots.racine: ن ح ل
mots.def: a gracious gift, a free offering without counterpart.

mots.racine: ه ن أ / م ر أ
mots.def: of agreeable and beneficial use, untroubled.

versets.ar: وَلا تُؤْتُوا السُّفَهاءَ أَمْوالَكُمُ الَّتِي جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ قِياماً وَارْزُقُوهُمْ فِيها وَاكْسُوهُمْ وَقُولُوا لَهُمْ قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً
versets.tr: wa-lā tuʾtū s-sufahāʾa amwālakumu llatī jaʿala llāhu lakum qiyāman wa-rzuqūhum fīhā wa-ksūhum wa-qūlū lahum qawlan maʿrūfan
versets.trad: Do not entrust to those who lack judgement the property that God has placed in your charge for their support; but provide for them out of it, clothe them, and address to them seemly words.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shifts the "fool" (al-safīh) toward the inward: the fool is the one who turns you from the Real and occupies you far from the Lord; and among your kin, the fool is the one whose interests you prefer to the rights of God. As for the wealth "that God has made an upholding for you," preserving one's countenance is better than exposing oneself to begging and cunning; giving is better than self-restraint only for the freed heart, sure of its patience. And he adds: if your soul urges you to spend on the vain, then you are the most foolish of fools — do not obey it.

mots.racine: س ف ه
mots.def: sufahāʾ: the weak of mind, unable to manage; in Qushayrī, whatever turns one from God.

mots.racine: ق و م
mots.def: that by which material existence subsists.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ع ر ف
mots.def: fitting speech, in keeping with what is recognised as good.

versets.ar: وَابْتَلُوا الْيَتامى حَتَّى إِذا بَلَغُوا النِّكاحَ فَإِنْ آنَسْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ رُشْداً فَادْفَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوالَهُمْ وَلا تَأْكُلُوها إِسْرافاً وَبِداراً أَنْ يَكْبَرُوا وَمَنْ كانَ غَنِيًّا فَلْيَسْتَعْفِفْ وَمَنْ كانَ فَقِيراً فَلْيَأْكُلْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ فَإِذا دَفَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوالَهُمْ فَأَشْهِدُوا عَلَيْهِمْ وَكَفى بِاللَّهِ حَسِيباً
versets.tr: wa-btalū l-yatāmā ḥattā idhā balaghū n-nikāḥa fa-in ānastum minhum rushdan fa-dfaʿū ilayhim amwālahum wa-lā taʾkulūhā isrāfan wa-bidāran an yakbarū wa-man kāna ghaniyyan fa-l-yastaʿfif wa-man kāna faqīran fa-l-yaʾkul bi-l-maʿrūfi fa-idhā dafaʿtum ilayhim amwālahum fa-ashhidū ʿalayhim wa-kafā bi-llāhi ḥasīban
versets.trad: Test the orphans until they reach marriageable age. If you discern in them sound judgement, hand over to them the property that belongs to them. Do not consume it wastefully and in haste before they grow up. Let the one who is rich abstain from it, and let the one who is poor use it with moderation. When you hand over their property to them, call witnesses; yet God suffices to keep the reckoning.
versets.commentaire: For Qushayrī, to detect "sound judgement" (rushd) in the orphan is to recognise in him chastity and religion, generosity and modesty, the company of the masters, and an ardent longing to contemplate the good and to perform worship as it ought to be. And he raises the notion higher: the truly upright is the one who has been guided to his Lord; when a need befalls him, he does not lean on his own strength, skill and choosing, but entrusts himself to God. "God suffices as reckoner": the ultimate witness of every restitution.

mots.racine: ب ل و
mots.def: to put to the test, to try one's capacity.

mots.racine: أ ن س / ر ش د
mots.def: rushd: soundness of judgement and conduct.

mots.racine: ع ف ف
mots.def: to refrain, out of decency, from touching the orphan's property.

mots.racine: ح س ب
mots.def: the One who keeps the exact account of all things.

versets.ar: لِلرِّجالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ وَلِلنِّساءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ نَصِيباً مَفْرُوضاً
versets.tr: li-r-rijāli naṣībun mimmā taraka l-wālidāni wa-l-aqrabūna wa-li-n-nisāʾi naṣībun mimmā taraka l-wālidāni wa-l-aqrabūna mimmā qalla minhu aw kathura naṣīban mafrūḍan
versets.trad: To men belongs a share of what their parents and near kin leave behind, and to women belongs a share of what their parents and near kin leave behind, be it little or much; a share ordained.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that inheritance does not vary according to merit or nobility, nor diminish for fault or defect: though one son be pious and the other rebellious, they inherit in equal shares. And from this he draws a secret: inheritance is first of all an original gift coming from God — that is why the pious and the depraved are made equal in it; and likewise faith is a first gift granted to the believers, as God says: "Then We caused those whom We chose among Our servants to inherit the Book."

mots.racine: ن ص ب
mots.def: the portion assigned in the estate.

mots.racine: و ل د / ق ر ب
ن ص ب / ف ر ض
mots.def: a portion obligatorily fixed by the Law.

versets.ar: وَإِذا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُوا الْقُرْبى وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينُ فَارْزُقُوهُمْ مِنْهُ وَقُولُوا لَهُمْ قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً
versets.tr: wa-idhā ḥaḍara l-qismata ulū l-qurbā wa-l-yatāmā wa-l-masākīnu fa-rzuqūhum minhu wa-qūlū lahum qawlan maʿrūfan
versets.trad: If kinsfolk, orphans and the poor are present at the division, give them something from the inheritance, and address to them seemly words.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī perceives in the division of the inheritance a subtle allusion to the Day of reckoning. Just as the poor present at the division are not sent away with nothing, on the Day when the sinners are brought forth onto the plain and the Real forgives the obedient and gives them the reward of their works, those among you who are among the poor of the believers will not be denied forgiveness. And he recalls: on the day of the first apportioning you were not present, you held no acquired right — it was by pure grace that He made you worthy to know Him, already knowing your future stumblings.

mots.racine: ح ض ر / ق س م
mots.def: to be present at the moment of dividing the estate.

mots.racine: ق ر ب
mots.def: non-inheriting relatives present at the division.

mots.racine: س ك ن
ر ز ق
mots.def: to give them something from the inheritance.

versets.ar: وَلْيَخْشَ الَّذِينَ لَوْ تَرَكُوا مِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّةً ضِعافاً خافُوا عَلَيْهِمْ فَلْيَتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَلْيَقُولُوا قَوْلاً سَدِيداً
versets.tr: wa-l-yakhsha lladhīna law tarakū min khalfihim dhurriyyatan ḍiʿāfan khāfū ʿalayhim fa-l-yattaqū llāha wa-l-yaqūlū qawlan sadīdan
versets.trad: Let those who would leave behind them vulnerable offspring, and would be anxious for their sake, hold God in pious awe and speak words that are just.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes that God does not say "let them amass wealth, multiply estates and leave furnishings," but: "let them be in awe of God." The verse thus shows that what the believer must bequeath to his offspring is reverential awe and rectitude, not fortune — for it is He who takes the virtuous into His charge. The true inheritance is invisible: taqwā entrusted as a legacy.

mots.racine: خ ش ي
ذ ر ر / ض ع ف
ق و ل / س د د
mots.def: upright, pertinent speech that strikes true.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوالَ الْيَتامى ظُلْماً إِنَّما يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ ناراً وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيراً
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yaʾkulūna amwāla l-yatāmā ẓulman innamā yaʾkulūna fī buṭūnihim nāran wa-sa-yaṣlawna saʿīran
versets.trad: Those who unjustly devour the property of orphans swallow only fire into their bellies, and they will be made to roast in a blazing Fire.
themes: God the avenger of the orphan
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains why God makes Himself the adversary of whoever plunders the orphan: because the orphan has none other than Him; and whoever commits his affair to God, divesting himself of his own strength, it is the Real who avenges him as He does not avenge for His own sake. An allusion addressed to the saints (awliyāʾ): they too have no support in faction, in power, or in any creature; so when wronged, it is God who takes up their cause.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ي ت م
mots.def: to consume unjustly the orphans' estate.

mots.racine: ظ ل م
mots.def: through iniquity, by violating the right.

mots.racine: ن و ر
mots.def: the plundered wealth turns to fire within the belly.

mots.racine: س ع ر

versets.ar: يُوصِيكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ فَإِنْ كُنَّ نِساءً فَوْقَ اثْنَتَيْنِ فَلَهُنَّ ثُلُثا ما تَرَكَ وَإِنْ كانَتْ واحِدَةً فَلَهَا النِّصْفُ وَلِأَبَوَيْهِ لِكُلِّ واحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ مِمَّا تَرَكَ إِنْ كانَ لَهُ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلَدٌ وَوَرِثَهُ أَبَواهُ فَلِأُمِّهِ الثُّلُثُ فَإِنْ كانَ لَهُ إِخْوَةٌ فَلِأُمِّهِ السُّدُسُ مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِي بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ آباؤُكُمْ وَأَبْناؤُكُمْ لا تَدْرُونَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ لَكُمْ نَفْعاً فَرِيضَةً مِنَ اللَّهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ عَلِيماً حَكِيماً
versets.tr: yūṣīkumu llāhu fī awlādikum li-dh-dhakari mithlu ḥaẓẓi l-unthayayni fa-in kunna nisāʾan fawqa thnatayni fa-lahunna thuluthā mā taraka wa-in kānat wāḥidatan fa-lahā n-niṣfu wa-li-abawayhi li-kulli wāḥidin minhumā s-sudusu mimmā taraka in kāna lahu waladun fa-in lam yakun lahu waladun wa-warithahu abawāhu fa-li-ummihi th-thuluthu fa-in kāna lahu ikhwatun fa-li-ummihi s-sudusu min baʿdi waṣiyyatin yūṣī bihā aw daynin ābāʾukum wa-abnāʾukum lā tadrūna ayyuhum aqrabu lakum nafʿan farīḍatan mina llāhi inna llāha kāna ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: Concerning your children, God enjoins upon you: to the male a share equal to that of two females. If there are more than two daughters, two thirds of the estate shall be theirs; if there is but one, half shall be hers. To each of his parents a sixth of the estate, if the deceased has left a child. If he has no child and his parents are his heirs, a third goes to his mother. If he has brothers, a sixth goes to his mother, after any bequest he may have made or any debt has been settled. Of your ascendants and your descendants, you do not know which are of greater benefit to you. This is an obligation imposed by God; truly God is all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: Before the law of inheritance Qushayrī bows: were the matter left to analogy, woman, by her weakness, would deserve more; but the decree of God — glorified be He — is motivated by no cause. This is the secret of the divine transcendence: His Law does not answer to our reason. And on "you do not know which are of greater benefit to you," he notes tenderly: children benefit you by their service, parents by their mercy; the parents in the time of your first weakness, the children in the time of your last.

mots.racine: و ص ي
mots.def: waṣiyya: here in the sense of command, prescription.

mots.racine: ح ظ ظ / أ ن ث
mots.def: the proportion fixed for the son.

mots.racine: ف ر ض
mots.def: a binding decree, not subject to reason.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ك م
mots.def: two Names: infinite Knowledge and Wisdom.

versets.ar: وَلَكُمْ نِصْفُ ما تَرَكَ أَزْواجُكُمْ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُنَّ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ كانَ لَهُنَّ وَلَدٌ فَلَكُمُ الرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْنَ مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِينَ بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ وَلَهُنَّ الرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ كانَ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌ فَلَهُنَّ الثُّمُنُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ تُوصُونَ بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ وَإِنْ كانَ رَجُلٌ يُورَثُ كَلالَةً أَوِ امْرَأَةٌ وَلَهُ أَخٌ أَوْ أُخْتٌ فَلِكُلِّ واحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ فَإِنْ كانُوا أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذلِكَ فَهُمْ شُرَكاءُ فِي الثُّلُثِ مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصى بِها أَوْ دَيْنٍ غَيْرَ مُضَارٍّ وَصِيَّةً مِنَ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lakum niṣfu mā taraka azwājukum in lam yakun lahunna waladun fa-in kāna lahunna waladun fa-lakumu r-rubuʿu mimmā tarakna min baʿdi waṣiyyatin yūṣīna bihā aw daynin wa-lahunna r-rubuʿu mimmā taraktum in lam yakun lakum waladun fa-in kāna lakum waladun fa-lahunna th-thumunu mimmā taraktum min baʿdi waṣiyyatin tūṣūna bihā aw daynin wa-in kāna rajulun yūrathu kalālatan awi mraʾatun wa-lahu akhun aw ukhtun fa-li-kulli wāḥidin minhumā s-sudusu fa-in kānū akthara min dhālika fa-hum shurakāʾu fī th-thuluthi min baʿdi waṣiyyatin yūṣā bihā aw daynin ghayra muḍārrin waṣiyyatan mina llāhi wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥalīmun
versets.trad: If your wives have no child, half of what they leave is yours. If they have a child, a quarter of what they leave is yours, after any bequest or debt has been settled. If you have no child, a quarter of what you leave goes to your wives. If you have a child, they have an eighth of what you leave, after any bequest or debt has been settled. If a man or a woman dies leaving neither ascendants nor descendants, but has a brother or a sister [on the mother's side], each of them has a sixth. If they are more, they share a third of the estate, after any bequest or debt has been settled, without harm to anyone. This is an injunction from God. God is all-knowing, forbearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the law of inheritance a reason of the heart: when the dead one dies, the near kinsman bears the weight of his sorrows; God then recompenses the heir for what he endures and for what afflicts his heart, by the wealth bequeathed. And he holds to his constant insistence: such is His way — to reward affliction by pure liberality, not by any obligation that would weigh upon Him, as some imagine. The closer one is by blood or the stronger by tie, the greater one's claim to inheritance.

mots.racine: ك ل ل
mots.def: kalāla: a deceased without father or direct child.

mots.racine: ش ر ك / ث ل ث
mots.def: co-partakers of one third of the estate.

mots.racine: ض ر ر
mots.def: without wronging anyone by the bequest or the debt.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ل م

versets.ar: تِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللَّهِ وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: tilka ḥudūdu llāhi wa-man yuṭiʿi llāha wa-rasūlahu yudkhilhu jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā wa-dhālika l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīmu
versets.trad: These are the bounds set by God. Whoever obeys God and His Messenger, He will admit him into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, there to abide for ever. That is the supreme triumph!
versets.commentaire: "The bounds of God" are, says Qushayrī, His commands and His prohibitions, and all whereby He has called His servants to worship Him. And he makes them the very foundation of servanthood: the principle of servanthood is to keep the bounds and to preserve the covenants; whoever keeps his bound, no harm nor calamity reaches him; and the root of every trial is the overstepping of bounds. To abide within the appointed threshold is to abide in peace.

mots.racine: ح د د
mots.def: ḥudūd: prescriptions and prohibitions that set the limits of conduct.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ر س ل
mots.def: to conform to the divine and prophetic command.

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَمَنْ يَعْصِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَيَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَهُ يُدْخِلْهُ ناراً خالِداً فِيها وَلَهُ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-man yaʿṣi llāha wa-rasūlahu wa-yataʿadda ḥudūdahu yudkhilhu nāran khālidan fīhā wa-lahu ʿadhābun muhīnun
versets.trad: But whoever disobeys God and His Messenger and transgresses the bounds set by God, He will make him enter a Fire, there to abide for ever, suffering an abasing chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes in the chastisement of the disobedient two penalties: one hastened, the other deferred — and to both clings humiliation. For sin bears within itself its own degradation: if all creatures strove to debase acts of disobedience as much as the one who commits them already debases them, they would not succeed. The sinner passes the night at ease and wakes covered with his own baseness, while the man of good wakes haloed with the dignity of his deed.

mots.racine: ع ص ي
mots.def: ʿiṣyān: rebellion against the divine command.

mots.racine: ع د و / ح د د
mots.def: to overstep the limits fixed by God.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: وَاللاَّتِي يَأْتِينَ الْفاحِشَةَ مِنْ نِسائِكُمْ فَاسْتَشْهِدُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ أَرْبَعَةً مِنْكُمْ فَإِنْ شَهِدُوا فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ فِي الْبُيُوتِ حَتَّى يَتَوَفَّاهُنَّ الْمَوْتُ أَوْ يَجْعَلَ اللَّهُ لَهُنَّ سَبِيلاً
versets.tr: wa-llātī yaʾtīna l-fāḥishata min nisāʾikum fa-stashhidū ʿalayhinna arbaʿatan minkum fa-in shahidū fa-amsikūhunna fī l-buyūti ḥattā yatawaffāhunna l-mawtu aw yajʿala llāhu lahunna sabīlan
versets.trad: Against those of your women who commit the indecency, call four witnesses from among you; if they bear witness [against them], confine them to their houses until death takes them or until God appoints for them a way out.
versets.commentaire: Why require four witnesses? Qushayrī reads in it an act of divine modesty: the condition of multiple witnesses was set in order to spread the veil of generosity over the faults of the servants; for to gather witnesses in the manner the Law requires is all but impossible. And he recalls how the Prophet — peace be upon him — by his questions turned aside the one who accused himself, sparing him a refuge in confession: a shining proof that He unfolds His veil over ugly acts.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ف ح ش
mots.def: fāḥisha: a gravely obscene act, here fornication.

mots.racine: ش ه د
م س ك
mots.def: to confine them to their houses (a measure later abrogated).

mots.racine: س ب ل
mots.def: a way of release that God will appoint (the later legal penalty).

versets.ar: وَالَّذانِ يَأْتِيانِها مِنْكُمْ فَآذُوهُما فَإِنْ تابا وَأَصْلَحا فَأَعْرِضُوا عَنْهُما إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ تَوَّاباً رَحِيماً
versets.tr: wa-lladhāni yaʾtiyānihā minkum fa-ādhūhumā fa-in tābā wa-aṣlaḥā fa-aʿriḍū ʿanhumā inna llāha kāna tawwāban raḥīman
versets.trad: And if two of you commit it, punish them both; but if they repent and amend, then let them be! God is the One who relents, the Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī understands the penalty as a pedagogy of deterrence: the command of various chastisements is the most eloquent means of restraining and turning away from the fault, so that the servant may guard against it and not incur the greater torment. The severity of this world is mercy veiled: it aims to spare the final agony. And as soon as repentance and amendment come, the command becomes: "turn away from them" — for God Himself is Tawwāb, the One who never ceases to accept the return.

mots.racine: أ ت ي
mots.def: dual: the two perpetrators of the indecency.

mots.racine: أ ذ ي
ت و ب / ص ل ح
mots.def: return to God followed by the reform of deeds.

mots.racine: ت و ب / ر ح م
mots.def: two Names: the One who returns ceaselessly toward the repentant, and the Merciful.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا التَّوْبَةُ عَلَى اللَّهِ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السُّوءَ بِجَهالَةٍ ثُمَّ يَتُوبُونَ مِنْ قَرِيبٍ فَأُولئِكَ يَتُوبُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيماً حَكِيماً
versets.tr: innamā t-tawbatu ʿalā llāhi li-lladhīna yaʿmalūna s-sūʾa bi-jahālatin thumma yatūbūna min qarībin fa-ulāʾika yatūbu llāhu ʿalayhim wa-kāna llāhu ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: The accepting of repentance rests with God only for those who do evil out of ignorance and then repent soon afterward. To these God relents; God is all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī states at the outset: there is no asking of pardon while persisting in the fault — repentance without rupture bears the seal of the liars. "Evil out of ignorance" is the deed of the heedless. And he refines it by states: "then repent soon after" means, in the tongue of the scholars: before death; and in the tongue of the Way: before the soul takes the fault into a habit that becomes a second nature. And he cites this verse: "I said to the soul: if you would return, return before the road is closed." Even the elect have their sin: to believe that by their obediences they merit a rank — an illusion concerning their station.

mots.racine: ت و ب
mots.def: the acceptance of repentance, which falls to the grace of God.

mots.racine: ج ه ل
mots.def: jahāla: to act as the heedless act.

mots.racine: ق ر ب
mots.def: before death, and before the fault becomes habit.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَلَيْسَتِ التَّوْبَةُ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السَّيِّئاتِ حَتَّى إِذا حَضَرَ أَحَدَهُمُ الْمَوْتُ قالَ إِنِّي تُبْتُ الْآنَ وَلا الَّذِينَ يَمُوتُونَ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ أُولئِكَ أَعْتَدْنا لَهُمْ عَذاباً أَلِيماً
versets.tr: wa-laysati t-tawbatu li-lladhīna yaʿmalūna s-sayyiʾāti ḥattā idhā ḥaḍara aḥadahumu l-mawtu qāla innī tubtu l-āna wa-lā lladhīna yamūtūna wa-hum kuffārun ulāʾika aʿtadnā lahum ʿadhāban alīman
versets.trad: But there is no acceptance of repentance for those who do evil until, when death presents itself to one of them, he cries: "Now indeed I repent!" Nor for those who die as unbelievers. For such We have prepared a painful chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains why the repentance of the death-throes is refused: when the veil is lifted and the things known become compelled certainty, the door of repentance closes; for a condition of the trial is that faith remain faith in the unseen. Repentance has worth only so long as it springs from the hidden. And he evokes David: long after the pardon received, he still wept; God said to him: "Why do you weep, when I have forgiven you?" — "Lord, the time of intimacy, will You restore it?" — "Far from it, O David: that time is past."

mots.racine: ع م ل / س و أ
ح ض ر / م و ت
mots.sens: death presents itself to one of them

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَنْ تَرِثُوا النِّساءَ كَرْهاً وَلا تَعْضُلُوهُنَّ لِتَذْهَبُوا بِبَعْضِ ما آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَأْتِينَ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ وَعاشِرُوهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ فَإِنْ كَرِهْتُمُوهُنَّ فَعَسى أَنْ تَكْرَهُوا شَيْئاً وَيَجْعَلَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ خَيْراً كَثِيراً
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā yaḥillu lakum an tarithū n-nisāʾa karhan wa-lā taʿḍulūhunna li-tadhhabū bi-baʿḍi mā ātaytumūhunna illā an yaʾtīna bi-fāḥishatin mubayyinatin wa-ʿāshirūhunna bi-l-maʿrūfi fa-in karihtumūhunna fa-ʿasā an takrahū shayʾan wa-yajʿala llāhu fīhi khayran kathīran
versets.trad: O you who believe! It is not lawful for you to take women as an inheritance against their will, nor to coerce them so as to take back part of what you have given them, unless they have committed a manifest indecency. Live with them in kindness. If you feel an aversion for them, it may be that you feel aversion for a thing in which God has placed much good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes "live with them in kindness" a school of patience: to live with them according to the teachings of religion and fair companionship, even when the soul recoils; to bear their hardship without imposing on them the burden of serving you, and to close your eyes to what makes them blush. And on the hidden good: the more a thing is grievous to the soul, the sweeter and more wholesome its outcome; God — glorified be He — has handed to no one the secret of His unseen: often what man abhors holds what is best, for to thwart the soul leads to the highest stations.

mots.racine: و ر ث / ك ر ه
mots.def: the pre-Islamic practice of appropriating the widow.

mots.racine: ع ض ل
mots.def: to prevent, to harass in order to recover the dowry.

mots.racine: ع ش ر / ع ر ف
خ ي ر / ك ث ر
mots.def: abundant good hidden in what one dislikes.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ أَرَدْتُمُ اسْتِبْدالَ زَوْجٍ مَكانَ زَوْجٍ وَآتَيْتُمْ إِحْداهُنَّ قِنْطاراً فَلا تَأْخُذُوا مِنْهُ شَيْئاً أَتَأْخُذُونَهُ بُهْتاناً وَإِثْماً مُبِيناً
versets.tr: wa-in aradtumu stibdāla zawjin makāna zawjin wa-ātaytum iḥdāhunna qinṭāran fa-lā taʾkhudhū minhu shayʾan a-taʾkhudhūnahu buhtānan wa-ithman mubīnan
versets.trad: If you wish to take one wife in place of another, even though you have given the first a heap of gold, take nothing back from it. Would you take it back through slander, inflicting a manifest wrong?
versets.commentaire: The verse, says Qushayrī, teaches the believers the beauty of fidelity to the covenant and nobility in the shared life: do not add to the separation the taking back of the wealth given, for that would be to abandon generosity. If you have lavished abundant wealth upon one, then wounded her by divorce, what you gave her is a small thing beside the bitterness of the separation you make her taste. The gift once offered belongs henceforth to the one who received it.

mots.racine: ب د ل / ز و ج
mots.def: to substitute one wife for another.

mots.racine: ق ن ط ر
mots.def: a great quantity of gold; a considerable dowry.

mots.racine: ب ه ت / أ ث م / ب ي ن
mots.def: to take back the gift would be imposture and a clear fault.

versets.ar: وَكَيْفَ تَأْخُذُونَهُ وَقَدْ أَفْضى بَعْضُكُمْ إِلى بَعْضٍ وَأَخَذْنَ مِنْكُمْ مِيثاقاً غَلِيظاً
versets.tr: wa-kayfa taʾkhudhūnahu wa-qad afḍā baʿḍukum ilā baʿḍin wa-akhadhna minkum mīthāqan ghalīẓan
versets.trad: How could you take it back, when you have given yourselves to one another, and your wives have received from you a solemn pledge?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the companionship that has passed has a firm inviolability; hold then to the honouring of the bond and fulfil the pledge. The intimacy shared creates a debt of honour that separation cannot erase. The "solemn pledge" (mīthāq ghalīẓ) of marriage echoes, in the Sufi tongue, the great primordial Covenant: every bond to which God is witness binds one to loyalty.

mots.racine: ف ض و
mots.sens: you have given yourselves to one another

mots.racine: و ث ق / غ ل ظ

versets.ar: وَلا تَنْكِحُوا ما نَكَحَ آباؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِلاَّ ما قَدْ سَلَفَ إِنَّهُ كانَ فاحِشَةً وَمَقْتاً وَساءَ سَبِيلاً
versets.tr: wa-lā tankiḥū mā nakaḥa ābāʾukum mina n-nisāʾi illā mā qad salafa innahu kāna fāḥishatan wa-maqtan wa-sāʾa sabīlan
versets.trad: Do not marry women whom your fathers have married, save what is already past; that was truly an indecency, a hateful act and an evil practice.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in the prohibition the safeguarding of honour and the keeping of the bounds of respect: noble nature of itself recoils from another taking its couch; so the sons are barred from encroaching on the rights of the fathers by taking the father's wife. The legal prohibition here weds the innate delicacy of the upright heart.

mots.racine: ن ك ح / أ ب و
mots.def: the former wives of the father.

mots.racine: س ل ف
mots.def: unions prior to the prohibition are not penalised.

mots.racine: ف ح ش / م ق ت
mots.def: an abominable obscenity, an object of divine loathing.

versets.ar: حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهاتُكُمْ وَبَناتُكُمْ وَأَخَواتُكُمْ وَعَمَّاتُكُمْ وَخالاتُكُمْ وَبَناتُ الْأَخِ وَبَناتُ الْأُخْتِ وَأُمَّهاتُكُمُ اللاَّتِي أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ وَأَخَواتُكُمْ مِنَ الرَّضاعَةِ وَأُمَّهاتُ نِسائِكُمْ وَرَبائِبُكُمُ اللاَّتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ مِنْ نِسائِكُمُ اللاَّتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَحَلائِلُ أَبْنائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلابِكُمْ وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ إِلاَّ ما قَدْ سَلَفَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً
versets.tr: ḥurrimat ʿalaykum ummahātukum wa-banātukum wa-akhawātukum wa-ʿammātukum wa-khālātukum wa-banātu l-akhi wa-banātu l-ukhti wa-ummahātukumu llātī arḍaʿnakum wa-akhawātukum mina r-raḍāʿati wa-ummahātu nisāʾikum wa-rabāʾibukumu llātī fī ḥujūrikum min nisāʾikumu llātī dakhaltum bihinna fa-in lam takūnū dakhaltum bihinna fa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum wa-ḥalāʾilu abnāʾikumu lladhīna min aṣlābikum wa-an tajmaʿū bayna l-ukhtayni illā mā qad salafa inna llāha kāna ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: Forbidden to you are: your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, your maternal aunts, your brothers' daughters, your sisters' daughters, the mothers who suckled you, your foster-sisters, the mothers of your wives, your stepdaughters under your guardianship, born of wives with whom you have consummated marriage — though there is no fault upon you if you have not consummated it — and the wives of your sons, born of your own loins. It is also forbidden to you to have two sisters as wives at one time, save what is already past. God is in truth forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Before the list of prohibitions, Qushayrī affirms the pure sovereignty of the Law: to seek to wrest out by force the causes for which this prohibition was established is impossible, for the Law (sharʿ) is not motivated by any reason; the Real has forbidden what He willed to whom He willed, and likewise for the permitted. No cause grounds the prescriptions: had God made these same women lawful, that too would have been lawful. This is the absolute freedom of the Lawgiver.

mots.racine: ح ر م
ر ض ع
mots.def: the kinship of milk creating the same prohibitions as blood.

mots.racine: ر ب ب
mots.def: the wife's daughters raised under guardianship.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَالْمُحْصَناتُ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِلاَّ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ كِتابَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأُحِلَّ لَكُمْ ما وَراءَ ذلِكُمْ أَنْ تَبْتَغُوا بِأَمْوالِكُمْ مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسافِحِينَ فَمَا اسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْهُنَّ فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً وَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيما تَراضَيْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرِيضَةِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ عَلِيماً حَكِيماً
versets.tr: wa-l-muḥṣanātu mina n-nisāʾi illā mā malakat aymānukum kitāba llāhi ʿalaykum wa-uḥilla lakum mā warāʾa dhālikum an tabtaghū bi-amwālikum muḥṣinīna ghayra musāfiḥīna fa-mā stamtaʿtum bihi minhunna fa-ātūhunna ujūrahunna farīḍatan wa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum fīmā tarāḍaytum bihi min baʿdi l-farīḍati inna llāha kāna ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: [Forbidden to you also] are married women, except those whom your right hand possesses. This is what God has prescribed for you. Beyond the prohibitions mentioned, it is lawful for you to seek, with your wealth, an honourable marriage, with no thought of debauchery. To the women whose intimacy you seek, give the prescribed dowry. There is no fault upon you if, after agreeing on the dowry, you decide by mutual consent to alter it. God is all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the condition of the peaceful licit: when the bounds are kept, the covenants honoured, and mutual consent established according to the Law, then there remains neither possible dispute among creatures nor reproach from the Real — and it becomes a pure and free permission. The purity of intention and the loyalty of the contract make the union without shadow.

mots.racine: ح ص ن
mots.def: women protected by a marriage bond.

mots.racine: ك ت ب
mots.sens: the prescription of God upon you

mots.racine: ح ص ن / س ف ح
أ ج ر / ف ر ض
mots.def: the nuptial gift due as an obligation.

versets.ar: وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ مِنْكُمْ طَوْلاً أَنْ يَنْكِحَ الْمُحْصَناتِ الْمُؤْمِناتِ فَمِنْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ مِنْ فَتَياتِكُمُ الْمُؤْمِناتِ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِإِيمانِكُمْ بَعْضُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ فَانْكِحُوهُنَّ بِإِذْنِ أَهْلِهِنَّ وَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ مُحْصَناتٍ غَيْرَ مُسافِحاتٍ وَلا مُتَّخِذاتِ أَخْدانٍ فَإِذا أُحْصِنَّ فَإِنْ أَتَيْنَ بِفاحِشَةٍ فَعَلَيْهِنَّ نِصْفُ ما عَلَى الْمُحْصَناتِ مِنَ الْعَذابِ ذلِكَ لِمَنْ خَشِيَ الْعَنَتَ مِنْكُمْ وَأَنْ تَصْبِرُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-man lam yastaṭiʿ minkum ṭawlan an yankiḥa l-muḥṣanāti l-muʾmināti fa-min mā malakat aymānukum min fatayātikumu l-muʾmināti wa-llāhu aʿlamu bi-īmānikum baʿḍukum min baʿḍin fa-nkiḥūhunna bi-idhni ahlihinna wa-ātūhunna ujūrahunna bi-l-maʿrūfi muḥṣanātin ghayra musāfiḥātin wa-lā muttakhidhāti akhdānin fa-idhā uḥṣinna fa-in atayna bi-fāḥishatin fa-ʿalayhinna niṣfu mā ʿalā l-muḥṣanāti mina l-ʿadhābi dhālika li-man khashiya l-ʿanata minkum wa-an taṣbirū khayrun lakum wa-llāhu ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Whoever among you cannot afford to marry free believing women, let him take believing maidens from those whom your right hands possess. God knows best your faith; you proceed one from another. Marry them with the permission of their people and give them their dowries in fairness, as chaste women, not as debauched or as keepers of secret lovers. If, after entering into wedlock, they commit an indecency, they incur half the chastisement of free women. This dispensation is for those of you who fear to fall into licentiousness. Yet it is more meritorious for you to be patient. God is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a parable of the stations: the dispensations (rukhaṣ) were instituted for the weak; as for the strong, their affair is resolute effort, vigilance and rigour, for they have no other occupation than to fulfil the right of the Real. Whoever descends from the degree of what is surest and firmest may avail himself of the leniency. And on the close of the verse, he notes a divine tenderness: He did not say "be patient," but "that you be patient is better for you" — a delicate way of drawing the servant without constraining him.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ط و ل
mots.def: ṭawl: means sufficient for the dowry.

mots.racine: ف ت ي / أ م ن
mots.def: young captives or bondwomen of faith.

mots.racine: خ ش ي / ع ن ت
mots.def: ʿanat: hardship, the peril of falling into debauchery.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / خ ي ر
mots.def: patience as the more meritorious way.

versets.ar: يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ لِيُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ وَيَهْدِيَكُمْ سُنَنَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ وَيَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
versets.tr: yurīdu llāhu li-yubayyina lakum wa-yahdiyakum sunana lladhīna min qablikum wa-yatūba ʿalaykum wa-llāhu ʿalīmun ḥakīmun
versets.trad: God wishes to make clear to you the ways of those who lived before you, to guide you and to relent toward you. God is all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: When the Prophet — peace be upon him — had related to his community the history of the bygone peoples and how God had dealt with them, some among them, being sinners, wondered in fear how God would deal with them. The verse reassures them. Qushayrī unfolds its meanings: "God wishes to make clear to you": He unveils to you His secrets and makes appear for you what remained hidden from others; He wishes to manifest to you that He alone creates and ordains, and that none possesses anything. And "relent toward you": He accepts your repentance after having Himself created it in you, then rewards you for the repentance He has fashioned.

mots.racine: ب ي ن
س ن ن / ق ب ل
mots.sens: the ways of those before you
mots.def: sunan: the paths and laws of the predecessors.

mots.racine: ت و ب

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَيُرِيدُ الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ الشَّهَواتِ أَنْ تَمِيلُوا مَيْلاً عَظِيماً
versets.tr: wa-llāhu yurīdu an yatūba ʿalaykum wa-yurīdu lladhīna yattabiʿūna sh-shahawāti an tamīlū maylan ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: God wishes to relent toward you, while those who follow their passions wish you to slide down a perilous slope.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sets the divine will against the will of the passion-ridden: whomever God wishes to bring back to Himself, no enemy will rejoice at his fall, and no harm will reach him in the two abodes. As for those who follow their passions, their will is overturned; before the will of the Real — glorified be He — it is vain, repelled, lost. The repentant's security rests on this: that God Himself wishes his return.

mots.racine: ر و د / ت و ب
mots.def: the divine will to accept repentance.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ش ه و
م ي ل / ع ظ م
mots.def: an enormous deviation away from uprightness.

versets.ar: يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَنْ يُخَفِّفَ عَنْكُمْ وَخُلِقَ الْإِنْسانُ ضَعِيفاً
versets.tr: yurīdu llāhu an yukhaffifa ʿankum wa-khuliqa l-insānu ḍaʿīfan
versets.trad: God wishes to lighten your burden, for man was created weak.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the senses of the lightening: God wishes to lighten the weight of burdens by the inrush of remembrances into your hearts; to lighten the toil of the spiritual struggles by the lights of contemplation that pervade the hearts; to lighten the labours of service by the sweetness of obediences; to lighten the weariness of the quest by the breath of arrival; to lighten the burden of the Trust by bearing it for you. And "man was created weak": by this He described their poverty and their distress, and by this He excused their weakness.

mots.racine: خ ف ف
mots.def: takhfīf: the lightening of the load.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / أ ن س
mots.def: a reference to the human condition.

mots.racine: ض ع ف
mots.def: ḍaʿf: the weakness constitutive of man, the excuse of his distress.

versets.ar: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوالَكُمْ بَيْنَكُمْ بِالْباطِلِ إِلاَّ أَنْ تَكُونَ تِجارَةً عَنْ تَراضٍ مِنْكُمْ وَلا تَقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيماً
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā taʾkulū amwālakum baynakum bi-l-bāṭili illā an takūna tijāratan ʿan tarāḍin minkum wa-lā taqtulū anfusakum inna llāha kāna bikum raḥīman
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not consume one another's wealth wrongfully, unless it be a trade by mutual consent. And do not kill yourselves! Truly, God is merciful toward you.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorises the verse: every expenditure made for other than God is the consuming of wealth through the vain; and every taking carried out in heedlessness, every gift accomplished outside the presence of the Truth — all that is vain. On "do not kill yourselves," he unfolds: by the committing of sins; by exposure to His wrath; by your complacent regard upon the soul and its observation; by the esteem you bear it, preferring it to the good pleasure of the Real. To kill one's soul is to flatter it against God.

mots.racine: ب ط ل
mots.def: bāṭil: an illegitimate way, without right; in Qushayrī, any action outside of God.

mots.racine: ت ج ر / ر ض و
ق ت ل / ن ف س
mots.def: the prohibition of murder and, in the hidden sense, of the soul's ruin.

mots.racine: ر ح م

versets.ar: وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ عُدْواناً وَظُلْماً فَسَوْفَ نُصْلِيهِ ناراً وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيراً
versets.tr: wa-man yafʿal dhālika ʿudwānan wa-ẓulman fa-sawfa nuṣlīhi nāran wa-kāna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīran
versets.trad: Whoever does this in aggression and injustice, We shall cast him into the Fire. That is easy for God!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the threat a subtle reach: whoever does this in aggression and injustice, We will not leave him without a severe chastisement — and that chastisement is to entrust him to himself, to throw his rope upon his own neck. The worst of torments is not so much the visible Fire as to be abandoned to one's own soul, left to oneself with no further succour from the Real.

mots.racine: ع د و / ظ ل م
ص ل ي / ن و ر
mots.def: to cast him into the Fire.

mots.racine: ي س ر
mots.def: a small matter in view of the divine power.

versets.ar: إِنْ تَجْتَنِبُوا كَبائِرَ ما تُنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ نُكَفِّرْ عَنْكُمْ سَيِّئاتِكُمْ وَنُدْخِلْكُمْ مُدْخَلاً كَرِيماً
versets.tr: in tajtanibū kabāʾira mā tunhawna ʿanhu nukaffir ʿankum sayyiʾātikum wa-nudkhilkum mudkhalan karīman
versets.trad: If you avoid the gravest of the things forbidden to you, We shall efface your evil deeds and admit you through a noble gate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī doubles the sense of the "major sins": in the tongue of the Law, the gravest is the association of partners with God; in the tongue of allusion, it is the hidden idolatry — and he gives the list: the regard paid to creatures, the complacency in their good pleasure, the seeking of their affection, and the neglecting of God's right for their sake. Then he rises higher: the greatest of the major sins is to affirm your own self; when you contemplate its effacement, you are delivered from the chains of trials. "A noble gate": to enter into one's affairs without seeing either one's entrance or one's exit, but Him alone who ordains them.

mots.racine: ك ب ر
mots.def: kabāʾir: the grave sins; in Qushayrī, hidden association.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / س و أ
mots.def: takfīr: the effacement of minor faults.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ك ر م
mots.def: an honourable place of entry, Paradise.

versets.ar: وَلا تَتَمَنَّوْا ما فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ بِهِ بَعْضَكُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ لِلرِّجالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا اكْتَسَبُوا وَلِلنِّساءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا اكْتَسَبْنَ وَسْئَلُوا اللَّهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيماً
versets.tr: wa-lā tatamannaw mā faḍḍala llāhu bihi baʿḍakum ʿalā baʿḍin li-r-rijāli naṣībun mimmā ktasabū wa-li-n-nisāʾi naṣībun mimmā ktasabna wa-sʾalū llāha min faḍlihi inna llāha kāna bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīman
versets.trad: Do not covet the favours by which God has preferred some of you over others: to men a share of what they have earned, and to women a share of what they have earned. Ask God of His bounty. God, assuredly, knows all things.
themes: ask for grace, not for the share
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the command is to effort, not to wishing; to submission before the divine decree and ordainment, not to wanting and desiring. And he warns: do not covet the station of the masters without following their paths, without treading their march, without accomplishing their work — that would be an injustice of the mind. To wish for the station of a saint is almost to hasten one's death. And he distinguishes wishing from asking: do not desire the gift, but ask God to grant you, by His grace, contentment in the privation of the gift — for that is more perfect than the gift; and to be freed from the bondage of things is better than to possess them.

mots.racine: م ن ي
mots.def: tamannī: to wish for what is allotted to another.

mots.racine: ف ض ل
ك س ب
mots.def: a share tied to effort and to work.

mots.racine: س أ ل / ف ض ل
mots.def: to seek the gift without despoiling another.

versets.ar: وَلِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنا مَوالِيَ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوالِدانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ وَالَّذِينَ عَقَدَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ فَآتُوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيداً
versets.tr: wa-li-kullin jaʿalnā mawāliya mimmā taraka l-wālidāni wa-l-aqrabūna wa-lladhīna ʿaqadat aymānukum fa-ātūhum naṣībahum inna llāha kāna ʿalā kulli shayʾin shahīdan
versets.trad: To each We have appointed heirs to what their parents and near kin leave behind. As for those with whom you have contracted an alliance, give them their due share. God is witness over all things.
themes: from the human pact to the divine pact
versets.commentaire: In the beginning of Islam, the pact of brotherly alliance counted, for inheritance, as the bond of blood; then the bequest was abrogated and only the respect due remained. Qushayrī then ascends from the human contract to the supreme Covenant: if the alliance between men has such an inviolability, what then of the alliance contracted with God? And he cites: "men who were faithful to the pledge they had made to God." Loyalty to the created pact is but a reflection of the fidelity owed to the primordial Covenant.

mots.racine: و ل ي
ع ق د / ي م ن
mots.def: pacts of alliance sealed by oath.

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.def: Witness over all things, who guarantees every pact.

versets.ar: الرِّجالُ قَوَّامُونَ عَلَى النِّساءِ بِما فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ بَعْضَهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ وَبِما أَنْفَقُوا مِنْ أَمْوالِهِمْ فَالصَّالِحاتُ قانِتاتٌ حافِظاتٌ لِلْغَيْبِ بِما حَفِظَ اللَّهُ وَاللاَّتِي تَخافُونَ نُشُوزَهُنَّ فَعِظُوهُنَّ وَاهْجُرُوهُنَّ فِي الْمَضاجِعِ وَاضْرِبُوهُنَّ فَإِنْ أَطَعْنَكُمْ فَلا تَبْغُوا عَلَيْهِنَّ سَبِيلاً إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ عَلِيًّا كَبِيراً
versets.tr: ar-rijālu qawwāmūna ʿalā n-nisāʾi bimā faḍḍala llāhu baʿḍahum ʿalā baʿḍin wa-bimā anfaqū min amwālihim fa-ṣ-ṣāliḥātu qānitātun ḥāfiẓātun li-l-ghaybi bimā ḥafiẓa llāhu wa-llātī takhāfūna nushūzahunna fa-ʿiẓūhunna wa-hjurūhunna fī l-maḍājiʿi wa-ḍribūhunna fa-in aṭaʿnakum fa-lā tabghū ʿalayhinna sabīlan inna llāha kāna ʿaliyyan kabīran
versets.trad: Men have charge of women, by virtue of the advantages God has bestowed on some over others, and because of the wealth they spend [on them]. Thus the righteous women are devout, guarding in secret what God would have guarded. As for those whose ill-conduct you fear, admonish them, leave them apart in their beds, and chastise them. But if they obey you, seek no way against them. God, assuredly, is sublime, infinitely great.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: men have been distinguished by strength, and the charge has been increased upon them in proportion — for the burden is measured by the strength. But what truly counts are hearts and lofty aspirations, not bodies and outer shells. On the graduated correction, he teaches gentleness: raise them little by little in their schooling, by gradation and forbearance; if the matter is set right by exhortation, do not use the rod — the verse teaches the fair manners of the shared life. And: if they return to obedience, do not avenge yourself for the past, do not overwhelm them, accept the excuse.

mots.racine: ق و م
mots.def: qawwām: a responsible protector, charged with providing.

mots.racine: ق ن ت / ح ف ظ / غ ي ب
mots.def: obedient to God, preserving in absence what ought to be preserved.

mots.racine: ن ش ز
ع ل و / ك ب ر
mots.def: two Names: the Exalted and the Most Great.

versets.ar: وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ شِقاقَ بَيْنِهِما فَابْعَثُوا حَكَماً مِنْ أَهْلِهِ وَحَكَماً مِنْ أَهْلِها إِنْ يُرِيدا إِصْلاحاً يُوَفِّقِ اللَّهُ بَيْنَهُما إِنَّ اللَّهَ كانَ عَلِيماً خَبِيراً
versets.tr: wa-in khiftum shiqāqa baynihimā fa-bʿathū ḥakaman min ahlihi wa-ḥakaman min ahlihā in yurīdā iṣlāḥan yuwaffiqi llāhu baynahumā inna llāha kāna ʿalīman khabīran
versets.trad: If you fear a breach between a married couple, appoint an arbiter from the husband's family and an arbiter from the wife's family. If the two desire reconciliation, God will bring about concord between them. God is all-knowing, fully aware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī illumines the limit of conjugal authority: you have a right to her obedience in body; but the love and inclination of the heart toward you, that belongs to God — so do not demand of her what God does not grant you from her, for hearts are in the power of God: He makes love whom He wills, and hate whom He wills. So human arbitration succeeds only if God Himself grants the concord: if the two desire reconciliation, God will make them concord. And he recalls not to forget past fidelity for a passing harshness.

mots.racine: ش ق ق
ح ك م
mots.def: ḥakam: a mediating judge chosen from each family.

mots.racine: و ف ق
mots.def: tawfīq: God gives the concord of hearts.

mots.racine: ع ل م / خ ب ر
mots.def: two Names: Knowledge and intimate awareness.

versets.ar: وَاعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَلا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئاً وَبِالْوالِدَيْنِ إِحْساناً وَبِذِي الْقُرْبى وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينِ وَالْجارِ ذِي الْقُرْبى وَالْجارِ الْجُنُبِ وَالصَّاحِبِ بِالْجَنْبِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ وَما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كانَ مُخْتالاً فَخُوراً
versets.tr: wa-ʿbudū llāha wa-lā tushrikū bihi shayʾan wa-bi-l-wālidayni iḥsānan wa-bi-dhī l-qurbā wa-l-yatāmā wa-l-masākīni wa-l-jāri dhī l-qurbā wa-l-jāri l-junubi wa-ṣ-ṣāḥibi bi-l-janbi wa-bni s-sabīli wa-mā malakat aymānukum inna llāha lā yuḥibbu man kāna mukhtālan fakhūran
versets.trad: Worship God! Associate nothing with Him! Toward your father and mother show kindness, and toward near kin, orphans, the needy, the neighbour who is of your kin and the neighbour who is a stranger, the close companion, the wayfarer, and those whom your right hands possess. God does not love the arrogant, the boastful,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines worship and association: servanthood is to embrace the command and to flee the prohibition; manifest association is to believe in a god other than Him, hidden association is to pay heed to any existent other than Him; oneness (tawḥīd) is to know that all events are by God, subsisting through Him — He brings them about, establishes them, sustains them, and none possesses a particle of creation. Then he unfolds the neighbourhood by degrees: if the neighbour of your dwelling deserves your beneficence, the neighbour of your soul — which is your heart — has more right still; and the neighbour of your heart — which is your spirit — yet more; and the neighbour of your spirit — which is your secret (sirr) — more than all: do not let it absent itself from the homelands of contemplation for a single instant.

mots.racine: ع ب د
ش ر ك
mots.def: the refusal of every associate, manifest or hidden.

mots.racine: و ل د / ح س ن
mots.def: iḥsān: the excellence of the good due to father and mother.

mots.racine: ج و ر / ج ن ب
mots.def: a neighbour without kinship; in Qushayrī, an allusion to the inner neighbourhoods.

mots.racine: خ ي ل / ف خ ر
mots.def: the proud one who regards himself and boasts.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ وَيَكْتُمُونَ ما آتاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَأَعْتَدْنا لِلْكافِرِينَ عَذاباً مُهِيناً
versets.tr: alladhīna yabkhalūna wa-yaʾmurūna n-nāsa bi-l-bukhli wa-yaktumūna mā ātāhumu llāhu min faḍlihi wa-aʿtadnā li-l-kāfirīna ʿadhāban muhīnan
versets.trad: nor those who are miserly and enjoin miserliness upon others, who conceal what God has given them of His bounty — for the unbelievers We have prepared an abasing chastisement,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī doubles miserliness: miserliness, in the tongue of knowledge, is to withhold what is due; in the tongue of allusion, it is to forsake preferring another (īthār) in the hour of distress. "Enjoining miserliness upon others" means, he says subtly, to turn them away from the quest of the realities under cover of legal compassion — whoever dissuades a weak one from divesting himself for God, claiming to protect him, quenches in him the surge of aspiration, and wrath seizes him at once. And he distinguishes two miserlinesses: the miserliness of the rich is to withhold the gift, the miserliness of the poor is to withhold the lofty aspiration.

mots.racine: ب خ ل
mots.def: bukhl: to withhold what is due; in Qushayrī, the refusal of īthār.

mots.racine: أ م ر / ب خ ل
ك ت م / أ ت ي
mots.sens: conceal what God has given them
mots.def: to hide the received grace out of covetousness.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوالَهُمْ رِئاءَ النَّاسِ وَلا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَلا بِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَمَنْ يَكُنِ الشَّيْطانُ لَهُ قَرِيناً فَساءَ قَرِيناً
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yunfiqūna amwālahum riʾāʾa n-nāsi wa-lā yuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-lā bi-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-man yakuni sh-shayṭānu lahu qarīnan fa-sāʾa qarīnan
versets.trad: nor those who spend their wealth to be seen by others and believe neither in God nor in the Last Day. Whoever has Satan for a companion has, indeed, an evil companion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī places these show-offs under the sentence: "God does not love the arrogant, the boastful" — their immediate chastisement is to be not among His beloved, and that is trial enough. And he sets two figures of the same hidden ill against each other: the arrogant (mukhtāl) is the one who looks upon his own self; the show-off (murāʾī) is the one who looks upon his fellows — both are branded with hidden association, and God does not love those who associate with Him. To give in order to be seen is yet another face of the subtle idolatry.

mots.racine: ر أ ي
mots.def: riyāʾ: to make a display before people.

mots.racine: أ م ن
ق ر ن
mots.def: qarīn: a constant associate; here Satan.

versets.ar: وَماذا عَلَيْهِمْ لَوْ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَأَنْفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَهُمُ اللَّهُ وَكانَ اللَّهُ بِهِمْ عَلِيماً
versets.tr: wa-mādhā ʿalayhim law āmanū bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri wa-anfaqū mimmā razaqahumu llāhu wa-kāna llāhu bihim ʿalīman
versets.trad: And what would they have had to lose if they had believed in God and the Last Day, and had spent [in alms] something of what God has granted them? God knows them perfectly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: there is in their faith in God no hardship for them; rather, had they believed, they would have attained the glory of this world and the next. Nothing turns them away but an inner deficiency: only the scantness of fidelity and the scantness of reverence drive them to turn from Him. Faith is never loss but pure gain; what holds man back is the heart's miserliness toward loyalty.

mots.sens: what would they have to lose
mots.def: a rhetorical question: no loss in faith.

mots.racine: أ م ن
ن ف ق / ر ز ق
mots.sens: spent of what God granted them
mots.def: alms drawn from the sustenance received.

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.def: the Name of Knowledge embracing their state.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقالَ ذَرَّةٍ وَإِنْ تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضاعِفْها وَيُؤْتِ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ أَجْراً عَظِيماً
versets.tr: inna llāha lā yaẓlimu mithqāla dharratin wa-in taku ḥasanatan yuḍāʿifhā wa-yuʾti min ladunhu ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: God wrongs no one, not even by the weight of an atom. If it be a good deed, He doubles its worth and gives, from His own presence, a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He withholds nothing of their reward; rather He forestalls them — without their having merited it — by His grace, and multiplies their wages beyond their works. As for injustice, it is unthinkable of Him: the unjust is the one who oversteps a bound traced for him; yet that is impossible in His description, by reason of His glory in the majesty of His power — for the creation is His creation and the dominion His dominion. The phrase "from His own presence" (min ladunhu) evokes the pure gift, that spontaneous liberality which He alone can bestow, as the infused knowledge (ʿilm ladunnī) owes nothing to human effort.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ذ ر ر
mots.sens: wrongs not by the weight of an atom
mots.def: absolute justice down to the smallest thing.

mots.racine: ض ع ف
mots.def: to multiply the reward of the good deed.

mots.racine: ل د ن
mots.def: a spontaneous and pure gift; the source of infused knowledge (ʿilm ladunnī).

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ع ظ م

versets.ar: فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَىٰ هَٰؤُلَاءِ شَهِيدًا
versets.tr: fa-kayfa idhā jiʾnā min kulli ummatin bi-shahīdin wa-jiʾnā bika ʿalā hāʾulāʾi shahīdan
versets.trad: What shall they do when We bring forth from every community a witness, and bring you forth as a witness against these?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls that the Messenger is at once the witness over his community and its intercessor: if he bears witness, he does so while leaving room for intercession, for he who pleads for his own attests only to what may still be redeemed. The verse opens the great scene where every people stands forth, its prophet at its side, "and you, over these, as a witness."

mots.def: an interrogation of menace: what shall they do?

mots.racine: ش ه د
ش ه د
mots.sens: and We shall bring you forth as a witness
mots.def: the Prophet as witness over his community.

versets.ar: يَوْمَئِذٍ يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَعَصَوُا الرَّسُولَ لَوْ تُسَوَّىٰ بِهِمُ الْأَرْضُ وَلَا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا
versets.tr: yawmaʾidhin yawaddu lladhīna kafarū wa-ʿaṣawu r-rasūla law tusawwā bihimu l-arḍu wa-lā yaktumūna llāha ḥadīthan
versets.trad: On that Day, those who disbelieved and disobeyed the Messenger will wish that the earth were levelled over them. They will be unable to hide from God any of what has come to pass.
themes: the barren remorse of the deniers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the state of the renegade on that Day: he will be seized by a remorse that avails him nothing, he will gnaw his fingers without his torment being eased, he will be wrapped in the mantle of humiliation and returned to the dwellings of trial and ill. To wish "that the earth be levelled over them" is to long to vanish, to become dust once more, rather than face what no secret can now withhold from God.

mots.racine: و د د
س و ي / أ ر ض
mots.sens: that the earth be levelled over them
mots.def: sawwā: to level; to be buried and merged with the ground.

mots.racine: ك ت م
mots.sens: they will hide from God no word
mots.def: katama: to conceal; nothing remains hidden.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَقْرَبُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَأَنتُمْ سُكَارَىٰ حَتَّىٰ تَعْلَمُوا مَا تَقُولُونَ وَلَا جُنُبًا إِلَّا عَابِرِي سَبِيلٍ حَتَّىٰ تَغْتَسِلُوا ۚ وَإِن كُنتُم مَّرْضَىٰ أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ أَوْ جَاءَ أَحَدٌ مِّنكُم مِّنَ الْغَائِطِ أَوْ لَامَسْتُمُ النِّسَاءَ فَلَمْ تَجِدُوا مَاءً فَتَيَمَّمُوا صَعِيدًا طَيِّبًا فَامْسَحُوا بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَفُوًّا غَفُورًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā taqrabū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-antum sukārā ḥattā taʿlamū mā taqūlūna wa-lā junuban illā ʿābirī sabīlin ḥattā taghtasilū wa-in kuntum marḍā aw ʿalā safarin aw jāʾa aḥadun minkum mina l-ghāʾiṭi aw lāmastumu n-nisāʾa fa-lam tajidū māʾan fa-tayammamū ṣaʿīdan ṭayyiban fa-msaḥū bi-wujūhikum wa-aydīkum inna llāha kāna ʿafuwwan ghafūran
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated, until you know what you say; nor in a state of major impurity — unless you are merely passing through — until you have bathed. If you are sick, or on a journey, or one of you comes from relieving himself, or you have had intercourse with women and find no water, then betake yourselves to clean earth and wipe your faces and your hands. Indeed, God is forbearing, forgiving.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shifts the prohibition of wine toward the intoxication of the soul: drunkenness is the loss of reason and awareness, and no intimate converse with the Real is valid under its sway. He who prays converses with his Lord; thus everything that ravishes the heart away from God falls, by allusion, under the same prohibition. And he distinguishes its degrees: "there is the drunkenness of wine, the drunkenness of heedlessness through the grip of love of the world; but the harshest is your drunkenness with yourself, the one that casts you into separation from Him." The drunkard of wine incurs only the lash; the one drunk on his ego knows separation from the Truth itself.

mots.racine: ق ر ب / س ك ر
ج ن ب
mots.def: junub: in a state of janāba.

mots.racine: ي م م / ص ع د
mots.def: tayammum: dry ablution; ṣaʿīd: surface of earth.

mots.racine: ع ف و / غ ف ر
mots.def: ʿafuww: who effaces; ghafūr: who forgives.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا نَصِيبًا مِّنَ الْكِتَابِ يَشْتَرُونَ الضَّلَالَةَ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَن تَضِلُّوا السَّبِيلَ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna ūtū naṣīban mina l-kitābi yashtarūna ḍ-ḍalālata wa-yurīdūna an taḍillū s-sabīla
versets.trad: Have you not seen those to whom a portion of the Book was given? They trade in error, and they desire that you should stray from the right path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes the paradox: they were given a portion of the Book, then deprived of the blessings of understanding, until they fell to altering and obstinacy. Knowledge does not save the one who betrays it: they "purchase error" and even desire it for others. He who deviates from the Path and would draw others into it has lost even the very light entrusted to him.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ن ص ب
mots.sens: given a portion of the Book

mots.racine: ش ر ي / ض ل ل
ض ل ل / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِأَعْدَائِكُمْ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-llāhu aʿlamu bi-aʿdāʾikum wa-kafā bi-llāhi waliyyan wa-kafā bi-llāhi naṣīran
versets.trad: God knows your enemies well; God suffices as protector; God suffices as helper.
versets.commentaire: The servant need not catalogue his enemies: God knows them better than he knows himself, and this knowledge takes the place of his vigilance. "God suffices as protector (waliyy), God suffices as helper (naṣīr)": whoever entrusts himself to this twofold sufficiency need seek neither support nor defence elsewhere. The repetition seals the trust: he to whom God makes Himself near has need of no other.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ع د و
ك ف ي / و ل ي
ن ص ر

versets.ar: مِّنَ الَّذِينَ هَادُوا يُحَرِّفُونَ الْكَلِمَ عَن مَّوَاضِعِهِ وَيَقُولُونَ سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا وَاسْمَعْ غَيْرَ مُسْمَعٍ وَرَاعِنَا لَيًّا بِأَلْسِنَتِهِمْ وَطَعْنًا فِي الدِّينِ ۚ وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا وَاسْمَعْ وَانظُرْنَا لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ وَأَقْوَمَ وَلَٰكِن لَّعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِكُفْرِهِمْ فَلَا يُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: mina lladhīna hādū yuḥarrifūna l-kalima ʿan mawāḍiʿihi wa-yaqūlūna samiʿnā wa-ʿaṣaynā wa-smaʿ ghayra musmaʿin wa-rāʿinā layyan bi-alsinatihim wa-ṭaʿnan fī d-dīni wa-law annahum qālū samiʿnā wa-aṭaʿnā wa-smaʿ wa-nẓurnā la-kāna khayran lahum wa-aqwama wa-lākin laʿanahumu llāhu bi-kufrihim fa-lā yuʾminūna illā qalīlan
versets.trad: Among the Jews are some who twist words out of their context. They say, in mockery: "We have heard and we have disobeyed!... Hear, you who are not heard!... rāʿinā (heed us)..." — twisting their tongues and reviling the Religion. Had they said: "We have heard and we have obeyed... Hear... and look upon us," it would have been better for them and more upright. But God has cursed them for their disbelief, so they do not believe save a few.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī touches the root of the evil: they forsook the reverence owed to the Messenger and denied his dignity; thus they were chastised by the doubt cast upon him. And he lays down a law of the heart: "none abandons the respect due to a man of honour without being shut out from the blessings of his company." Had they hastened to tear from their breasts the envy that gnawed at them, had they received him with veneration, they would have found the blessings of discipleship and the bliss of the two abodes. But whom destiny has cast down, no stratagem can raise up.

mots.racine: ح ر ف / و ض ع
mots.sens: they twist words out of their place

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ص ي
mots.def: a mocking inversion of the formula of faith.

mots.racine: ر ع ي / ل و ي
ل ع ن / ك ف ر
mots.sens: God cursed them for their denial

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ آمِنُوا بِمَا نَزَّلْنَا مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا مَعَكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن نَّطْمِسَ وُجُوهًا فَنَرُدَّهَا عَلَىٰ أَدْبَارِهَا أَوْ نَلْعَنَهُمْ كَمَا لَعَنَّا أَصْحَابَ السَّبْتِ ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ مَفْعُولًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba āminū bimā nazzalnā muṣaddiqan limā maʿakum min qabli an naṭmisa wujūhan fa-naruddahā ʿalā adbārihā aw nalʿanahum kamā laʿannā aṣḥāba s-sabti wa-kāna amru llāhi mafʿūlan
versets.trad: O you to whom the Book was given! Believe in what We have sent down, confirming what you already possess, before We efface faces and turn them about backwards, or curse them as We cursed the People of the Sabbath. And the command of God is ever accomplished!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the "effacement of faces" as a spiritual reversal: hearts are turned away from aspiration toward the states of the people of habit, until one who once burned to renounce the world can no longer do without amassing and clutching it. To be "turned about backwards" (radd ʿalā l-adbār) is to regress, the back turned to the first light. And the command of God is accomplished without appeal.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ن ز ل
mots.sens: believe in what We have sent down

mots.racine: ط م س / و ج ه
mots.def: ṭams: to blot out the features.

mots.racine: س ب ت
mots.def: those who transgressed the sabbatical rest.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَن يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدِ افْتَرَىٰ إِثْمًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: inna llāha lā yaghfiru an yushraka bihi wa-yaghfiru mā dūna dhālika liman yashāʾu wa-man yushrik bi-llāhi fa-qadi ftarā ithman ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Indeed, God does not forgive that any partner be ascribed to Him; but, beyond that, He forgives whom He wills. Whoever ascribes a partner to God has forged a monstrous sin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the warning to subtle shirk: the common are asked to leave off manifest association; the elect, to leave off hidden association. Thus "he who draws near to God through his works, imagining that they proceed from himself, or who believes that the divine decrees depend on his deeds and rests, or who contemplates his own self" — such a one, in the eyes of the people of realities, is marked with a secret associationism. And so too is he who imagines that his disobedience comes to pass outside the decree of God.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ش ر ك
mots.sens: He does not forgive that a partner be ascribed to Him
mots.def: shirk: to associate another with God.

mots.racine: د و ن
ف ر ي / أ ث م

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ يُزَكُّونَ أَنفُسَهُم ۚ بَلِ اللَّهُ يُزَكِّي مَن يَشَاءُ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلًا
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna yuzakkūna anfusahum bali llāhu yuzakkī man yashāʾu wa-lā yuẓlamūna fatīlan
versets.trad: Have you not seen those who claim purity for themselves? Rather, it is God who purifies whom He wills, and they shall not be wronged by so much as the skin of a date-stone.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes self-satisfaction the thickest of veils: he who delights in the praise of men, who savours being approved by the elite — still more by the common — such a one purifies himself; yet the seeing of self is the greatest of veils. And likewise the one who imagines that, through litanies and toil, gestures and retreats, he renders himself pure by his own labour: "he abides in the shroud of his ignorance." God alone purifies whom He wills.

mots.racine: ز ك و / ن ف س
mots.def: tazkiya: to claim one's own purity.

mots.racine: ز ك و
mots.sens: rather it is God who purifies whom He wills

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ف ت ل
mots.sens: they shall not be wronged by a thread
mots.def: fatīl: the fibre within a date-stone.

versets.ar: انظُرْ كَيْفَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ ۖ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِ إِثْمًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: unẓur kayfa yaftarūna ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba wa-kafā bihi ithman mubīnan
versets.trad: Behold how they forge falsehood against God: that, in itself, is already a manifest sin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī aims at "the one who loosens the tongue of pretension without inner realization": the slanderer, so long as he holds forth on the matter, captivates the ears and stirs the hearts; but the moment he falls silent, he returns to a heart in ruins. The lie against God is a "manifest" sin because it exposes itself: speech without ground betrays itself the instant the brightness of the voice has ceased.

mots.racine: ن ظ ر
ف ر ي / ك ذ ب
أ ث م / ب ي ن

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا نَصِيبًا مِّنَ الْكِتَابِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْجِبْتِ وَالطَّاغُوتِ وَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا هَٰؤُلَاءِ أَهْدَىٰ مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna ūtū naṣīban mina l-kitābi yuʾminūna bi-l-jibti wa-ṭ-ṭāghūti wa-yaqūlūna lilladhīna kafarū hāʾulāʾi ahdā mina lladhīna āmanū sabīlan
versets.trad: Have you not noticed those to whom a portion of the Scripture was given? They believe in sorcery and false deities (the ṭāghūt), and they say of the disbelievers: "These are better guided on the path than the believers."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the idols: each man's ṭāghūt is his soul and his passion; his jibt is whatever he seeks from others than God. Thus "he who turns toward a creature, watches for a secondary cause, leans on a pretext, or obeys a desire — that is his jibt and his ṭāghūt." And the people of the jibt and the ṭāghūt merit the curse: banishment from the carpet of servanthood, and the veil that hides the contemplation of the Lordship.

mots.racine: ج ب ت / ط غ ي
mots.sens: they believe in the jibt and the ṭāghūt

mots.racine: ه د ي / س ب ل
mots.def: a perverted judgment upon the straight path.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ ۖ وَمَن يَلْعَنِ اللَّهُ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُ نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna laʿanahumu llāhu wa-man yalʿani llāhu fa-lan tajida lahu naṣīran
versets.trad: Those are the ones whom God has cursed; and for him whom God curses you will find no helper.
versets.commentaire: To be cursed, in Qushayrī's idiom, is to be driven from the carpet of servanthood and veiled from the contemplation of the Lordship. And whom God sets aside, no helper can draw near: no intercession, no ruse, no created support can make up for the refusal of the Real. The door is closed where God has closed it.

mots.racine: ل ع ن
و ج د / ن ص ر
mots.sens: you will find for him no helper

versets.ar: أَمْ لَهُمْ نَصِيبٌ مِّنَ الْمُلْكِ فَإِذًا لَّا يُؤْتُونَ النَّاسَ نَقِيرًا
versets.tr: am lahum naṣībun mina l-mulki fa-idhan lā yuʾtūna n-nāsa naqīran
versets.trad: Were they to possess a share of dominion, they would not give their fellow men so much as the speck on a date-stone.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the miser as one parched for strong drink: he who is kneaded out of avarice grows rich only to grieve the more at the least good that reaches others — as though, having sipped a single drop of water, he believed he had drunk down the very water of his life. The naqīr, that tiny hollow of the date-stone, measures their meanness: they would let go of nothing, not even the most trifling thing.

mots.racine: ن ص ب / م ل ك
أ ت ي / ن ق ر
mots.sens: they would give people not a speck
mots.def: naqīr: the groove of a date-stone.

versets.ar: أَمْ يَحْسُدُونَ النَّاسَ عَلَىٰ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِ ۖ فَقَدْ آتَيْنَا آلَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَآتَيْنَاهُم مُّلْكًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: am yaḥsudūna n-nāsa ʿalā mā ātāhumu llāhu min faḍlihi fa-qad ātaynā āla ibrāhīma l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-ātaynāhum mulkan ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Or do they envy people for what God has granted them of His Bounty? Indeed, We gave the descendants of Abraham the Book and Wisdom, and We gave them a vast dominion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks envy as a refusal of the divine choosing: they deny that the Real should distinguish His friends with whatever He wills, out of a jealousy born of their own depths, and they refuse to render them homage. Yet God's custom toward His friends is to honour them. And the "vast dominion" given to the line of Abraham he interprets thus: the immense kingdom is the knowledge of the King; it is also said: sovereignty over the soul, or "the gaze cast upon the secrets of the kingdom, until nothing remains hidden from it."

mots.racine: ح س د
ك ت ب / ح ك م
م ل ك
mots.def: according to Qushayrī: the knowledge of the King.

versets.ar: فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ آمَنَ بِهِ وَمِنْهُم مَّن صَدَّ عَنْهُ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِجَهَنَّمَ سَعِيرًا
versets.tr: fa-minhum man āmana bihi wa-minhum man ṣadda ʿanhu wa-kafā bi-jahannama saʿīran
versets.trad: Among them are some who believed in his mission, while others turned away from it. Gehenna shall be a sufficient punishment for them.
versets.commentaire: Before grace, souls divide: some believed in it, others turned their faces from it. Qushayrī makes this verse the image of the eternal custom: faced with God's gift and His friends, humankind splits — faith on one side, aversion on the other, and aversion bears its own blaze within it. "Gehenna suffices as a fire": the sanction needs no surfeit added to it.

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.def: adherence to the grace or to the Messenger.

mots.racine: ص د د
mots.def: ṣadd: to bar oneself, to obstruct.

mots.racine: ج ه ن م / س ع ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِنَا سَوْفَ نُصْلِيهِمْ نَارًا كُلَّمَا نَضِجَتْ جُلُودُهُم بَدَّلْنَاهُمْ جُلُودًا غَيْرَهَا لِيَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū bi-āyātinā sawfa nuṣlīhim nāran kullamā naḍijat julūduhum baddalnāhum julūdan ghayrahā li-yadhūqū l-ʿadhāba inna llāha kāna ʿazīzan ḥakīman
versets.trad: Those who reject Our signs We shall soon cast into a Fire. Each time their skin is cooked through, We shall replace it with new skin, that they may taste the chastisement. Indeed, God is mighty, wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies the warning to those who deny the signs of the friends of God (āyāt al-awliyāʾ): He keeps them in humiliation and leaves them in the solitude of denial; each time a glimmer of that truth rises to their hearts, their denial draws them back to disbelief and to contempt for the saints, out of a bias against the improbable. Hence their eternal pain: the fire that renews the skin figures the obstinacy that never ceases to renew itself.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / أ ي ي
ن ض ج / ج ل د
mots.sens: each time their skin is cooked

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۖ لَّهُمْ فِيهَا أَزْوَاجٌ مُّطَهَّرَةٌ ۖ وَنُدْخِلُهُمْ ظِلًّا ظَلِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti sa-nudkhiluhum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā abadan lahum fīhā azwājun muṭahharatun wa-nudkhiluhum ẓillan ẓalīlan
