versets.trad: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, We shall admit them into Gardens beneath which the rivers flow, where they shall abide forever. There they shall have spouses immaculate, and We shall bring them into deep, abundant shade.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds a ladder of shades: today they are in the shade of His keeping, tomorrow in the shade of His protection and sufficiency; nay, in this world and the next they are in the shade of His solicitude. And he ranks men: "one is in the shade of His mercy, one in the shade of His keeping, one in the shade of His generosity, one in the shade of His solicitude, and one — the highest — in the shade of His nearness." The "deep shade" (ẓill ẓalīl) crowns this unveiling.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ص ل ح
ز و ج / ط ه ر
ظ ل ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تُؤَدُّوا الْأَمَانَاتِ إِلَىٰ أَهْلِهَا وَإِذَا حَكَمْتُم بَيْنَ النَّاسِ أَن تَحْكُمُوا بِالْعَدْلِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ نِعِمَّا يَعِظُكُم بِهِ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ سَمِيعًا بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: inna llāha yaʾmurukum an tuʾaddū l-amānāti ilā ahlihā wa-idhā ḥakamtum bayna n-nāsi an taḥkumū bi-l-ʿadli inna llāha niʿimmā yaʿiẓukum bihi inna llāha kāna samīʿan baṣīran
versets.trad: God commands you to render trusts to those entitled to them, and, when you judge between people, to judge with justice. Excellent indeed is that to which God exhorts you. In truth, God is all-hearing, all-seeing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī spiritualizes the trust: God has trusts that He has placed within you; to render them to the one entitled is to hand them back to Him intact, without betrayal. And he names the subtle betrayals: "to betray the trust of the heart is to claim mastery over it; to betray the trust of the secret (sirr) is to contemplate it as your own." As for judging with equity: to treat near and far alike in giving, without letting a grudge move you to avenge your soul.

mots.racine: أ د ي / أ م ن
mots.sens: render the trusts to those entitled

mots.racine: ح ك م / ع د ل
س م ع / ب ص ر

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنكُمْ ۖ فَإِن تَنَازَعْتُمْ فِي شَيْءٍ فَرُدُّوهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَالرَّسُولِ إِن كُنتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَحْسَنُ تَأْوِيلًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū aṭīʿū llāha wa-aṭīʿū r-rasūla wa-ulī l-amri minkum fa-in tanāzaʿtum fī shayʾin fa-ruddūhu ilā llāhi wa-r-rasūli in kuntum tuʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri dhālika khayrun wa-aḥsanu taʾwīlan
versets.trad: O you who believe, obey God and obey the Messenger, and those among you who hold authority. Should you differ over anything, refer it to God and to the Messenger, if you believe in God and the Last Day. That is best, and finest in outcome.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that God has coupled obedience to Himself with obedience to the Messenger so as to magnify his rank and exalt him. As for "those who hold authority" (ūlū l-amr), he declines its senses by degrees: "in the language of the scholars, it is the sovereign; in the language of gnosis, it is the gnostic (ʿārif) who has authority over the novice; the master (shaykh) has authority over the disciple, and the imam of each group has authority over his own." And in case of dispute: for the learned, recourse to the Book and the Sunna; but the man of heart entrusts himself to the Real and observes what is inspired to him without intermediary in his secret.

mots.racine: ط و ع
أ م ر
mots.sens: and those among you who hold authority

mots.racine: ر د د
mots.sens: refer it to God and the Messenger
mots.def: radd: to refer back, to report.

mots.racine: ح س ن / أ و ل

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ يَزْعُمُونَ أَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَا أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ يُرِيدُونَ أَن يَتَحَاكَمُوا إِلَى الطَّاغُوتِ وَقَدْ أُمِرُوا أَن يَكْفُرُوا بِهِ وَيُرِيدُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَن يُضِلَّهُمْ ضَلَالًا بَعِيدًا
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna yazʿumūna annahum āmanū bimā unzila ilayka wa-mā unzila min qablika yurīdūna an yataḥākamū ilā ṭ-ṭāghūti wa-qad umirū an yakfurū bihi wa-yurīdu sh-shayṭānu an yuḍillahum ḍalālan baʿīdan
versets.trad: Have you not seen those who claim to believe in what has been sent down to you and in what was sent down before you? They wish to refer their disputes to the false judge (the ṭāghūt), though they were commanded to reject him. And Satan desires to lead them into a far-reaching error.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks the hypocrite: they display sincerity and betray in secret; God has confounded them by the tongue of Gabriel. To wish to "refer to the ṭāghūt" whom they were commanded to reject is, on the inward plane, to abandon one's true guide: he who turns from his path and returns to another than his master merits deprivation and reproach.

mots.racine: ز ع م / أ م ن
ح ك م / ط غ ي
mots.sens: to refer to the judgment of the ṭāghūt
mots.def: to seek arbitration from the idol.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ع د

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا إِلَىٰ مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ وَإِلَى الرَّسُولِ رَأَيْتَ الْمُنَافِقِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَنكَ صُدُودًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahum taʿālaw ilā mā anzala llāhu wa-ilā r-rasūli raʾayta l-munāfiqīna yaṣuddūna ʿanka ṣudūdan
versets.trad: When it is said to them: "Come to what God has sent down, come to the Messenger!" — you see the hypocrites turn away from you in firm aversion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī uncovers the root of their flight: everything that is not the word of the Truth weighs little upon the hypocrite; but Oneness is heard only by the sincere. The people of lukewarmness toward God and of aversion cannot bear the truth, "for what crosses one's passion is harsh only to the truthful." And he draws out the image: just as the eye of a creature cannot face the sun, the hypocrite cannot stand before the Messenger — hence his turning away.

mots.racine: ع ل و / ن ز ل
mots.sens: come to what God has sent down

mots.racine: ص د د

versets.ar: فَكَيْفَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ثُمَّ جَاءُوكَ يَحْلِفُونَ بِاللَّهِ إِنْ أَرَدْنَا إِلَّا إِحْسَانًا وَتَوْفِيقًا
versets.tr: fa-kayfa idhā aṣābathum muṣībatun bimā qaddamat aydīhim thumma jāʾūka yaḥlifūna bi-llāhi in aradnā illā iḥsānan wa-tawfīqan
versets.trad: And what then, when a calamity strikes them for what their own hands have wrought, and they come to you swearing by God: "We desired only good and concord!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī holds the supplication of the insincere to be without worth: the prayer of one who is not sincere, at the moment when affliction falls upon him, has no root; it cannot be reckoned, for it lasts only until the trial is lifted. And he points to the disaster of disasters: "the greatest calamity is to cease to care about one's own shortcoming," or again, "that your lifetime be squandered on what profits you nothing."

mots.racine: ص و ب
ق د م / ي د ي
mots.sens: for what their hands have sent ahead
mots.def: the fruit of their own deeds.

mots.racine: ر و د / ح س ن
mots.def: iḥsān: beautiful action; an oath of excuse.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُ اللَّهُ مَا فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَعِظْهُمْ وَقُل لَّهُمْ فِي أَنفُسِهِمْ قَوْلًا بَلِيغًا
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna yaʿlamu llāhu mā fī qulūbihim fa-aʿriḍ ʿanhum wa-ʿiẓhum wa-qul lahum fī anfusihim qawlan balīghan
versets.trad: Those are they whose hearts God knows. So turn away from them, yet admonish them, and speak to them words that penetrate their souls.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī holds the subtle balance of the educator: unfold for them the tongue of admonition out of compassion, but withdraw your heart from attachment to them and from reposing in them. And he sets the measure: "know that for the one whose support We are not, nothing you bring him can suffice." One warns out of duty, without leaning on the outcome.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ق ل ب
ع ر ض
mots.def: the iʿrāḍ of the heart, not of counsel.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ب ل غ

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا لِيُطَاعَ بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ إِذ ظَّلَمُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ جَاءُوكَ فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا اللَّهَ وَاسْتَغْفَرَ لَهُمُ الرَّسُولُ لَوَجَدُوا اللَّهَ تَوَّابًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnā min rasūlin illā li-yuṭāʿa bi-idhni llāhi wa-law annahum idh ẓalamū anfusahum jāʾūka fa-staghfarū llāha wa-staghfara lahumu r-rasūlu la-wajadū llāha tawwāban raḥīman
versets.trad: We sent no Messenger but that he be obeyed, by the leave of God. Had these people, after wronging themselves, come to you imploring God's pardon, and had the Messenger sought pardon for them, they would have found God ever-relenting, merciful.
themes: the Messenger, a door toward God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers the prophetic mission into a single phrase: We commanded the messengers only to summon the creatures toward Us. And he reads intercession as a way of access: "had they taken you as a means, they would have reached Us"; or again, had they kept to lowliness and indigence, and mounted the steed of imploring, they would have made their camel kneel at the threshold of the Most Generous.

mots.racine: ط و ع / أ ذ ن
mots.sens: save to be obeyed by the leave of God

mots.racine: غ ف ر
mots.sens: they implored the pardon of God

mots.racine: ت و ب / ر ح م
mots.def: tawwāb: who ever returns toward the penitent.

versets.ar: فَلَا وَرَبِّكَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ حَتَّىٰ يُحَكِّمُوكَ فِيمَا شَجَرَ بَيْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُوا فِي أَنفُسِهِمْ حَرَجًا مِّمَّا قَضَيْتَ وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا
versets.tr: fa-lā wa-rabbika lā yuʾminūna ḥattā yuḥakkimūka fīmā shajara baynahum thumma lā yajidū fī anfusihim ḥarajan mimmā qaḍayta wa-yusallimū taslīman
versets.trad: But no, by your Lord! They will not truly believe until they make you judge of what is disputed among them, and then find within themselves no resistance to what you have decided, and submit in full submission.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes this oath the seal of the path: God has barred to all the road toward Him save after faith in Muḥammad; whoever does not walk beneath his banner has with God no breath. Then he teaches the second condition: the total erasure of objections within your heart, the welcoming of the decree "with a smiling face," until one feels neither unease nor inner resistance before what is decided.

mots.racine: ح ك م
mots.def: taḥkīm: to entrust oneself to arbitration.

mots.racine: ش ج ر
ح ر ج
س ل م

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّا كَتَبْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ أَنِ اقْتُلُوا أَنفُسَكُمْ أَوِ اخْرُجُوا مِن دِيَارِكُم مَّا فَعَلُوهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ مِّنْهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ فَعَلُوا مَا يُوعَظُونَ بِهِ لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ وَأَشَدَّ تَثْبِيتًا
versets.tr: wa-law annā katabnā ʿalayhim ani qtulū anfusakum awi khrujū min diyārikum mā faʿalūhu illā qalīlun minhum wa-law annahum faʿalū mā yūʿaẓūna bihi la-kāna khayran lahum wa-ashadda tathbītan
versets.trad: Had We prescribed to them: "Slay your own souls!" or: "Go forth from your homes!" — they would not have done it, save a few of them. Yet it would have been better for them, and stronger in confirming them, had they done what they were exhorted to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī shifts the prescription into allusion: the matter comes down to crossing the passion and slaughtering the souls by weaning them from their habits; to going forth from the dwellings of the ego's complacency, and leaving the homelands of desire for the world. To "slay one's soul" and to "go forth from one's houses" thus become the two gestures of the inner combat — costly, which is why only "a few" resolve upon them.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ن ف س
mots.def: inner sense: to mortify the ego.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / د و ر
mots.def: to leave the homelands of habit.

mots.racine: ش د د / ث ب ت

versets.ar: وَإِذًا لَّآتَيْنَاهُم مِّن لَّدُنَّا أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-idhan la-ātaynāhum min ladunnā ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Then We would have granted them, from Our presence, a magnificent reward,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī comments on the gift: had they acted thus, We would have bestowed upon them from Our presence an immense recompense, We would have guided them upon a straight path, and We would have favoured them with an enduring gift. The "from Our presence" (min ladunnā) marks that the reward is not earned: it wells up from sheer largesse, there where the soul has learned to die to itself.

mots.racine: ل د ن
mots.def: ladun: from the nearest to Us, pure grace.

mots.racine: أ ج ر

versets.ar: وَلَهَدَيْنَاهُمْ صِرَاطًا مُّسْتَقِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-la-hadaynāhum ṣirāṭan mustaqīman
versets.trad: and We would have guided them upon a straight path.
versets.commentaire: Guidance crowns obedience: he who mortifies his passion and goes forth from the dwellings of the ego, God leads upon the straight path. For Qushayrī, the ṣirāṭ mustaqīm is not only the rectitude of the Law, but the unswerving track that brings the heart to the Real, once the soul has ceased to choose itself.

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.def: hidāya: the direction given by God.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.ar: وَمَن يُطِعِ اللَّهَ وَالرَّسُولَ فَأُولَٰئِكَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ النَّبِيِّينَ وَالصِّدِّيقِينَ وَالشُّهَدَاءِ وَالصَّالِحِينَ ۚ وَحَسُنَ أُولَٰئِكَ رَفِيقًا
versets.tr: wa-man yuṭiʿi llāha wa-r-rasūla fa-ulāʾika maʿa lladhīna anʿama llāhu ʿalayhim mina n-nabiyyīna wa-ṣ-ṣiddīqīna wa-sh-shuhadāʾi wa-ṣ-ṣāliḥīna wa-ḥasuna ulāʾika rafīqan
versets.trad: Those who obey God and the Messenger are joined with those whom God has favoured: the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous. What excellent companions are these!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes obedience to the Chosen One the key to the summit: God has set obedience to Muḥammad as the key that opens the stations of the prophets, the truthful, and the martyrs, insofar as that is possible to the community; and it suffices the Messenger, as glory, to be that door. To be "with" them is to share their nearness, not their rank: the goodly company (rafīq) is the fruit of fidelity.

mots.racine: ن ع م
mots.sens: with those whom God has favoured

mots.racine: ن ب أ / ص د ق
ش ه د / ص ل ح
ح س ن / ر ف ق
mots.def: rafīq: a companion on the road.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ الْفَضْلُ مِنَ اللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ عَلِيمًا
versets.tr: dhālika l-faḍlu mina llāhi wa-kafā bi-llāhi ʿalīman
versets.trad: Such is the Bounty that comes from God; and God suffices as the All-Knowing!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī strips all merit from the work: "such is the bounty that comes from God" divests their station of every cause, every due, every motive; for what has appeared to them and fallen to them is nothing but the sheer outpouring of His grace and the first stirring of His generosity. Salvation is gift, not wage; and "God suffices as the All-Knowing" of what is earned therein and what is not.

mots.racine: ف ض ل
mots.sens: such is the bounty of God

mots.racine: ك ف ي / ع ل م

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا خُذُوا حِذْرَكُمْ فَانفِرُوا ثُبَاتٍ أَوِ انفِرُوا جَمِيعًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū khudhū ḥidhrakum fa-nfirū thubātin awi nfirū jamīʿan
versets.trad: O you who believe! Take your precaution; go forth in detachments, or go forth all together.
versets.commentaire: On the occasion of "go forth to combat," Qushayrī opens a typology of flight: flight toward God is the way of those who aspire (al-qāṣidūn); flight with God is the way of those who have arrived (al-wāṣilūn); and none finds rest with God save he who was sincere in his flight toward Him. "Taking precaution" then becomes the vigilance of the heart, and "to flee from all other than Him is the affair of every unifier (muwaḥḥid)."

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ح ذ ر
ن ف ر / ث ب و
ن ف ر / ج م ع

versets.ar: وَإِنَّ مِنكُمْ لَمَن لَّيُبَطِّئَنَّ فَإِنْ أَصَابَتْكُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالَ قَدْ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ إِذْ لَمْ أَكُن مَّعَهُمْ شَهِيدًا
versets.tr: wa-inna minkum la-man la-yubaṭṭiʾanna fa-in aṣābatkum muṣībatun qāla qad anʿama llāhu ʿalayya idh lam akun maʿahum shahīdan
versets.trad: Among you there is surely one who lags behind. If a misfortune befalls you, he says: "God has indeed favoured me, since I was not present with them!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī diagnoses the lingerer: their convictions found no footing upon a single state; bound to their portions of self-interest, the moment they see misfortune shadow the Muslims, they give thanks and say: "Praise be to God who preserved us from following them." Thus they drift: neither an abominable disbeliever, nor a sincere believer. The misfortune of others becomes for them an occasion of ease — sign of a heart that the earthly portion has not let go.

mots.racine: ب ط أ
mots.def: tabṭīʾ: to drag, to delay, to hold back.

mots.racine: ن ع م
mots.def: the false gratitude of the absent.

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.def: shahīd: here, present at the battle.

versets.ar: وَلَئِنْ أَصَابَكُمْ فَضْلٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ لَيَقُولَنَّ كَأَن لَّمْ تَكُن بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُ مَوَدَّةٌ يَا لَيْتَنِي كُنتُ مَعَهُمْ فَأَفُوزَ فَوْزًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-laʾin aṣābakum faḍlun mina llāhi la-yaqūlanna ka-an lam takun baynakum wa-baynahu mawaddatun yā laytanī kuntu maʿahum fa-afūza fawzan ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: But if a bounty from God falls to you, he is sure to say — as though there had never been any affection between you — "Would that I had been with them! I should have won a great triumph!"
themes: the opportunism of the lukewarm heart
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī illumines the "as though there had never been any affection between you": they cast down the decency of shared life and showed no regard for your sanctity. The lukewarm man clings only to gain: absent in peril, he covets the spoils of triumph; his "mawadda" was but calculation, and the bounty granted to others stirs in him only an interested regret.

mots.racine: ص و ب / ف ض ل
mots.sens: a bounty from God falls to you

mots.racine: و د د
mots.sens: as though no affection between you

mots.racine: ف و ز

versets.ar: فَلْيُقَاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ الَّذِينَ يَشْرُونَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا بِالْآخِرَةِ ۚ وَمَن يُقَاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَيُقْتَلْ أَوْ يَغْلِبْ فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: fa-l-yuqātil fī sabīli llāhi lladhīna yashrūna l-ḥayāta d-dunyā bi-l-ākhirati wa-man yuqātil fī sabīli llāhi fa-yuqtal aw yaghlib fa-sawfa nuʾtīhi ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Let those, then, fight in the way of God who would barter the life of this world for the life to come. And to whoever fights in the way of God, whether he is slain or is victorious, We shall grant a magnificent reward.
themes: combat in the way of God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the outward combat the mirror of the inward: he who has not slain his soul within himself wages no valid combat through himself; one must first drive from the heart all concern for the soul, and then deliver the person over to death. And on the "magnificent reward": Our sustenance after him is better for him than his own life lived for himself — the martyr finds in God a substitute that surpasses any survival of the ego.

mots.racine: ش ر ي
mots.sens: they barter this world for the next

mots.racine: ق ت ل / غ ل ب
mots.def: the two outcomes of the combatant.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ ج ر
mots.sens: We shall grant him an immense reward

versets.ar: وَمَا لَكُمْ لَا تُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ وَالْوِلْدَانِ الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا أَخْرِجْنَا مِنْ هَٰذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ الظَّالِمِ أَهْلُهَا وَاجْعَل لَّنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا وَاجْعَل لَّنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-mā lakum lā tuqātilūna fī sabīli llāhi wa-l-mustaḍʿafīna mina r-rijāli wa-n-nisāʾi wa-l-wildāni lladhīna yaqūlūna rabbanā akhrijnā min hādhihi l-qaryati ẓ-ẓālimi ahluhā wa-jʿal lanā min ladunka waliyyan wa-jʿal lanā min ladunka naṣīran
versets.trad: And why should you not fight in the way of God, and for the oppressed among men, women, and children who cry out: "Our Lord! Bring us out of this town whose people are unjust. Raise up for us, from Your presence, a protector; raise up for us, from Your presence, a helper!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the address into a pressing examination of conscience: what holds you back from fighting in the way of God? What would it cost you to offer your souls in God and for God? Do you fear loss with God? Or are you unaware that you shall be brought back to God? And he settles it: why should it not suffice you that He remains, after your annihilation in God? The cry of the oppressed calls for a waliyy and a naṣīr "from Your presence": every real succour descends from sheer largesse.

mots.racine: ض ع ف
ق ر ي / ظ ل م
mots.sens: the town whose people are unjust

mots.racine: ل د ن / و ل ي

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ الطَّاغُوتِ فَقَاتِلُوا أَوْلِيَاءَ الشَّيْطَانِ ۖ إِنَّ كَيْدَ الشَّيْطَانِ كَانَ ضَعِيفًا
versets.tr: alladhīna āmanū yuqātilūna fī sabīli llāhi wa-lladhīna kafarū yuqātilūna fī sabīli ṭ-ṭāghūti fa-qātilū awliyāʾa sh-shayṭāni inna kayda sh-shayṭāni kāna ḍaʿīfan
versets.trad: Those who believe fight in the way of God, while those who disbelieve fight in the way of the false deity (the ṭāghūt). Fight, then, the allies of Satan. Indeed, the cunning of Satan is feeble.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two postures of the heart: the sincere in God prefer nothing to God, withhold nothing from God; they ceaselessly sacrifice their souls for Him — the disbelievers being the very opposite. Then he hears the command "fight the allies of Satan" as a reassurance: "harbour no fear of them, for I am your protector, and I suffice you against your enemies." The cunning of the Devil is feeble because it shatters upon the divine support.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / ط غ ي
و ل ي / ش ط ن
ك ي د / ض ع ف
mots.sens: the cunning of Satan is feeble

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ قِيلَ لَهُمْ كُفُّوا أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ فَلَمَّا كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقِتَالُ إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمْ يَخْشَوْنَ النَّاسَ كَخَشْيَةِ اللَّهِ أَوْ أَشَدَّ خَشْيَةً ۚ وَقَالُوا رَبَّنَا لِمَ كَتَبْتَ عَلَيْنَا الْقِتَالَ لَوْلَا أَخَّرْتَنَا إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ ۗ قُلْ مَتَاعُ الدُّنْيَا قَلِيلٌ وَالْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّمَنِ اتَّقَىٰ وَلَا تُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلًا
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna qīla lahum kuffū aydiyakum wa-aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ātū z-zakāta fa-lammā kutiba ʿalayhimu l-qitālu idhā farīqun minhum yakhshawna n-nāsa ka-khashyati llāhi aw ashadda khashyatan wa-qālū rabbanā lima katabta ʿalaynā l-qitāla lawlā akhkhartanā ilā ajalin qarībin qul matāʿu d-dunyā qalīlun wa-l-ākhiratu khayrun limani ttaqā wa-lā tuẓlamūna fatīlan
versets.trad: Have you not seen those to whom it was said: "Restrain your hands, but perform the prayer and give the alms"? Yet when fighting was prescribed for them, behold, a party of them feared men as one fears God, or with a fear yet keener, and they said: "Our Lord! Why have You prescribed fighting for us? Could You not have granted us a brief respite?" Say: "The enjoyment of this world is little; the world to come is better for him who fears God, and you shall not be wronged by so much as the skin of a date-stone."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the "restrain your hands" into degrees: withdraw your hands from your own affairs and entrust them to your Beloved; abstain from seizing the unlawful; renounce covetings; raise them only toward God, in supplication and entreaty. On "the littleness of the world," he sets an unveiling: He gave you the world, then said: its enjoyment is little; He held it as nothing in your eyes — and yet, if you gave in alms even half a date-stone, you would be delivered from the Fire: behold the height of generosity. Then He weans them from both worlds: "God is better and abides."

mots.racine: ك ف ف / ي د ي
mots.def: kaffa: to abstain, to hold back.

mots.racine: خ ش ي
mots.sens: fearing men as they fear God
mots.def: khashya: a reverential fear, here misplaced.

mots.racine: م ت ع / ق ل ل
mots.sens: the enjoyment of the world is little

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ف ت ل
mots.sens: you shall not be wronged by a thread
mots.def: fatīl: the fibre of the date-stone.

versets.ar: أَيْنَمَا تَكُونُوا يُدْرِككُّمُ الْمَوْتُ وَلَوْ كُنتُمْ فِي بُرُوجٍ مُّشَيَّدَةٍ ۗ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ حَسَنَةٌ يَقُولُوا هَٰذِهِ مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ يَقُولُوا هَٰذِهِ مِنْ عِندِكَ ۚ قُلْ كُلٌّ مِّنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ ۖ فَمَالِ هَٰؤُلَاءِ الْقَوْمِ لَا يَكَادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ حَدِيثًا
versets.tr: aynamā takūnū yudrikkumu l-mawtu wa-law kuntum fī burūjin mushayyadatin wa-in tuṣibhum ḥasanatun yaqūlū hādhihi min ʿindi llāhi wa-in tuṣibhum sayyiʾatun yaqūlū hādhihi min ʿindika qul kullun min ʿindi llāhi fa-māli hāʾulāʾi l-qawmi lā yakādūna yafqahūna ḥadīthan
versets.trad: Wherever you may be, death will overtake you, even were you in towers built high. If good befalls them, they say: "This is from God!" And if ill befalls them, they say: "This is from you!" Say: "All is from God!" What ails these people that they can scarcely grasp what is told them?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reverses the dread of death into joy: death is a joy for the believer; the tidings of its nearness are good news to him, for it is the means that brings him to the Real — and whoever loves the meeting with God, God loves to meet him. As for those who ascribe ill to the creature: through weakness of discernment and a sickness in their belief, the moment an ill touches them they do not trace it back to God; the ancient vein of dualism stirs again within them. Hence the answer: "all is from God," by creation and origination, by working and inventing, by decree and facilitation.

mots.racine: ك و ن / د ر ك / م و ت
mots.sens: wherever you are, death overtakes you

mots.racine: ب ر ج / ش ي د
ك ل ل
mots.def: an affirmation of the oneness of the agency.

mots.racine: ف ق ه

versets.ar: مَّا أَصَابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ فَمِنَ اللَّهِ ۖ وَمَا أَصَابَكَ مِن سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِن نَّفْسِكَ ۚ وَأَرْسَلْنَاكَ لِلنَّاسِ رَسُولًا ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا
versets.tr: mā aṣābaka min ḥasanatin fa-mina llāhi wa-mā aṣābaka min sayyiʾatin fa-min nafsika wa-arsalnāka li-n-nāsi rasūlan wa-kafā bi-llāhi shahīdan
versets.trad: Whatever good befalls you is from God, and whatever ill befalls you is from yourself. We have sent you to mankind as a Messenger, and God suffices as Witness!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī holds both sides together, with no contradiction to the preceding verse: the good that reaches you is from God, by pure bounty (faḍl); the ill that reaches you is from your soul, by acquisition (kasb) — yet both belong to God as to their creation (khalq). The servant ascribes the fault to himself, that he may be humbled, and refers the good to God, that he may give thanks; the creation of both abides with the Real. "God suffices as Witness."

mots.racine: ح س ن
س و أ / ن ف س
mots.sens: all ill is from your soul

mots.racine: ك ف ي / ش ه د

versets.ar: مَّن يُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطَاعَ اللَّهَ ۖ وَمَن تَوَلَّىٰ فَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظًا
versets.tr: man yuṭiʿi r-rasūla fa-qad aṭāʿa llāha wa-man tawallā fa-mā arsalnāka ʿalayhim ḥafīẓan
versets.trad: Whoever obeys the Messenger has obeyed God. As for those who turn away, We have not sent you over them as a keeper.
themes: obeying the Messenger is obeying God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads this verse as a seal of union (jamʿ) concerning the Messenger: God has said: his obedience is Our obedience; thus, whoever draws near to him draws near to Us, what he is pleased with We are pleased with, what he rejects We reject. The person of the Prophet becomes the transparent locus through which the divine will passes: to cleave to him is to cleave to God Himself.

mots.ar: مَّن يُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطَاعَ اللَّهَ
mots.tr: man yuṭiʿi r-rasūla fa-qad aṭāʿa llāha
mots.racine: ط و ع
mots.sens: to obey the Messenger is to obey God
mots.def: the identification of the two obediences.

mots.racine: و ل ي
ر س ل / ح ف ظ
mots.sens: We have not made you their keeper

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ طَاعَةٌ فَإِذَا بَرَزُوا مِنْ عِندِكَ بَيَّتَ طَائِفَةٌ مِّنْهُمْ غَيْرَ الَّذِي تَقُولُ ۖ وَاللَّهُ يَكْتُبُ مَا يُبَيِّتُونَ ۖ فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna ṭāʿatun fa-idhā barazū min ʿindika bayyata ṭāʾifatun minhum ghayra lladhī taqūlu wa-llāhu yaktubu mā yubayyitūna fa-aʿriḍ ʿanhum wa-tawakkal ʿalā llāhi wa-kafā bi-llāhi wakīlan
versets.trad: They say: "Obedience!", yet as soon as they leave your presence a party of them plot by night something other than what they professed to you. God records what they scheme. So turn away from them and place your trust in God: God suffices as a Guardian.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī says: in your presence they submit, for they are bathed in the light of your countenance; but the moment they withdraw, that ray of welcome is taken from them and they fall back into their own darkness. Their obedience was but a reflection, not a clarity of their own. To this ebbing God opposes the command: "turn away from them and rely upon Him," for whoever leans upon the Real need no longer fear the secret councils of the night.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ط و ع
ب ر ز
mots.def: baraza: to withdraw, to come forth.

mots.racine: ب ي ت / ط و ف
mots.def: bayyata: to contrive under cover of night.

mots.racine: و ك ل
ك ف ي / و ك ل

versets.ar: أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ ۚ وَلَوْ كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ غَيْرِ اللَّهِ لَوَجَدُوا فِيهِ اخْتِلَافًا كَثِيرًا
versets.tr: a-fa-lā yatadabbarūna l-qurʾāna wa-law kāna min ʿindi ghayri llāhi la-wajadū fīhi khtilāfan kathīran
versets.trad: Do they not ponder the Qurʾān attentively? Had it come from any other than God, they would have found within it many an inconsistency.
versets.commentaire: To "meditate" (tadabbur), says Qushayrī, is to plunge with the mind to the depths of the meanings and to draw forth their pearls by the subtlety of elucidation. The Qurʾān does not yield itself to hasty reading; its flawless coherence is itself a sign of its origin, for no human hand could weave a discourse free of all contradiction.

mots.racine: د ب ر
mots.def: tadabbara: to consider the deep import.

mots.racine: ق ر أ
غ ي ر
خ ل ف / ك ث ر

versets.ar: وَإِذَا جَاءَهُمْ أَمْرٌ مِّنَ الْأَمْنِ أَوِ الْخَوْفِ أَذَاعُوا بِهِ ۖ وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلَىٰ أُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ لَاتَّبَعْتُمُ الشَّيْطَانَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā jāʾahum amrun mina l-amni awi l-khawfi adhāʿū bihi wa-law raddūhu ilā r-rasūli wa-ilā ulī l-amri minhum la-ʿalimahu lladhīna yastanbiṭūnahu minhum wa-law-lā faḍlu llāhi ʿalaykum wa-raḥmatuhu la-ttabaʿtumu sh-shayṭāna illā qalīlan
versets.trad: When a rumour reaches them, reassuring or alarming, they broadcast it. Had they referred it to the Prophet and to those among them who hold authority, those who could probe the matter would have known whether it deserved credence. Were it not for God's grace and mercy upon you, you would surely have followed the Devil, all but a few of you.
themes: referring matters to those in authority
versets.commentaire: Those who are distracted from the Real, says Qushayrī, have no confidant to whom they may entrust their secret, and so they divulge it to one another. But the believer need not noise his confidence abroad before any creature, for the Knower of his secrets is his Master; it is God who hears his whisper and knows his intimate converse. And were the matter referred to those who can sound its depths, they would dispel the confusion and uphold the others with the light of guidance.

mots.racine: أ م ن / خ و ف
mots.sens: news of safety or of fear

mots.racine: ذ ي ع
mots.def: adhāʿa: to divulge, to spread abroad.

mots.racine: أ م ر
mots.def: ūlū l-amr: those entrusted with charge.

mots.racine: ن ب ط
mots.def: istanbaṭa: to make spring forth, to deduce.

mots.racine: ف ض ل
mots.sens: were it not for God's grace

versets.ar: فَقَاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ لَا تُكَلَّفُ إِلَّا نَفْسَكَ ۚ وَحَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ عَسَى اللَّهُ أَن يَكُفَّ بَأْسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۚ وَاللَّهُ أَشَدُّ بَأْسًا وَأَشَدُّ تَنكِيلًا
versets.tr: fa-qātil fī sabīli llāhi lā tukallafu illā nafsaka wa-ḥarriḍi l-muʾminīna ʿasā llāhu an yakuffa baʾsa lladhīna kafarū wa-llāhu ashaddu baʾsan wa-ashaddu tankīlan
versets.trad: Fight in the path of God. You are accountable only for yourself. Rouse the believers! It may be that God will restrain the violence of those who disbelieve. God is mightier in violence, and mightier in chastisement.
themes: fighting in the path of God
versets.commentaire: "You are charged only with yourself": Qushayrī hears in this an address to the unique model. Stand upright with Us, surrendering your whole being to Us; for just as none is your equal in rank, by the elevation through which you surpass all, We charge no other with that wherewith We have charged you. The solitude of the station is also the solitude of its demand.

mots.racine: ق ت ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: fight in the path of God

mots.racine: ك ل ف / ن ف س
mots.sens: you are charged only with yourself

mots.racine: ح ر ض / أ م ن
mots.def: ḥarraḍa: to incite, to spur on.

mots.racine: ك ف ف / ب أ س
mots.sens: to restrain the violence of the disbelievers

mots.racine: ش د د / ن ك ل

versets.ar: مَّن يَشْفَعْ شَفَاعَةً حَسَنَةً يَكُن لَّهُ نَصِيبٌ مِّنْهَا ۖ وَمَن يَشْفَعْ شَفَاعَةً سَيِّئَةً يَكُن لَّهُ كِفْلٌ مِّنْهَا ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُّقِيتًا
versets.tr: man yashfaʿ shafāʿatan ḥasanatan yakun lahu naṣībun minhā wa-man yashfaʿ shafāʿatan sayyiʾatan yakun lahu kiflun minhā wa-kāna llāhu ʿalā kulli shayʾin muqītan
versets.trad: Whoever intercedes in a good cause shall have a share of it, and whoever intercedes in an evil cause shall bear the burden of it. God is the One who apportions all things with measure.
versets.commentaire: The intercessor, says Qushayrī, by his mediation unties the difficulty of the one for whom he intercedes, and thereby earns, with God, an immense rank. Conversely, whoever labours for corruption takes upon himself the weight and heaps up the sin. Thus every word cast on another's behalf returns upon its speaker, whether as grace or as burden.

mots.racine: ش ف ع / ح س ن
ن ص ب
ك ف ل
ق و ت
mots.sens: who provides for and measures all
mots.def: muqīt: who disposes and nourishes all things with measure.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا حُيِّيتُم بِتَحِيَّةٍ فَحَيُّوا بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْهَا أَوْ رُدُّوهَا ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَسِيبًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā ḥuyyītum bi-taḥiyyatin fa-ḥayyū bi-aḥsana minhā aw ruddūhā inna llāha kāna ʿalā kulli shayʾin ḥasīban
versets.trad: When a greeting is offered to you, return one still more gracious, or at least give the like in reply. God keeps account of all things.
versets.commentaire: A verse of training in fair dealing and the proprieties of companionship, says Qushayrī. Whoever charges you with a kindness lays it upon you as a trust, like a debt: either you surpass his gesture, or you render him no less than its like. Courtesy is no empty politeness, but the acknowledgement of a gift received.

mots.racine: ح ي ي
mots.sens: you are greeted with a greeting

mots.racine: ح ي ي / ح س ن
ر د د
mots.def: radda: to give back, to return.

mots.racine: ح س ب

versets.ar: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ۗ وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللَّهِ حَدِيثًا
versets.tr: allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa la-yajmaʿannakum ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati lā rayba fīhi wa-man aṣdaqu mina llāhi ḥadīthan
versets.trad: God! There is no god but He! He shall surely gather you for the Day of Resurrection, of which there is no doubt. And who is more truthful than God in what He speaks?
versets.commentaire: This word, says Qushayrī, contains a negation and an affirmation: the negation excludes all other, and it is impossible for any other than He to be what He has negated; the affirmation assigns to Him divinity, and it is impossible to deny of Him what He has affirmed. The "lā ilāha illā huwa" is thus the very balance of tawḥīd: nothing enters it, nothing is withdrawn from it.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
mots.def: the formula of tawḥīd: negation then affirmation.

mots.racine: ج م ع
ق و م
mots.def: qiyāma: the raising of the dead.

mots.racine: ص د ق / ح د ث
mots.sens: who is more truthful than God in speech

versets.ar: فَمَا لَكُمْ فِي الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِئَتَيْنِ وَاللَّهُ أَرْكَسَهُم بِمَا كَسَبُوا ۚ أَتُرِيدُونَ أَن تَهْدُوا مَنْ أَضَلَّ اللَّهُ ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: fa-mā lakum fī l-munāfiqīna fiʾatayni wa-llāhu arkasahum bi-mā kasabū a-turīdūna an tahdū man aḍalla llāhu wa-man yuḍlili llāhu fa-lan tajida lahu sabīlan
versets.trad: What ails you that you split into two parties concerning the hypocrites, when God has cast them back for what they earned? Do you wish to guide one whom God has led astray? For him whom God leads astray you shall find no way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī traces these hypocrites back to the immutable decree: My covenant concerning them is that they are My enemies; they shall obtain from Me neither in this world nor the next My good pleasure. You shall not save, by your efforts, the one whom I have fixed in My apportionment. For all rests upon the lot assigned from all eternity, which no creature has power to alter: none guides whom God leads astray.

mots.racine: ن ف ق / ف أ و
mots.sens: why two camps over the hypocrites

mots.racine: ر ك س / ك س ب
mots.sens: He cast them back for their deeds
mots.def: arkasa: to make fall back, to overturn.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ض ل ل
mots.sens: to guide whom God leads astray
mots.def: hadā: to guide; aḍalla: to lead astray.

mots.racine: و ج د / س ب ل
mots.sens: you will find for him no way

versets.ar: وَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَمَا كَفَرُوا فَتَكُونُونَ سَوَاءً ۖ فَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ حَتَّىٰ يُهَاجِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَخُذُوهُمْ وَاقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ وَجَدتُّمُوهُمْ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْهُمْ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: waddū law takfurūna kamā kafarū fa-takūnūna sawāʾan fa-lā tattakhidhū minhum awliyāʾa ḥattā yuhājirū fī sabīli llāhi fa-in tawallaw fa-khudhūhum wa-qtulūhum ḥaythu wajadtumūhum wa-lā tattakhidhū minhum waliyyan wa-lā naṣīran
versets.trad: They would love for you to disbelieve as they have disbelieved, so that you would be alike. Take none of them, then, as protectors until they emigrate in the path of God. If they turn away, seize them and slay them wherever you find them, and take none of them as protector or helper,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads in this the sign addressed to the people of confusion: they dream that the truthful might become like them; but how vain is their wish! So long as they remain dissimilar and discordant, set yourself apart from them, oppose them, do not pattern yourself on them nor keep their company, for a companion attuned to your quest is better than an opponent you would frequent against your heart.

mots.racine: و د د / ك ف ر
mots.sens: they love that you should disbelieve

mots.racine: س و ي
ه ج ر
أ خ ذ / ق ت ل
mots.def: akhadha: to seize; qatala: to slay.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ن ص ر

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُم مِّيثَاقٌ أَوْ جَاءُوكُمْ حَصِرَتْ صُدُورُهُمْ أَن يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ أَوْ يُقَاتِلُوا قَوْمَهُمْ ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَسَلَّطَهُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ فَلَقَاتَلُوكُمْ ۚ فَإِنِ اعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ فَمَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: illā lladhīna yaṣilūna ilā qawmin baynakum wa-baynahum mīthāqun aw jāʾūkum ḥaṣirat ṣudūruhum an yuqātilūkum aw yuqātilū qawmahum wa-law shāʾa llāhu la-sallaṭahum ʿalaykum fa-la-qātalūkum fa-ini ʿtazalūkum fa-lam yuqātilūkum wa-alqaw ilaykumu s-salama fa-mā jaʿala llāhu lakum ʿalayhim sabīlan
versets.trad: save those who join a people with whom you have a pact, or who come to you with breasts straitened at having to fight you or to fight their own people. Had God so willed, He would have given them power over you, and then they would have fought you. So if they withdraw from you, do not fight you, and offer you peace, then God grants you no way against them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the exception a gentleness: in the hour of excuses, it is permitted to keep outward company, out of regard for the weak. And from the withdrawal of others a wisdom: if someone who is not of your quest keeps your company, lingering at the encampments of his worldly portion, do not summon him to your way; leave him to his states. If you can regard him with the eye of mercy so that your aspiration reaches him, do so; if not, commit him to his lot.

mots.racine: و ص ل
و ث ق
ح ص ر / ص د ر
mots.def: ḥaṣira: to be straitened, to recoil.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / س ل م

mots.ar: فَمَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سَبِيلًا
mots.tr: fa-mā jaʿala llāhu lakum ʿalayhim sabīlan
mots.racine: ج ع ل / س ب ل
mots.sens: God grants you no recourse against them

versets.ar: سَتَجِدُونَ آخَرِينَ يُرِيدُونَ أَن يَأْمَنُوكُمْ وَيَأْمَنُوا قَوْمَهُمْ كُلَّ مَا رُدُّوا إِلَى الْفِتْنَةِ أُرْكِسُوا فِيهَا ۚ فَإِن لَّمْ يَعْتَزِلُوكُمْ وَيُلْقُوا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ وَيَكُفُّوا أَيْدِيَهُمْ فَخُذُوهُمْ وَاقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ ۚ وَأُولَٰئِكُمْ جَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: sa-tajidūna ākharīna yurīdūna an yaʾmanūkum wa-yaʾmanū qawmahum kulla mā ruddū ilā l-fitnati urkisū fīhā fa-in lam yaʿtazilūkum wa-yulqū ilaykumu s-salama wa-yakuffū aydiyahum fa-khudhūhum wa-qtulūhum ḥaythu thaqiftumūhum wa-ulāʾikum jaʿalnā lakum ʿalayhim sulṭānan mubīnan
versets.trad: You will find others who wish to be secure both from you and from their own people; yet whenever they are led back to subversion, they plunge into it headlong. If they do not withdraw from you, offer you peace, and stay their hands, then seize them and slay them wherever you overtake them. Against these We have granted you a clear warrant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever wishes to join the two opposites finds his effort frustrated and his design fallen away. As none can be at once hypocrite and submissive, so none can be at once aspirant to the Real and fixed in the usages of habit. For aspiration (irāda) and habit (ʿāda) are two opposites: the duty is to separate the contraries and to keep the strangers at a distance.

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.sens: to be safe on both sides

mots.racine: ر د د / ف ت ن
ر ك س
ث ق ف
س ل ط / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ أَن يَقْتُلَ مُؤْمِنًا إِلَّا خَطَأً ۚ وَمَن قَتَلَ مُؤْمِنًا خَطَأً فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُّؤْمِنَةٍ وَدِيَةٌ مُّسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَىٰ أَهْلِهِ إِلَّا أَن يَصَّدَّقُوا ۚ فَإِن كَانَ مِن قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَّكُمْ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُّؤْمِنَةٍ ۖ وَإِن كَانَ مِن قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُم مِّيثَاقٌ فَدِيَةٌ مُّسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَىٰ أَهْلِهِ وَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُّؤْمِنَةٍ ۖ فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ تَوْبَةً مِّنَ اللَّهِ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna li-muʾminin an yaqtula muʾminan illā khaṭaʾan wa-man qatala muʾminan khaṭaʾan fa-taḥrīru raqabatin muʾminatin wa-diyatun musallamatun ilā ahlihi illā an yaṣṣaddaqū fa-in kāna min qawmin ʿaduwwin lakum wa-huwa muʾminun fa-taḥrīru raqabatin muʾminatin wa-in kāna min qawmin baynakum wa-baynahum mīthāqun fa-diyatun musallamatun ilā ahlihi wa-taḥrīru raqabatin muʾminatin fa-man lam yajid fa-ṣiyāmu shahrayni mutatābiʿayni tawbatan mina llāhi wa-kāna llāhu ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: It is not for a believer to slay a believer, except by mistake. Whoever slays a believer by mistake shall free a believing slave and deliver the blood-price to the family of the slain, unless they remit it as charity. If the slain belonged to a people hostile to you yet was himself a believer, then the freeing of a believing slave. If he belonged to a people with whom you have a pact, then the blood-price delivered to his family and the freeing of a believing slave. Whoever cannot find the means shall fast two consecutive months, as a turning imposed by God. God is all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lightens the case of error by referring it to the solidarity of the clan, then transposes it to the way: the elect are the clan responsible for the weak of the community, the gnostics are the clan of the aspirants, the masters are the clan of the poor. Their rule is to bear the burdens of the weak in whatever befalls them; the high station is no privilege, but a fraternal charge.

mots.racine: خ ط أ
ح ر ر / ر ق ب
و د ي / س ل م
ص و م / ت ب ع
ت و ب

versets.ar: وَمَن يَقْتُلْ مُؤْمِنًا مُّتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَاؤُهُ جَهَنَّمُ خَالِدًا فِيهَا وَغَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَلَعَنَهُ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُ عَذَابًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-man yaqtul muʾminan mutaʿammidan fa-jazāʾuhu jahannamu khālidan fīhā wa-ghaḍiba llāhu ʿalayhi wa-laʿanahu wa-aʿadda lahu ʿadhāban ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Whoever slays a believer deliberately, his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever. God's wrath is upon him; He has cursed him and prepared for him a terrible chastisement.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the prohibition: as it is forbidden you to slay another, so it is forbidden you to slay your own soul; whoever follows his passion sets about shedding the blood of his soul. And he joins to it the responsibility of the guide: whoever does not counsel the aspirant with a fair admonition, and does not assist him with his own aspiration, has set about shedding his blood, and answers for his state before God.

mots.racine: ع م د
ج ز ي
خ ل د
غ ض ب
ل ع ن
mots.def: laʿana: to put far from mercy.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَتَبَيَّنُوا وَلَا تَقُولُوا لِمَنْ أَلْقَىٰ إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَامَ لَسْتَ مُؤْمِنًا تَبْتَغُونَ عَرَضَ الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا فَعِندَ اللَّهِ مَغَانِمُ كَثِيرَةٌ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كُنتُم مِّن قَبْلُ فَمَنَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَتَبَيَّنُوا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū idhā ḍarabtum fī sabīli llāhi fa-tabayyanū wa-lā taqūlū li-man alqā ilaykumu s-salāma lasta muʾminan tabtaghūna ʿaraḍa l-ḥayāti d-dunyā fa-ʿinda llāhi maghānimu kathīratun kadhālika kuntum min qablu fa-manna llāhu ʿalaykum fa-tabayyanū inna llāha kāna bi-mā taʿmalūna khabīran
versets.trad: O you who believe! When you set out in the path of God, be discerning, and do not say to one who offers you peace, "You are no believer!", seeking the fleeting goods of this lower life. With God are abundant spoils. Such were you yourselves before, then God bestowed His grace upon you. So be discerning! God is indeed well aware of what you do.
themes: to accept the outward and entrust the secret
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a charter of good opinion: keep company with people according to what they show of their states, and do not presume falsehood in them, for the Master of secrets is God. This holds for one driven by a corrupt design rising from the soul; but whoever sees by God, from whom nothing is veiled, let him guard the secret that God has unveiled to him and show none of it to his brother.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / س ب ل
mots.sens: when you set out in the path of God
mots.def: ḍaraba fī l-arḍ: to travel, to campaign.

mots.racine: ب ي ن
ل ق ي / س ل م
mots.sens: offers you the greeting of peace

mots.racine: ع ر ض / د ن و
mots.sens: the fleeting goods of this world

mots.racine: غ ن م / ك ث ر

versets.ar: لَّا يَسْتَوِي الْقَاعِدُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ غَيْرُ أُولِي الضَّرَرِ وَالْمُجَاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ عَلَى الْقَاعِدِينَ دَرَجَةً ۚ وَكُلًّا وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ وَفَضَّلَ اللَّهُ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ عَلَى الْقَاعِدِينَ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: lā yastawī l-qāʿidūna mina l-muʾminīna ghayru ulī ḍ-ḍarari wa-l-mujāhidūna fī sabīli llāhi bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim faḍḍala llāhu l-mujāhidīna bi-amwālihim wa-anfusihim ʿalā l-qāʿidīna darajatan wa-kullan waʿada llāhu l-ḥusnā wa-faḍḍala llāhu l-mujāhidīna ʿalā l-qāʿidīna ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Not equal are those believers who sit at home, save those disabled by injury, and those who strive in the path of God with their wealth and their persons. God has raised those who strive with their wealth and their persons a degree above those who sit at home. To each God has promised the fairest reward, yet God has favoured the strivers over those who sit with a magnificent recompense:
themes: the striving of wealth and souls
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Real has gathered all His beloved within His generosity, yet He has set among them degrees. This one is rich, and another richer still; this one is great, and another greater. And he weaves the image: these stars are bright, but the moon is above them; and when the sun rises, it eclipses them all in its light. Thus mounts the ladder of nearnesses.

mots.racine: س و ي / ق ع د
mots.sens: those who sit are not equal
mots.def: istawā: to be equal; qāʿid: one who remains seated.

mots.racine: ض ر ر
ج ه د / م و ل / ن ف س
mots.sens: those who strive with wealth and souls
mots.def: mujāhid: one who strives in the path of God.

versets.ar: دَرَجَاتٍ مِّنْهُ وَمَغْفِرَةً وَرَحْمَةً ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: darajātin minhu wa-maghfiratan wa-raḥmatan wa-kāna llāhu ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: degrees from Him, and forgiveness and mercy. God is ever-forgiving, ever-merciful.
versets.commentaire: Continuation of the preceding verse: the preference unfolds in degrees from Him, and what crowns these degrees is not conquest but forgiveness and mercy. Qushayrī sees in this that the hierarchy of nearnesses remains wholly suspended upon pure favour: none attains the rank save by a gracious gift from the All-Forgiving.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ ظَالِمِي أَنفُسِهِمْ قَالُوا فِيمَ كُنتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا كُنَّا مُسْتَضْعَفِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ قَالُوا أَلَمْ تَكُنْ أَرْضُ اللَّهِ وَاسِعَةً فَتُهَاجِرُوا فِيهَا ۚ فَأُولَٰئِكَ مَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَسَاءَتْ مَصِيرًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna tawaffāhumu l-malāʾikatu ẓālimī anfusihim qālū fīma kuntum qālū kunnā mustaḍʿafīna fī l-arḍi qālū a-lam takun arḍu llāhi wāsiʿatan fa-tuhājirū fīhā fa-ulāʾika maʾwāhum jahannamu wa-sāʾat maṣīran
versets.trad: To those whom the angels take in death while they were wronging their own souls, the angels say: "In what state were you?" They reply: "We were oppressed upon the earth." The angels say: "Was not God's earth spacious enough for you to emigrate within it?" Such men shall have Hell for their abode: an evil destination!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorises the emigration: it concerns the one whom the appointed hour seized while he was captive to his soul and slave to his passions; he has no excuse, having failed to emigrate toward the shade of nearness so as to be delivered from the desire of self. And he seals it with a cutting word: there is, between you and this converse, no veil other than your passion.

mots.racine: و ف ي / م ل ك
mots.sens: the angels take them in death
mots.def: tawaffā: to take up the soul.

versets.ar: إِلَّا الْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ وَالْوِلْدَانِ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ حِيلَةً وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: illā l-mustaḍʿafīna mina r-rijāli wa-n-nisāʾi wa-l-wildāni lā yastaṭīʿūna ḥīlatan wa-lā yahtadūna sabīlan
versets.trad: Save the truly powerless among men, women, and children who can devise no means and are guided to no way.
themes: the soul effaced by the Real
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the weak one incapable of contrivance the sign of another, blessed weakness: those whom the divine realities have so seized as to annihilate them to themselves; they remain moved by Him, with no force or power of their own. Once the Real is established within them, they find toward any other no way, and breathe for no other a single breath: their powerlessness is extinction in God.

versets.ar: فَأُولَٰئِكَ عَسَى اللَّهُ أَن يَعْفُوَ عَنْهُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَفُوًّا غَفُورًا
versets.tr: fa-ulāʾika ʿasā llāhu an yaʿfuwa ʿanhum wa-kāna llāhu ʿafuwwan ghafūran
versets.trad: These, it may be that God will pardon them. God is ever-pardoning, ever-forgiving.
versets.commentaire: According to the apparent sense, says Qushayrī, those whom excuses have held back from the choice, it may be that the Real will overwhelm them with His indulgence out of pure favour. The "it may be" (ʿasā) is not hesitation but a veiled promise: it opens the space of hope where the soul thought itself without escape, for the two Names ʿafuww and ghafūr speak of effacement even before pardon.

mots.racine: ع س ي / ع ف و
mots.sens: it may be that God will efface
mots.def: ʿasā: it may be (hope); ʿafā: to efface.

mots.racine: ع ف و
mots.def: ʿafuww: the One who effaces faults.

mots.racine: غ ف ر

versets.ar: وَمَن يُهَاجِرْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ يَجِدْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُرَاغَمًا كَثِيرًا وَسَعَةً ۚ وَمَن يَخْرُجْ مِن بَيْتِهِ مُهَاجِرًا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ يُدْرِكْهُ الْمَوْتُ فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُ عَلَى اللَّهِ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-man yuhājir fī sabīli llāhi yajid fī l-arḍi murāghaman kathīran wa-saʿatan wa-man yakhruj min baytihi muhājiran ilā llāhi wa-rasūlihi thumma yudrik-hu l-mawtu fa-qad waqaʿa ajruhu ʿalā llāhi wa-kāna llāhu ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: Whoever emigrates in the path of God will find upon the earth many a refuge and ample room. Whoever leaves his home as an emigrant toward God and His Messenger, and is then overtaken by death, his reward falls due upon God. God is ever-forgiving, ever-merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever emigrates in God away from all other than God, and makes his intention upright toward God, finds an ample space in the courtyard of generosity, a lodging in the shade of good pleasure, a life and a breadth beneath the wing of nearness. And he defines the true emigrant: the one who has fled his soul and his passion; and whoever sets out and is seized by the appointed term before arrival alights only in the courtyards of union, and has no halt for his spirit other than the encampments of nearness.

mots.racine: ه ج ر / س ب ل
mots.sens: to emigrate in the path of God

mots.racine: ر غ م
و س ع
د ر ك / م و ت
و ق ع / أ ج ر

versets.ar: وَإِذَا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَقْصُرُوا مِنَ الصَّلَاةِ إِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَن يَفْتِنَكُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۚ إِنَّ الْكَافِرِينَ كَانُوا لَكُمْ عَدُوًّا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā ḍarabtum fī l-arḍi fa-laysa ʿalaykum junāḥun an taqṣurū mina ṣ-ṣalāti in khiftum an yaftinakumu lladhīna kafarū inna l-kāfirīna kānū lakum ʿaduwwan mubīnan
versets.trad: When you travel through the land, there is no blame upon you if you shorten the prayer, for fear that those who disbelieve may harass you. The disbelievers are for you a manifest enemy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this licence a trait of divine generosity: the permission for intimate converse remains open at every time. If you wish to enter, enter when you will; and if you wish to step aside by availing yourself of the dispensation, you have what you will. And he concludes: therein is the height of generosity, the safeguard of fidelity, and the realisation of the true meaning of attachment (walāʾ).

mots.racine: ض ر ب / أ ر ض
mots.sens: when you travel through the land

mots.racine: ج ن ح
ق ص ر / ص ل و

versets.ar: وَإِذَا كُنتَ فِيهِمْ فَأَقَمْتَ لَهُمُ الصَّلَاةَ فَلْتَقُمْ طَائِفَةٌ مِّنْهُم مَّعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوا أَسْلِحَتَهُمْ فَإِذَا سَجَدُوا فَلْيَكُونُوا مِن وَرَائِكُمْ وَلْتَأْتِ طَائِفَةٌ أُخْرَىٰ لَمْ يُصَلُّوا فَلْيُصَلُّوا مَعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوا حِذْرَهُمْ وَأَسْلِحَتَهُمْ ۗ وَدَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ تَغْفُلُونَ عَنْ أَسْلِحَتِكُمْ وَأَمْتِعَتِكُمْ فَيَمِيلُونَ عَلَيْكُم مَّيْلَةً وَاحِدَةً ۚ وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِن كَانَ بِكُمْ أَذًى مِّن مَّطَرٍ أَوْ كُنتُم مَّرْضَىٰ أَن تَضَعُوا أَسْلِحَتَكُمْ ۖ وَخُذُوا حِذْرَكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا
versets.tr: wa-idhā kunta fīhim fa-aqamta lahumu ṣ-ṣalāta fa-l-taqum ṭāʾifatun minhum maʿaka wa-l-yaʾkhudhū asliḥatahum fa-idhā sajadū fa-l-yakūnū min warāʾikum wa-l-taʾti ṭāʾifatun ukhrā lam yuṣallū fa-l-yuṣallū maʿaka wa-l-yaʾkhudhū ḥidhrahum wa-asliḥatahum wadda lladhīna kafarū law taghfulūna ʿan asliḥatikum wa-amtiʿatikum fa-yamīlūna ʿalaykum maylatan wāḥidatan wa-lā junāḥa ʿalaykum in kāna bikum adhan min maṭarin aw kuntum marḍā an taḍaʿū asliḥatakum wa-khudhū ḥidhrakum inna llāha aʿadda li-l-kāfirīna ʿadhāban muhīnan
versets.trad: When you are among them and lead them in prayer, let one party of them stand with you, bearing their arms. When they have prostrated, let them fall back behind you, and let another party who have not yet prayed come and pray with you, taking their precaution and their arms. The disbelievers would love for you to be heedless of your arms and your baggage, so as to fall upon you in a single onslaught. There is no blame upon you, if rain harms you or you are sick, to lay down your arms; but take your precaution. God has prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating chastisement.
themes: the prayer that cannot be relinquished
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the prayer of danger a truth of principle: prayer never lapses from the servant so long as there remains in him a breath of free choice — neither in fear nor in security; neither under the sway of the prescriptions of the Law when you are in dispersion (tafriqa), nor under the dominion of the Reality when you are in the eye of union (jamʿ). No station exempts from the face-to-face.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
mots.sens: you lead the prayer for them
mots.def: aqāma ṣ-ṣalāt: to perform the prayer.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / س ل ح
ح ذ ر
م ي ل
mots.sens: to fall upon you in a single onslaught

versets.ar: فَإِذَا قَضَيْتُمُ الصَّلَاةَ فَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِكُمْ ۚ فَإِذَا اطْمَأْنَنتُمْ فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ ۚ إِنَّ الصَّلَاةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَّوْقُوتًا
versets.tr: fa-idhā qaḍaytumu ṣ-ṣalāta fa-dhkurū llāha qiyāman wa-quʿūdan wa-ʿalā junūbikum fa-idhā ṭmaʾnantum fa-aqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta inna ṣ-ṣalāta kānat ʿalā l-muʾminīna kitāban mawqūtan
versets.trad: When you have completed the prayer, remember God standing, sitting, and lying upon your sides. When you are secure, perform the prayer in full. The prayer is for the believers a prescription fixed to appointed times.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes the rite from the heart: the outward tasks are at fixed hours, but the presence of the heart through remembrance (dhikr) is perpetual, without interruption. And he warns: through the forms, remembrance has its time and its untime; but through the hearts — beware absence from the Reality, even for an instant, whatever the states that pass over you. The dhikr is of all times; the prayer, when you are at peace.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / ص ل و
ذ ك ر
ق و م / ق ع د / ج ن ب
ط م أ ن
mots.def: iṭmaʾanna: to be at peace, secure.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَهِنُوا فِي ابْتِغَاءِ الْقَوْمِ ۖ إِن تَكُونُوا تَأْلَمُونَ فَإِنَّهُمْ يَأْلَمُونَ كَمَا تَأْلَمُونَ ۖ وَتَرْجُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَرْجُونَ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-lā tahinū fī btighāʾi l-qawmi in takūnū taʾlamūna fa-innahum yaʾlamūna kamā taʾlamūna wa-tarjūna mina llāhi mā lā yarjūna wa-kāna llāhu ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: Do not grow faint in the pursuit of these people. If you suffer, they too suffer as you suffer; but you hope from God for what they cannot hope. God is all-knowing, all-wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: stand upright by God, and let your support in the struggle be in God. Then he draws out the difference: your adversaries share with you the sensation of pain, but they differ from you in the contemplation of the heart; you see what they do not see, you find for your hearts what they do not find — it does not befit you, then, to lag behind them in the effort.

ب غ ي
أ ل م

mots.ar: وَتَرْجُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَرْجُونَ
mots.tr: wa-tarjūna mina llāhi mā lā yarjūna

versets.ar: إِنَّا أَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِتَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِمَا أَرَاكَ اللَّهُ ۚ وَلَا تَكُن لِّلْخَائِنِينَ خَصِيمًا
versets.tr: innā anzalnā ilayka l-kitāba bi-l-ḥaqqi li-taḥkuma bayna n-nāsi bi-mā arāka llāhu wa-lā takun li-l-khāʾinīna khaṣīman
versets.trad: We have indeed sent down to you the Book with the Truth, that you may judge between men by what God has shown you. So do not be a pleader for the treacherous,
themes: to judge by the revealed light
not to plead for the treacherous
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He did not command you to judge among them blindly, but "by what God has shown you" — that is, by what He has unveiled to you of the lights of clear-sightedness (baṣīra), until you halt upon it, through His instruction and His support; and likewise for whoever judges with truth within your community. As for "do not be the advocate of the treacherous": do not plead for the people of the portions of ego, but stand with the people of the rights of the Real.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب
mots.sens: We sent down to you the Book

mots.racine: ح ق ق
ح ك م / ن و س
ر أ ي
mots.sens: by what God has shown you
mots.def: arā: to show, to make see.

mots.racine: خ و ن / خ ص م

versets.ar: وَاسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-staghfiri llāha inna llāha kāna ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: but seek forgiveness of God! God is ever-forgiving, ever-merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the command toward intercession: seek forgiveness of God for your community, for We have already spared you concern for your own case by Our word: "that God may forgive you your former and latter faults." The istighfār of the Prophet is thus not for himself but an overflowing of mercy upon his own: the station of the forgiven becomes a source of forgiveness for others.

mots.racine: غ ف ر
غ ف ر
ر ح م

versets.ar: وَلَا تُجَادِلْ عَنِ الَّذِينَ يَخْتَانُونَ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَن كَانَ خَوَّانًا أَثِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-lā tujādil ʿani lladhīna yakhtānūna anfusahum inna llāha lā yuḥibbu man kāna khawwānan athīman
versets.trad: Do not plead on behalf of those who betray themselves. God loves not the one who is a sinful traitor.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are those who prefer their portions of ego to the rights of the Real, who delight to linger at the encampments of their passion rather than emigrate toward the dwellings of good pleasure. Now God loves not the people of treachery: He abases them — inevitably — and does not honour them. To betray oneself is to fail the trust of the fear that the soul once bore.

mots.racine: ج د ل
خ و ن / ن ف س
mots.def: ikhtāna: to betray, to abuse a trust.

mots.racine: ح ب ب
خ و ن / أ ث م
mots.def: khawwān: great traitor; athīm: steeped in sin.

versets.ar: يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَلَا يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ مَا لَا يَرْضَىٰ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطًا
versets.tr: yastakhfūna mina n-nāsi wa-lā yastakhfūna mina llāhi wa-huwa maʿahum idh yubayyitūna mā lā yarḍā mina l-qawli wa-kāna llāhu bi-mā yaʿmalūna muḥīṭan
versets.trad: They seek to hide from men, but cannot hide from God, who is with them when they contrive by night discourse that He does not approve. God encompasses all that they do.
themes: to hide from men, not from God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes their blindness: what dominates their hearts is the sight of creatures, and they do not sense that the Real is acquainted with their hearts. Behold those upon whose heart God has set the seal of separation (firqa): they tremble before the gaze of men and forget the Gaze that encompasses them. To hide from the creature and lay oneself bare before the Creator — such is the illusion of the dissociated.

mots.racine: خ ف ي / ن و س
mots.def: istakhfā: to conceal oneself from sight.
maʿiyya: the encompassing presence of God.

mots.racine: ب ي ت
mots.def: bayyata: to contrive in the dark.

mots.racine: ر ض و / ق و ل
mots.sens: discourse that He does not approve

mots.racine: ح و ط

versets.ar: هَا أَنتُمْ هَٰؤُلَاءِ جَادَلْتُمْ عَنْهُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا فَمَن يُجَادِلُ اللَّهَ عَنْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ أَم مَّن يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: hā antum hāʾulāʾi jādaltum ʿanhum fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā fa-man yujādilu llāha ʿanhum yawma l-qiyāmati am man yakūnu ʿalayhim wakīlan
versets.trad: There you are, pleading their cause in the life of this world. But who will plead their cause before God on the Day of Resurrection, and who will be their guardian?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī hears in this a warning concerning fleeting protection: We turn evil away from them through your honour, O believers, because you are among them. But what will their state be on the Day of Resurrection, when your blessings have withdrawn from them? The presence of the righteous is a veil of grace over the guilty; that veil lifted, they will appear alone and naked before the Judge.

mots.racine: ج د ل
mots.def: jādala: to argue on behalf of.

mots.racine: ج د ل
mots.sens: who will plead before God for them

mots.racine: و ك ل

versets.ar: وَمَن يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-man yaʿmal sūʾan aw yaẓlim nafsahu thumma yastaghfiri llāha yajidi llāha ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: Whoever does an evil or wrongs his own soul, then seeks forgiveness of God, shall find God ever-forgiving, ever-merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on two words. The "then" (thumma) marks the delay: they pass their life in dissipation and transgressions, then, at the term of their days, they implore the forgiveness of God. And on "he shall find (yajid) God": finding (wujūd) is the height of converse. The sinner asks only for pardon, but God, by His favour, leads him if He will to the very term — for His custom is to grant beyond the hoped-for to whoever hopes in Him.

mots.racine: ع م ل / س و أ
ظ ل م / ن ف س
غ ف ر
و ج د / غ ف ر

versets.ar: وَمَن يَكْسِبْ إِثْمًا فَإِنَّمَا يَكْسِبُهُ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-man yaksib ithman fa-innamā yaksibuhu ʿalā nafsihi wa-kāna llāhu ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: Whoever earns a sin earns it only against himself. And God is all-knowing, all-wise.
themes: the sin that returns upon oneself
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī marks the transcendence: the Real has no need of the obedience of the obedient nor of the failing of the rebellious. Whoever obeys has but acquired his own portion; whoever disobeys has but taken his own portion. The act adds nothing to God nor takes anything from Him: it returns wholly to its author, as favour or as burden. Such is the hidden sense of "against himself."

mots.racine: ك س ب / أ ث م
ك س ب / ن ف س
mots.def: ʿalā nafsihi: to his own charge.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَمَن يَكْسِبْ خَطِيئَةً أَوْ إِثْمًا ثُمَّ يَرْمِ بِهِ بَرِيئًا فَقَدِ احْتَمَلَ بُهْتَانًا وَإِثْمًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: wa-man yaksib khaṭīʾatan aw ithman thumma yarmi bihi barīʾan fa-qadi ḥtamala buhtānan wa-ithman mubīnan
versets.trad: Whoever earns a fault or a sin, then casts it upon an innocent, has burdened himself with a slander and a manifest sin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reveals the secret reversal: whoever imputes to an innocent a flaw that is his own, God turns the affair against him: He clothes that innocent in the reward of the fine deeds of his calumniator, draws the hem of pardon over the innocent's faults, and overturns the state of the transgressor so as to unmask him among his peers, in the whole of his states. The lie cast upon another becomes the mirror in which the liar is read.

mots.racine: ك س ب / خ ط أ / أ ث م
mots.sens: earns a fault or a sin

mots.racine: ر م ي / ب ر أ
mots.sens: then casts it upon an innocent
mots.def: ramā: to cast, to accuse; barīʾ: innocent.

mots.racine: ح م ل
mots.def: iḥtamala: to bear, to take on.

mots.racine: ب ه ت / أ ث م / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكَ وَرَحْمَتُهُ لَهَمَّت طَّائِفَةٌ مِّنْهُمْ أَن يُضِلُّوكَ وَمَا يُضِلُّونَ إِلَّا أَنفُسَهُمْ ۖ وَمَا يَضُرُّونَكَ مِن شَيْءٍ ۚ وَأَنزَلَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَكَ مَا لَمْ تَكُن تَعْلَمُ ۚ وَكَانَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكَ عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-law-lā faḍlu llāhi ʿalayka wa-raḥmatuhu la-hammat ṭāʾifatun minhum an yuḍillūka wa-mā yuḍillūna illā anfusahum wa-mā yaḍurrūnaka min shayʾin wa-anzala llāhu ʿalayka l-kitāba wa-l-ḥikmata wa-ʿallamaka mā lam takun taʿlamu wa-kāna faḍlu llāhi ʿalayka ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: Were it not for God's grace upon you and His mercy, a party of them would have sought to lead you astray; yet they lead astray none but themselves, and in nothing do they harm you. God has sent down upon you the Book and the Wisdom, and has taught you what you did not know. God's grace upon you is immense.
versets.commentaire: "Favour," says Qushayrī, "is an unmerited benefit"; and here it denotes the preservation (ʿiṣma) granted to the Prophet: the Real has guarded him, by a grace that distinguishes him, from any failing in His right, and has turned away from him the guile of His creatures. And he marvels at "He has taught you what you did not know": no light is for anyone save as borrowed from your light; whoever does not walk beneath your banner does not attain the whole of Our bounty, nor enjoys Our nearness and Our union. Such is the secret of "your grace is immense."

mots.racine: ف ض ل
mots.sens: were it not for God's grace upon you

mots.racine: ه م م / ط و ف
mots.sens: a party of them would have purposed

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ن ف س
mots.sens: they lead astray none but themselves

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ح ك م
ع ل م
mots.sens: He taught you what you did not know

versets.ar: لَّا خَيْرَ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِّن نَّجْوَاهُمْ إِلَّا مَنْ أَمَرَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَوْ مَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ إِصْلَاحٍ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ ابْتِغَاءَ مَرْضَاتِ اللَّهِ فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: lā khayra fī kathīrin min najwāhum illā man amara bi-ṣadaqatin aw maʿrūfin aw iṣlāḥin bayna n-nāsi wa-man yafʿal dhālika btighāʾa marḍāti llāhi fa-sawfa nuʾtīhi ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: There is no good in much of their secret talk, save in him who enjoins a charity, a kindness, or a reconciliation between people. To whoever does this seeking the good pleasure of God, We shall grant a magnificent reward.
themes: the act that overflows onto others
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the highest works are those whose blessing overflows from their author onto others. He widens charity (ṣadaqa) to embrace every form of beneficence, and distinguishes charity toward oneself — to bring one's soul to render the rights of God, to restrain it from transgressing His command, to withdraw one's hand from harming people, to preserve one's thoughts and beliefs from evil — and charity toward others, through wealth, through the heart, and through the body. And fotowwa (spiritual chivalry) is that your effort be for another than yourself, for "the worst of men is the one who eats alone."

mots.ar: لَّا خَيْرَ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِّن نَّجْوَاهُمْ
mots.tr: lā khayra fī kathīrin min najwāhum
mots.racine: خ ي ر / ن ج و
mots.sens: no good in much of their secret talk

mots.racine: أ م ر / ص د ق
ع ر ف
mots.def: maʿrūf: every good approved by the Law.

mots.racine: ص ل ح
ب غ ي / ر ض و
mots.sens: seeking the good pleasure of God

versets.ar: وَمَن يُشَاقِقِ الرَّسُولَ مِن بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الْهُدَىٰ وَيَتَّبِعْ غَيْرَ سَبِيلِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ نُوَلِّهِ مَا تَوَلَّىٰ وَنُصْلِهِ جَهَنَّمَ ۖ وَسَاءَتْ مَصِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-man yushāqiqi r-rasūla min baʿdi mā tabayyana lahu l-hudā wa-yattabiʿ ghayra sabīli l-muʾminīna nuwallihi mā tawallā wa-nuṣlihi jahannama wa-sāʾat maṣīran
versets.trad: Whoever splits from the Messenger after the right guidance has become clear to him, and follows a way other than that of the believers, We shall consign him to what he has turned to, and We shall make him burn in Hell. An evil destination!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorises the rupture: the inspirations of the Real (khawāṭir al-Ḥaqq) are His emissaries to the servant; whoever contradicts the indications to which he is summoned by the inward way merits the chastisements of hearts — among them, to be blinded to the sight of his own rectitude. And just as one who opposes the consensus departs from the religion, one who opposes what he has known of the Reality after the way had become clear to him — such a one falls.

mots.racine: ش ق ق / ر س ل
mots.def: shāqqa: to split off, to rise against.

mots.ar: مِن بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الْهُدَىٰ
mots.tr: min baʿdi mā tabayyana lahu l-hudā
mots.racine: ب ي ن / ه د ي
mots.sens: after the guidance was made clear to him

mots.racine: س ب ل / أ م ن
mots.sens: a way other than the believers'

mots.racine: و ل ي
mots.sens: We turn him to what he has chosen
mots.def: wallā: to consign to, to abandon to.

mots.racine: ص ل ي
mots.sens: and We make him burn in Hell
mots.def: aṣlā: to make enter the fire.

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَن يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلَالًا بَعِيدًا
versets.tr: inna llāha lā yaghfiru an yushraka bihi wa-yaghfiru mā dūna dhālika li-man yashāʾu wa-man yushrik bi-llāhi fa-qad ḍalla ḍalālan baʿīdan
versets.trad: God does not forgive that anything be associated with Him, but He forgives what is short of that to whom He wills. Whoever associates aught with God has strayed into a far-distant error.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī delves into tawḥīd to its very depth: to posit another, even in the imagining of an atom of creative power, is the very essence of association: no pardon has place there. But short of association, pardon has its place. And he adds, formidably: whoever seeks a means toward Him through what he imagines to come from his own soul has already associated without knowing it. Nay! He is God, the One. The subtle shirk is to claim a being of one's own.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ش ر ك
mots.sens: He does not forgive being associated with

mots.racine: غ ف ر
mots.sens: He forgives what is short of that
mots.def: mā dūna: that which is lesser.

mots.racine: ش ي أ
ض ل ل / ب ع د

versets.ar: إِن يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ إِلَّا إِنَاثًا وَإِن يَدْعُونَ إِلَّا شَيْطَانًا مَّرِيدًا
versets.tr: in yadʿūna min dūnihi illā ināthan wa-in yadʿūna illā shayṭānan marīdan
versets.trad: They invoke, apart from Him, none but female deities, and they invoke none but a rebellious Satan,
themes: the illusion of a name laid upon the inert
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have laid upon inert things names that those things do not bear, embarked upon the thread of imagination, trusted to the mirages of conjecture — and so have strayed far from the Reality. As for the "rebellious Satan": they invoke none but Iblīs, whom the Real has put far from His mercy and cast down by His estrangement; and Iblīs himself is but turned about within the Hand, according to what the Creator wills. Had he in himself an atom of affirmed being, he would thereby be an associate in divinity. Nay!

mots.racine: د ع و / د و ن
mots.def: daʿā: to invoke; dūn: apart from.

mots.racine: أ ن ث
ش ط ن / م ر د

versets.ar: لَّعَنَهُ اللَّهُ ۘ وَقَالَ لَأَتَّخِذَنَّ مِنْ عِبَادِكَ نَصِيبًا مَّفْرُوضًا
versets.tr: laʿanahu llāhu wa-qāla la-attakhidhanna min ʿibādika naṣīban mafrūḍan
versets.trad: whom God has cursed, and who said: "I shall surely take an appointed portion of Your servants;
versets.commentaire: Continuation of Qushayrī's commentary on the cursed demon: he recalls that Iblīs, cast out from mercy, is but an instrument turned about within the divine power. His claim to "take an appointed portion" of the servants has no reality save what the Real deposits therein: it is God who sets states running over creatures, and who creates, in the wake of the demon's suggestions, their straying — He is therefore the Guide and the Misleader, who disposes of all things.

mots.racine: ل ع ن
ق و ل
أ خ ذ
ن ص ب / ف ر ض

versets.ar: وَلَأُضِلَّنَّهُمْ وَلَأُمَنِّيَنَّهُمْ وَلَآمُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُبَتِّكُنَّ آذَانَ الْأَنْعَامِ وَلَآمُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُغَيِّرُنَّ خَلْقَ اللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّخِذِ الشَّيْطَانَ وَلِيًّا مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ فَقَدْ خَسِرَ خُسْرَانًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: wa-la-uḍillannahum wa-la-umanniyannahum wa-la-āmurannahum fa-la-yubattikunna ādhāna l-anʿāmi wa-la-āmurannahum fa-la-yughayyirunna khalqa llāhi wa-man yattakhidhi sh-shayṭāna waliyyan min dūni llāhi fa-qad khasira khusrānan mubīnan
versets.trad: and I shall surely lead them astray, and fill them with false hopes, and command them so that they will slit the ears of the cattle, and command them so that they will alter the creation of God." Whoever takes Satan for a protector instead of God has suffered a manifest loss.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī restores the full weight of the demon's verbs by referring them to the divine sovereignty: He gives rise in their hearts, in the wake of the suggestions, to the lengthening of hopes (ṭūl al-amal), He makes the ugliness of acts seem fair in their eyes, then grants their cravings no fulfilment and follows what they hoped for with no confirmation. Thus God is the author of all these effects, which He ascribes now to the demon, now to the disbeliever: "I shall lead them astray and lull them with hopes."

mots.racine: ض ل ل
mots.sens: I shall surely lead them astray

mots.racine: م ن ي
mots.sens: I shall surely lull them with chimeras
mots.def: mannā: to make hope in vain.

mots.racine: ب ت ك / أ ذ ن / ن ع م
mots.sens: they will slit the ears of the cattle

mots.racine: غ ي ر / خ ل ق
mots.sens: they will alter the creation of God

mots.racine: خ س ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: يَعِدُهُمْ وَيُمَنِّيهِمْ ۖ وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ إِلَّا غُرُورًا
versets.tr: yaʿiduhum wa-yumannīhim wa-mā yaʿiduhumu sh-shayṭānu illā ghurūran
versets.trad: The Devil makes them promises and stirs their longings; yet the Devil promises them nothing but delusion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī seals the sequence: the demon makes craving seem fair, lengthens hope, but realises nothing and keeps nothing. His promise is ghurūr — pure lure, a mirage without water. Such is the lot of whoever entrusts himself to other than God: he is painted a future that never comes, for the one who promises has himself no power to accomplish, being but an instrument turned about within the Hand of the one Real.

mots.racine: و ع د
م ن ي
و ع د / غ ر ر
mots.sens: the demon promises them only delusion

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ مَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَلَا يَجِدُونَ عَنْهَا مَحِيصًا
versets.tr: ulāʾika maʾwāhum jahannamu wa-lā yajidūna ʿanhā maḥīṣan
versets.trad: Such as these shall have Gehenna for their abode, and they shall find no refuge from it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to whomever the eternal apportioning has assigned wandering in the present moment, the decree has fixed chastisement in the world to come. At once he stresses how rare the true station is: "to stand firmly upon the truth of Tawḥīd is a precious thing, and the people of Tawḥīd are few." The absence of any "refuge" (maḥīṣ) figures the enclosure of the one whom nothing but God could ever deliver.

mots.racine: أ و ي
mots.def: maʾwā: the place to which one withdraws.

mots.racine: ح ي ص
mots.def: maḥīṣ: a place of flight, of evasion.
