versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۖ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقًّا ۚ وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللَّهِ قِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti sa-nudkhiluhum jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā abadan waʿda llāhi ḥaqqan wa-man aṣdaqu mina llāhi qīlan
versets.trad: But those who believe and perform righteous deeds, We shall admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever. Such is the true promise of God. And who, in His speech, is more truthful than God?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those whom We have made happy by the decree and by the word, We succoured at the very instant We brought them into being, out of pure generosity and bounty. The promised beatitude is no wage wrenched from Him, but the fulfilment of a gift: God realizes for them the announced reward by honouring them with the fair return. And none is "more truthful than God in His speech."

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
و ع د / ح ق ق
mots.sens: the promise of God, in truth

mots.racine: ص د ق / ق و ل
mots.def: aṣdaq: more truthful; qīl: saying, utterance.

versets.ar: لَّيْسَ بِأَمَانِيِّكُمْ وَلَا أَمَانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ ۗ مَن يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ وَلَا يَجِدْ لَهُ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: laysa bi-amāniyyikum wa-lā amāniyyi ahli l-kitābi man yaʿmal sūʾan yujza bihi wa-lā yajid lahu min dūni llāhi waliyyan wa-lā naṣīran
versets.trad: It is not by your desires, nor by the desires of the People of the Book! Whoever does evil shall be requited for it, and he shall find, apart from God, neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī cuts through the illusion of pious wishes with an image: whoever sows colocynth reaps neither the rose nor the narcissus; whoever drinks deadly poison does not taste the savour of honey. Likewise whoever squanders the right of service cannot take his seat upon the carpet of nearness. Salvation rests not upon desires but upon the sincerity of servanthood — and there is no recourse apart from God.

mots.racine: م ن ي
ج ز ي
mots.sens: he shall be requited for it
mots.def: juziya: to be repaid in kind.

mots.racine: و ل ي / ن ص ر
mots.def: walī: a near ally; naṣīr: one who brings aid.

versets.ar: وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِنَ الصَّالِحَاتِ مِن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولَٰئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ نَقِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-man yaʿmal mina ṣ-ṣāliḥāti min dhakarin aw unthā wa-huwa muʾminun fa-ulāʾika yadkhulūna l-jannata wa-lā yuẓlamūna naqīran
versets.trad: And whoever performs righteous deeds, whether male or female, while being a believer, such shall enter the Garden, and they shall not be wronged so much as the speck on a date-stone.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever labours in Our service is not left bereft of attaining Our favour; nay, the one whom We enrich in his quest for Us, We honour with Our presence; and the one to whom We give to drink the cup of Our yearning, We grant the intimacy of Our encounter. Man and woman are here upon the same carpet: it is active faith that opens the door, and none is wronged even by "the hollow of a date-stone."

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / أ ن ث
أ م ن
ن ق ر
mots.def: naqīr: the tiny groove on the back of the stone.

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دِينًا مِّمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ وَاتَّبَعَ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا ۗ وَاتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-man aḥsanu dīnan mimman aslama wajhahu li-llāhi wa-huwa muḥsinun wa-ttabaʿa millata ibrāhīma ḥanīfan wa-ttakhadha llāhu ibrāhīma khalīlan
versets.trad: And who professes a fairer religion than he who surrenders his whole self to God, conducts himself with excellence, and follows the creed of Abraham, a pure believer, whom God took as an intimate friend?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: none has a fairer religion than he who "surrenders his face to God" — that is, who unifies his intention toward Him alone, purifies his bond of all that is not He, then abandons himself in all his states to God by God, retaining nothing for himself: neither of his wealth, nor of his body, nor of his soul, nor of his kin. Such was the way of Abraham. As for khulla: intimate friendship is an adornment in which the Real clothes one, not a quality the servant acquires — the khalīl is he who is in utter need of God at every breath, possessing nothing of himself, but being through God and for God.

mots.racine: س ل م / و ج ه
mots.def: aslama: to deliver up; wajh: the face, the whole person.

mots.racine: ح ن ف
mots.sens: pure believer, inclined toward the true
mots.def: ḥanīf: pure monotheist, turned away from the false.

mots.racine: خ ل ل
mots.def: khalīl: the friend whose love pervades the whole being.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُّحِيطًا
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-kāna llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin muḥīṭan
versets.trad: To God belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth, and He encompasses all things in His Knowledge and His Power.
versets.commentaire: All that is in the heavens and the earth is His property and His domain; nothing escapes His encompassing (iḥāṭa). If Abraham was taken as an intimate friend, it was not that God had need of him: khulla is a pure gift, for He to whom all things belong and who girds all things with His knowledge and His power has need of no one, while the servant cannot for a single instant do without Him.

mots.racine: س م و
ح و ط
mots.def: muḥīṭ: that which surrounds on every side.

versets.ar: وَيَسْتَفْتُونَكَ فِي النِّسَاءِ ۖ قُلِ اللَّهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِيهِنَّ وَمَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الْكِتَابِ فِي يَتَامَى النِّسَاءِ اللَّاتِي لَا تُؤْتُونَهُنَّ مَا كُتِبَ لَهُنَّ وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَن تَنكِحُوهُنَّ وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الْوِلْدَانِ وَأَن تَقُومُوا لِلْيَتَامَىٰ بِالْقِسْطِ ۚ وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِهِ عَلِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-yastaftūnaka fī n-nisāʾi quli llāhu yuftīkum fīhinna wa-mā yutlā ʿalaykum fī l-kitābi fī yatāmā n-nisāʾi llātī lā tuʾtūnahunna mā kutiba lahunna wa-targhabūna an tankiḥūhunna wa-l-mustaḍʿafīna mina l-wildāni wa-an taqūmū li-l-yatāmā bi-l-qisṭi wa-mā tafʿalū min khayrin fa-inna llāha kāna bihi ʿalīman
versets.trad: They ask you for instruction concerning women. Say: "God instructs you concerning them. What is recited to you in the Book bears upon the orphan girls to whom you do not give what is prescribed in their favour, while you desire to marry them; it bears also upon your duties toward minor children, and upon your acting with equity toward the orphans." Whatever good you do, God knows it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He forbade them the covetousness that drives them to injustice and oppression against the weak — women and orphans — and made known that it is He who takes their vengeance. Likewise: whoever watches over them out of fear of God loses nothing with Him, but finds the beauty of the reward; whoever dares to wrong them endures for it a grievous trial. The frailty of another is entrusted to the believer's vigilance beneath the gaze of the All-Knowing.

mots.racine: ف ت ي
mots.def: istaftā: to seek a legal opinion.

mots.racine: ي ت م / ن س و
mots.def: yatāmā: plural of yatīm, the fatherless.

mots.racine: ض ع ف / و ل د
mots.def: mustaḍʿaf: held to be weak; wildān: children.

versets.ar: وَإِنِ امْرَأَةٌ خَافَتْ مِن بَعْلِهَا نُشُوزًا أَوْ إِعْرَاضًا فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا أَن يُصْلِحَا بَيْنَهُمَا صُلْحًا ۚ وَالصُّلْحُ خَيْرٌ ۗ وَأُحْضِرَتِ الْأَنفُسُ الشُّحَّ ۚ وَإِن تُحْسِنُوا وَتَتَّقُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-ini mraʾatun khāfat min baʿlihā nushūzan aw iʿrāḍan fa-lā junāḥa ʿalayhimā an yuṣliḥā baynahumā ṣulḥan wa-ṣ-ṣulḥu khayrun wa-uḥḍirati l-anfusu sh-shuḥḥa wa-in tuḥsinū wa-tattaqū fa-inna llāha kāna bi-mā taʿmalūna khabīran
versets.trad: When a woman fears ill-treatment or estrangement from her husband, no blame shall fall upon them if they reach an amicable settlement, for such a settlement is the better course; yet souls are prone to avarice. If you act with excellence and fear God, know that God is informed of all you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of the verse a school of magnanimity: he who returns to God with his heart sees his affair unified, his breast widens to bear another's ill character; he draws the hem of pardon over the faults of all, and prefers reconciliation by relinquishing his own portion. Hence: "peace is better." As for the avarice of the soul, it is that the servant halts at his own portion; for whoever is veiled from the contemplation of the Real is handed back to the contemplation of his self. And godfearing (taqwā) amounts to "melting away into the vision of your Lord, beyond the vision of your own rank."

mots.racine: ن ش ز
ص ل ح
ش ح ح

versets.ar: وَلَن تَسْتَطِيعُوا أَن تَعْدِلُوا بَيْنَ النِّسَاءِ وَلَوْ حَرَصْتُمْ ۖ فَلَا تَمِيلُوا كُلَّ الْمَيْلِ فَتَذَرُوهَا كَالْمُعَلَّقَةِ ۚ وَإِن تُصْلِحُوا وَتَتَّقُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-lan tastaṭīʿū an taʿdilū bayna n-nisāʾi wa-law ḥaraṣtum fa-lā tamīlū kulla l-mayli fa-tadharūhā ka-l-muʿallaqati wa-in tuṣliḥū wa-tattaqū fa-inna llāha kāna ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: You will not be able to be perfectly equitable among your wives, even should you so desire. Yet do not turn away from one of them entirely, leaving her as though suspended. If you maintain concord and fear God, know that He is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the verse a spiritual law: when your uprightness is troubled in your affairs, the state turns against you, and the mending of your bonds becomes corruption; but when you stand by God in your affairs, life is brought into balance and your time is cleared of all disturbance. Above all this pearl on trial: "when the path of your personal portions closes upon you, He opens for you the contemplation of His truth and the finding of His kindness; for whoever's loss is in God, the Real Himself is his substitute."

mots.racine: ع د ل
mots.def: ʿadala: to act with justice, balance.

mots.racine: ع ل ق
mots.def: muʿallaqa: suspended, neither free nor wedded.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.def: ghafūr: who covers faults; raḥīm: merciful.

versets.ar: وَإِن يَتَفَرَّقَا يُغْنِ اللَّهُ كُلًّا مِّن سَعَتِهِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ وَاسِعًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-in yatafarraqā yughni llāhu kullan min saʿatihi wa-kāna llāhu wāsiʿan ḥakīman
versets.trad: And if the two should part, God will enrich each of them out of His abundance. For God is all-embracing, wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises human parting toward the one enduring bond: the only companionship one cannot do without is that of the heart with its perpetual need of God, for of Him one cannot do without; creatures, for their part, need one another only in appearance — an illusion of the people of dispersion; but for the people of the Reality, the need of all creation reverts entirely to God. So, whether they part or unite, it is from His abundance alone (saʿa) that they are filled.

mots.racine: ف ر ق
mots.def: tafarraqa: to divide, to break apart.

mots.racine: و س ع
و س ع / ح ك م
mots.def: wāsiʿ: of infinite amplitude; ḥakīm: wise.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ وَلَقَدْ وَصَّيْنَا الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ أَنِ اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ ۚ وَإِن تَكْفُرُوا فَإِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَنِيًّا حَمِيدًا
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-la-qad waṣṣaynā lladhīna ūtū l-kitāba min qablikum wa-iyyākum ani ttaqū llāha wa-in takfurū fa-inna li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-kāna llāhu ghaniyyan ḥamīdan
versets.trad: To God belongs all that is in the heavens and on the earth. We have indeed enjoined upon those who received the Book before you, and upon yourselves: "Fear God!" Yet if you disbelieve... [it matters not in the end, for] to God belongs all that is in the heavens and on the earth. And God is self-sufficient, worthy of all praise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He charged all of mankind to return to Him, to turn aside from all that is other than He, and to hold fast to His command; yet one company was assisted and another forsaken. Then He discloses to the people of the Reality what dissolves all pretension: "He has no need of the obedience of any friend and remains untouched by the straying of any deviant." God's self-sufficiency (ghaniyy) strips from the soul the illusion of bringing Him anything.

mots.racine: و ص ي
و ق ي
mots.def: ittaqā: to guard oneself through piety.

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح م د
mots.def: ghaniyy: who needs nothing; ḥamīd: greatly praised.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-kafā bi-llāhi wakīlan
versets.trad: To God belongs all that is in the heavens and on the earth. And God suffices as a guardian!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He severed the secrets of their attachment to others by making them know that He alone possesses what is in the heavens and on the earth, then He gave them hope in the beauty of His taking-charge and His support in all their needs, through the grace of His kindness and His perfect sufficiency: "God suffices as a warrant" — it befits you to entrust your case to Him, for He governs your state without ever diverting what is yours.

mots.racine: ك ف ي
و ك ل
mots.def: wakīl: the one to whom one commits one's affairs.

versets.ar: إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ وَيَأْتِ بِآخَرِينَ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ قَدِيرًا
versets.tr: in yashaʾ yudhhibkum ayyuhā n-nāsu wa-yaʾti bi-ākharīna wa-kāna llāhu ʿalā dhālika qadīran
versets.trad: If He will, He can make you pass away, O mankind, and bring forth others in your place. God is fully able to do so!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He who dispensed with you in His pre-eternity has no need of you in His eternity; He depends on no one, while the servant cannot do without Him for a single breath. And again: "His possibles have no limit: in default of ʿAmr, here is Zayd; in default of one servant, here are others; but He who has neither substitute nor replacement is the One, the Singular."

mots.racine: ذ ه ب
mots.def: adhhaba: to carry off, to annihilate.

mots.racine: أ خ ر
ق د ر

versets.ar: مَّن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ثَوَابَ الدُّنْيَا فَعِندَ اللَّهِ ثَوَابُ الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ سَمِيعًا بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: man kāna yurīdu thawāba d-dunyā fa-ʿinda llāhi thawābu d-dunyā wa-l-ākhirati wa-kāna llāhu samīʿan baṣīran
versets.trad: Whoever desires the reward of this world, let him know that with God is the reward of this world and of the world to come. And God is all-hearing, all-seeing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because they had bound their hearts to the fleeting things of the lower world, He reminded them of the matter of the hereafter, making them know that above their base ambitions there is something higher: the delights of the world to come. And when their aims rose toward the hereafter, He severed them from every created and bounded object by His word: "And God is better and more lasting." Every aspiration finds its truth by widening until it reaches Him.

mots.racine: ر و د / ث و ب
mots.sens: desires the reward of this world
mots.def: thawāb: requital; dunyā: the near world.

mots.racine: أ خ ر
س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.def: samīʿ: who hears all; baṣīr: who sees all.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ بِالْقِسْطِ شُهَدَاءَ لِلَّهِ وَلَوْ عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَوِ الْوَالِدَيْنِ وَالْأَقْرَبِينَ ۚ إِن يَكُنْ غَنِيًّا أَوْ فَقِيرًا فَاللَّهُ أَوْلَىٰ بِهِمَا ۖ فَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا الْهَوَىٰ أَن تَعْدِلُوا ۚ وَإِن تَلْوُوا أَوْ تُعْرِضُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū kūnū qawwāmīna bi-l-qisṭi shuhadāʾa li-llāhi wa-law ʿalā anfusikum awi l-wālidayni wa-l-aqrabīna in yakun ghaniyyan aw faqīran fa-llāhu awlā bihimā fa-lā tattabiʿū l-hawā an taʿdilū wa-in talwū aw tuʿriḍū fa-inna llāha kāna bi-mā taʿmalūna khabīran
versets.trad: O you who believe! Be firm upholders of equity, witnesses before God, even though it be against yourselves, or your parents, or your near kin; and whether the matter concern a rich man or a poor one, God is closer to both. So do not let passion lead you, lest you be unjust; but if you prevaricate, or turn away from bearing witness, know that God is well aware of all you do.
themes: preferring the right of the Real
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the station: al-qisṭ is equity, and to stand by God is to render to each his due by discharging God's rights upon yourself and claiming His rights from everyone over whom you have authority. But he lays down the foundation: "the root of Religion is to prefer the right of the Real to the right of the creature; whoever prefers anyone to God — father, mother, child, kinsman — or retains for himself any portion short of Him, remains a stranger to the fulfilment of equity."

mots.racine: ق و م / ق س ط
mots.def: qawwām: who rises to uphold; qisṭ: justice.

mots.racine: ش ه د
ت ب ع / ه و ي
mots.def: hawā: the inclination of the soul.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا آمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالْكِتَابِ الَّذِي نَزَّلَ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ وَالْكِتَابِ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِاللَّهِ وَمَلَائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلَالًا بَعِيدًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū āminū bi-llāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-l-kitābi lladhī nazzala ʿalā rasūlihi wa-l-kitābi lladhī anzala min qablu wa-man yakfur bi-llāhi wa-malāʾikatihi wa-kutubihi wa-rusulihi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri fa-qad ḍalla ḍalālan baʿīdan
versets.trad: O you who believe! Have faith in God and His Messenger, in the Book He has sent down upon His Messenger and in the Scripture He sent down before. Whoever disbelieves in God, His angels, His Books, His messengers and the Last Day has strayed far from the truth.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī raises faith by degrees: "O you who believe by proof, believe now by clarification, until you believe by unveiling and direct vision (kashf and ʿiyān)." Deeper still: "O you who believe, believe with the certainty that your salvation rests upon His grace, not upon your faith." Finally: "believe beyond all union and all separation, all finding and all loss" — for the Pure Self-Sufficiency of God is too holy for any nearness and any distance.

mots.racine: أ م ن
ك ت ب
ض ل ل / ب ع د
mots.def: ḍalāl: loss of the way; baʿīd: profound.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا ثُمَّ آمَنُوا ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا ثُمَّ ازْدَادُوا كُفْرًا لَّمْ يَكُنِ اللَّهُ لِيَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ وَلَا لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū thumma kafarū thumma āmanū thumma kafarū thumma zdādū kufran lam yakuni llāhu li-yaghfira lahum wa-lā li-yahdiyahum sabīlan
versets.trad: Those who, having believed, then disbelieved, then believed again, and then relapsed into disbelief, growing only more entrenched in it — them God will not forgive, nor will He guide them upon any path.
themes: the seal of the evil end
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those whose states never ceased to lurch about — they rise then fall, recover then are sealed upon evil — are those whom the majesty of Omnipotence has shattered by decree, whom the misfortune of the apportioning has seized in their end and in their state. The Real guides them toward no upright aim nor leads them to any restoration; so He announces to them eternal separation and chastisement without end. The heart's instability, left to itself, seals straying.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ك ف ر
mots.def: the alternation of faith and denial.

mots.racine: ز ي د / ك ف ر
ه د ي / س ب ل
mots.sens: to guide them upon a path

versets.ar: بَشِّرِ الْمُنَافِقِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا
versets.tr: bashshiri l-munāfiqīna bi-anna lahum ʿadhāban alīman
versets.trad: Give tidings to the hypocrites that a painful chastisement awaits them.
versets.commentaire: The tidings (bishāra), ordinarily glad news, here turn into a bitter warning for the hypocrites. Qushayrī: they are those whose fortune was overturned by the misfortune of the apportioning, weaned from restoration by the evil end; the Real announces to them eternal rupture and unending pain. The word of joy becomes a sentence — a way of disclosing that the appearance of faith without its reality saves from nothing.

mots.racine: ب ش ر
ن ف ق
ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَتَّخِذُونَ الْكَافِرِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِن دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۚ أَيَبْتَغُونَ عِندَهُمُ الْعِزَّةَ فَإِنَّ الْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا
versets.tr: alladhīna yattakhidhūna l-kāfirīna awliyāʾa min dūni l-muʾminīna a-yabtaghūna ʿindahumu l-ʿizzata fa-inna l-ʿizzata li-llāhi jamīʿan
versets.trad: They take the disbelievers as friends rather than the believers. Do they seek glory in their company? Glory, in truth, belongs wholly to God.
versets.commentaire: To seek might among the deniers is to mistake the source: ʿizza belongs wholly to God. Qushayrī makes of the heart's alliances an unveiling of secrets: "the clearest proof of a man's inmost being is the company he chooses and the confidant he keeps; every like is bound to its like, and the branch unfolds according to its root." To seek glory elsewhere than in God is to attach oneself to a root that collapses.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ل ي
mots.sens: they take the disbelievers as allies

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ع ز ز
ج م ع

versets.ar: وَقَدْ نَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الْكِتَابِ أَنْ إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ يُكْفَرُ بِهَا وَيُسْتَهْزَأُ بِهَا فَلَا تَقْعُدُوا مَعَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَخُوضُوا فِي حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِ ۚ إِنَّكُمْ إِذًا مِّثْلُهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ جَامِعُ الْمُنَافِقِينَ وَالْكَافِرِينَ فِي جَهَنَّمَ جَمِيعًا
versets.tr: wa-qad nazzala ʿalaykum fī l-kitābi an idhā samiʿtum āyāti llāhi yukfaru bihā wa-yustahzaʾu bihā fa-lā taqʿudū maʿahum ḥattā yakhūḍū fī ḥadīthin ghayrihi innakum idhan mithluhum inna llāha jāmiʿu l-munāfiqīna wa-l-kāfirīna fī jahannama jamīʿan
versets.trad: He has indeed sent down to you in the Book that when you hear the signs of God being denied and mocked, you are not to sit with them until they turn to some other discourse; otherwise you would become like them. Truly, God will gather the hypocrites and the disbelievers together in Gehenna.
versets.commentaire: On "you would then be like them," Qushayrī teaches the danger of company: "the clearest proof of a man's inmost being is the company of whoever accompanies him and the familiarity of whoever becomes his confidant; for like is bound fast to like, and the branch proceeds from its stock." To sit beside the mockery of the signs is to take up its very sap. Gehenna will gather in a single crucible hypocrites and deniers alike.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ه ز أ
mots.def: istahzaʾa: to hold up to derision.

mots.racine: خ و ض
mots.sens: until they enter (upon some other talk)
mots.def: khāḍa: to plunge into a discourse.

mots.racine: ج م ع

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَتَرَبَّصُونَ بِكُمْ فَإِن كَانَ لَكُمْ فَتْحٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ قَالُوا أَلَمْ نَكُن مَّعَكُمْ وَإِن كَانَ لِلْكَافِرِينَ نَصِيبٌ قَالُوا أَلَمْ نَسْتَحْوِذْ عَلَيْكُمْ وَنَمْنَعْكُم مِّنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۚ فَاللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۗ وَلَن يَجْعَلَ اللَّهُ لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: alladhīna yatarabbaṣūna bikum fa-in kāna lakum fatḥun mina llāhi qālū a-lam nakun maʿakum wa-in kāna li-l-kāfirīna naṣībun qālū a-lam nastaḥwidh ʿalaykum wa-namnaʿkum mina l-muʾminīna fa-llāhu yaḥkumu baynakum yawma l-qiyāmati wa-lan yajʿala llāhu li-l-kāfirīna ʿalā l-muʾminīna sabīlan
versets.trad: Such as lie in wait, watching you. If a victory comes to you from God, they say: "Were we not with you?" And if the disbelievers gain some share, they say: "Did we not have the upper hand over you, and shield you from the believers?" God will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection, and God will never grant the disbelievers a way over the believers.
themes: the double game of the hypocrites
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: lacking all sincerity in the Reality and having tasted no savour of the faith they claimed, they were set apart from the Muslims by the decree and severed from the disbelievers by name alone. Then God guarantees His own a fair sufficiency by "God will never grant the deniers a way over the believers": "the recoil of their cunning is turned back upon them, the wage of their guile suspended over their heads; the true, on the side of the Real, sees its people succoured, and the false, by the victory of the Real, sees its root torn out."

mots.racine: ر ب ص
mots.sens: they lie in wait for you
mots.def: tarabbaṣa: to watch, to await the chance.

mots.racine: ف ت ح
ج ع ل / س ب ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ يُخَادِعُونَ اللَّهَ وَهُوَ خَادِعُهُمْ وَإِذَا قَامُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ قَامُوا كُسَالَىٰ يُرَاءُونَ النَّاسَ وَلَا يَذْكُرُونَ اللَّهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: inna l-munāfiqīna yukhādiʿūna llāha wa-huwa khādiʿuhum wa-idhā qāmū ilā ṣ-ṣalāti qāmū kusālā yurāʾūna n-nāsa wa-lā yadhkurūna llāha illā qalīlan
versets.trad: The hypocrites seek to deceive God, but it is He who deceives them. When they rise to prayer, they rise sluggishly, to be seen by men, and they remember God but little.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dismantles the twofold deceit: the hypocrites' cunning is to display agreement in the way while harbouring associationism in their belief; the Real's cunning toward them is what they imagined to be salvation, what they claimed for themselves as election — until the veil is lifted: then they will have the certainty that what they took for drink was but a mirage. And the unfailing mark: "the sign of hypocrisy is briskness when the creature looks on, and languor of desire the moment another's gaze withdraws."

mots.racine: خ د ع
ك س ل
ر أ ي

versets.ar: مُّذَبْذَبِينَ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ لَا إِلَىٰ هَٰؤُلَاءِ وَلَا إِلَىٰ هَٰؤُلَاءِ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: mudhabdhabīna bayna dhālika lā ilā hāʾulāʾi wa-lā ilā hāʾulāʾi wa-man yuḍlili llāhu fa-lan tajida lahu sabīlan
versets.trad: Wavering between the two, belonging neither to these nor to those. For whomever God leads astray, you will find no way.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "the most wretched of men is he who casts off the mantle of servanthood without finding a way to the reality of freedom: he has of the Real no least share of glory, nor in heedlessness any happy life." This wavering (tadhbīb) is the torment of one who belongs neither to God nor to himself: suspended between two banks, he comes to land on neither, and the one led astray by divine decree remains without a path.

mots.racine: ذ ب ذ ب
mots.def: mudhabdhab: who vacillates between two poles.
neither to one side nor the other.

mots.racine: ض ل ل
mots.def: aḍalla: to let one go astray.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَتَّخِذُوا الْكَافِرِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِن دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۚ أَتُرِيدُونَ أَن تَجْعَلُوا لِلَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطَانًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū lā tattakhidhū l-kāfirīna awliyāʾa min dūni l-muʾminīna a-turīdūna an tajʿalū li-llāhi ʿalaykum sulṭānan mubīnan
versets.trad: O you who believe! Do not take the disbelievers as friends rather than the believers. Would you furnish God with a manifest warrant against you?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He repeated the exhortation to them and confirmed the command to set themselves apart from the enemies, in an excess of warning and severity of dissuasion. Then he unveils its gravity: "to prefer another to the Worshipped, to prefer another to the Beloved, is among the greatest sins in the laws of love; if to fill with a stranger a place of the heart reserved for the believers already calls down chastisement, what then of filling with a stranger the place of the heart that belongs to the Real?"

mots.racine: أ خ ذ
mots.def: ittakhadha: to adopt, to take as.

mots.racine: و ل ي
س ل ط / ب ي ن

versets.ar: إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الْأَسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ وَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُمْ نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: inna l-munāfiqīna fī d-darki l-asfali mina n-nāri wa-lan tajida lahum naṣīran
versets.trad: The hypocrites shall dwell in the lowest depth of the Fire, and you will find for them no helper —
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the verse proves that the hypocrite is in no security, for faith is what procures safety; the believer is saved from the Fire by his faith, so that which casts him into the lowest depth cannot be faith. And this grave saying: "they earned the abyss of the Fire because here below they kept company with God's supreme Name without the way of sacred reverence"; they are already, in this world, in the depth of the veil. The fate of the hereafter only prolongs the present state.

mots.racine: د ر ك / س ف ل
mots.def: dark: a degree; asfal: the most inferior.

mots.racine: ن و ر
ن ص ر

versets.ar: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا وَأَصْلَحُوا وَاعْتَصَمُوا بِاللَّهِ وَأَخْلَصُوا دِينَهُمْ لِلَّهِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ مَعَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ وَسَوْفَ يُؤْتِ اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: illā lladhīna tābū wa-aṣlaḥū wa-ʿtaṣamū bi-llāhi wa-akhlaṣū dīnahum li-llāhi fa-ulāʾika maʿa l-muʾminīna wa-sawfa yuʾti llāhu l-muʾminīna ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: save those who repent, amend their ways, hold fast to God, and render their religion sincerely to God; for those shall be reckoned with the believers. And God will grant the believers a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the subtlety of "with the believers" rather than "among": He set for the return of the hypocrites four conditions that He set for no other's repentance, so grave was their state in disbelief; then He says "those are with the believers," not "of the believers" — a sign of the lowliness of their rank, even when overtaken by sincerity. And he defines holding fast (iʿtiṣām): "to cling to God is to declare oneself quit of one's own strength and one's own power"; and the sincere religion is to know that "their salvation rests upon the grace of their Lord, not upon their faith."

mots.racine: ت و ب / ص ل ح
mots.def: tāba: to return; aṣlaḥa: to set right.

mots.racine: ع ص م
mots.def: iʿtaṣama: to cling, to seek refuge.

mots.racine: خ ل ص / د ي ن
mots.def: akhlaṣa: to purify, to devote; dīn: religion.

versets.ar: مَّا يَفْعَلُ اللَّهُ بِعَذَابِكُمْ إِن شَكَرْتُمْ وَآمَنتُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ شَاكِرًا عَلِيمًا
versets.tr: mā yafʿalu llāhu bi-ʿadhābikum in shakartum wa-āmantum wa-kāna llāhu shākiran ʿalīman
versets.trad: What would God do with your chastisement, if you are thankful and have faith? God is ever-thankful, all-knowing!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī holds this verse to be one of those that impose fair hope and the strength of expectation: He made two light things — gratitude and faith — the signs of safety; shukr is word, īmān is state; how greatly He has lightened the way for the servant by being content with his word and his state! And he unveils that God makes Himself "thankful": "the Lord thanks the servant, that is, praises him by mentioning his fair deed, all while knowing that he has many sins"; nay, "He thanks him while knowing his weakness, and knowing that he does not sin out of any purpose to oppose Him, but through the grip of human states."

mots.racine: ف ع ل / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: what would God do with your chastisement

mots.racine: ش ك ر / أ م ن
mots.def: shakara: to give thanks; āmana: to believe.

mots.racine: ش ك ر / ع ل م
mots.def: shākir: who accepts and praises; ʿalīm: who knows all.

versets.ar: لَّا يُحِبُّ اللَّهُ الْجَهْرَ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ إِلَّا مَن ظُلِمَ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ سَمِيعًا عَلِيمًا
versets.tr: lā yuḥibbu llāhu l-jahra bi-s-sūʾi mina l-qawli illā man ẓulima wa-kāna llāhu samīʿan ʿalīman
versets.trad: God does not love the open utterance of evil speech, save from one who has been wronged; God is all-hearing, all-knowing.
themes: the guarding of the inmost self
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the verse: "whoever knows that his Master hears is ashamed to utter most of what his soul inclines him to"; nay, "the evil uttered is also what your soul lets you hear it murmuring within you against the creatures, for the elect are held to account for what they converse within themselves, where the common folk are not even troubled." And this elevation: "whoever contemplates the creatures through the eye of their attachment to the Real — that they are servants of God — no longer unfurls upon them the tongue of blame."

mots.racine: ج ه ر / س و أ
mots.def: jahr: open enunciation; sūʾ: evil, offence.

mots.racine: ظ ل م
mots.sens: save one who has been wronged
mots.def: ẓulima: to be a victim of injustice.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م
mots.def: samīʿ: who hears; ʿalīm: who knows.

versets.ar: إِن تُبْدُوا خَيْرًا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ أَوْ تَعْفُوا عَن سُوءٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَفُوًّا قَدِيرًا
versets.tr: in tubdū khayran aw tukhfūhu aw taʿfū ʿan sūʾin fa-inna llāha kāna ʿafuwwan qadīran
versets.trad: If you do good, whether openly or in secret, or if you pardon an evil, then God is ever-pardoning, all-powerful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the three gestures: "to show forth the good is to adorn yourself with the fair manners of the Law; to conceal it is to realize yourself in the truths of the Reality; to pardon an evil is to take from God that to which He invites you of noble character." And the promise in mirror: "God is pardoning of your defects, and all-powerful to realize what you love and to accomplish what you seek." Pardon out of generosity, and you will find His pardon over what you yourself have committed — for your faults are the more numerous.

mots.racine: ب د و / خ ي ر
mots.def: abdā: to make apparent; khayr: good.

mots.racine: خ ف ي
ع ف و / ق د ر
mots.def: ʿafuww: who effaces faults; qadīr: powerful.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَن يُفَرِّقُوا بَيْنَ اللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَيَقُولُونَ نُؤْمِنُ بِبَعْضٍ وَنَكْفُرُ بِبَعْضٍ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَن يَتَّخِذُوا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ سَبِيلًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna yakfurūna bi-llāhi wa-rusulihi wa-yurīdūna an yufarriqū bayna llāhi wa-rusulihi wa-yaqūlūna nuʾminu bi-baʿḍin wa-nakfuru bi-baʿḍin wa-yurīdūna an yattakhidhū bayna dhālika sabīlan
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve in God and His messengers, or who would draw a distinction between God and His messengers, saying: "We believe in some of them but disbelieve in others," and who seek to take a middle way —
versets.commentaire: To seek "a middle way" between faith and denial, to believe in some messengers and deny others, is to rupture the unity of belief. Qushayrī teaches that indivisible faith refuses fragmentation: "just as the faith of one who does not extend it to all the times of his life is not received, so the faith of one who does not extend it to all in whom belief was commanded is not received"; for this totality is set down as the condition of its reality and its perfection.

mots.racine: ف ر ق / ر س ل
mots.sens: to separate God from His messengers

mots.racine: أ م ن / ك ف ر / ب ع ض
mots.sens: we believe in one part and deny another

mots.racine: س ب ل

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْكَافِرُونَ حَقًّا ۚ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا
versets.tr: ulāʾika humu l-kāfirūna ḥaqqan wa-aʿtadnā li-l-kāfirīna ʿadhāban muhīnan
versets.trad: those are the disbelievers in truth. And for the disbelievers We have prepared a degrading chastisement.
versets.commentaire: The "true denial" (al-kāfirūna ḥaqqan) is the one that masks itself with a partial faith. Qushayrī: He made known that they had added to the ugliness of their disbelief what is counted among their blameworthy deeds, then He doubled for them the chastisement that was the wage of their crime, that it might be known that He lies in wait for the people of corruption. The "degrading" torment (muhīn) answers the pride of one who fancied himself raised up by sifting the revelation.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ح ق ق
ع ت د
ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَلَمْ يُفَرِّقُوا بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُمْ أُولَٰئِكَ سَوْفَ يُؤْتِيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ ۗ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū bi-llāhi wa-rusulihi wa-lam yufarriqū bayna aḥadin minhum ulāʾika sawfa yuʾtīhim ujūrahum wa-kāna llāhu ghafūran raḥīman
versets.trad: But those who believe in God and His messengers, making no distinction between any of them — to those God will give their rewards. And God is forgiving, merciful!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because they believed in all the messengers and assented to all that was commanded them, they merited acceptance and the beauty of the reward. And he lays down the law of totality through two images of the Prophet: "the pilgrimage is ʿArafa" — whoever crosses the distance from the farthest pass yet remains short of ʿArafāt by a hair's breadth has not performed the ḥajj; and "the slave under a contract of manumission remains a slave so long as a single dirham of his price remains." Thus faith is saved only when whole.

mots.racine: ف ر ق
أ ح د
أ ت ي / أ ج ر
mots.sens: He will give them their wages

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُكَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ أَن تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ كِتَابًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ ۚ فَقَدْ سَأَلُوا مُوسَىٰ أَكْبَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ فَقَالُوا أَرِنَا اللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ بِظُلْمِهِمْ ۚ ثُمَّ اتَّخَذُوا الْعِجْلَ مِن بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ فَعَفَوْنَا عَن ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَآتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ سُلْطَانًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: yasʾaluka ahlu l-kitābi an tunazzila ʿalayhim kitāban mina s-samāʾi fa-qad saʾalū mūsā akbara min dhālika fa-qālū arinā llāha jahratan fa-akhadhathumu ṣ-ṣāʿiqatu bi-ẓulmihim thumma ttakhadhū l-ʿijla min baʿdi mā jāʾathumu l-bayyinātu fa-ʿafawnā ʿan dhālika wa-ātaynā mūsā sulṭānan mubīnan
versets.trad: The People of the Book ask you to bring down upon them a Book from heaven. They had already asked of Moses something even greater, saying: "Show us God plainly!", and the thunderbolt struck them for their iniquity. Later they adopted the calf, after clear proofs had come to them. We pardoned them that, and We gave Moses an incontestable authority.
versets.commentaire: On the "manifest authority" granted, Qushayrī opens an eschatological vista for the community: "the manifest authority of this community, tomorrow, will be their subsistence in the state of the encounter" — and he cites the Prophet's word: "you will not be jostled in the vision of Him." What the People of the Book demanded as an outward wonder, the way accomplishes as an intimate unveiling: not a book fallen from heaven, but the promised face-to-face.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ج ه ر
mots.def: arā: to make see; jahra: in the open.

mots.racine: ص ع ق
س ل ط / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَهُمُ الطُّورَ بِمِيثَاقِهِمْ وَقُلْنَا لَهُمُ ادْخُلُوا الْبَابَ سُجَّدًا وَقُلْنَا لَهُمْ لَا تَعْدُوا فِي السَّبْتِ وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُم مِّيثَاقًا غَلِيظًا
versets.tr: wa-rafaʿnā fawqahumu ṭ-ṭūra bi-mīthāqihim wa-qulnā lahumu dkhulū l-bāba sujjadan wa-qulnā lahum lā taʿdū fī s-sabti wa-akhadhnā minhum mīthāqan ghalīẓan
versets.trad: We raised the Mount above them as a token of the covenant made with them, and We said to them: "Enter the gate prostrating!" We said to them also: "Do not transgress the Sabbath!" And We took from them a solemn pledge.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: each time We added to them an outward sign, they added in their hearts only denial and repudiation; the increase of their share of warning availed them nothing, since the eyes of their hearts did not open to contemplate it. And he cites: "what avail the signs and the warners to a people who do not believe?" The Mount upraised, the gate, the Sabbath: so many proofs that a closed heart does not receive.

mots.racine: ر ف ع / ط و ر
mots.sens: We raised the Mount above them

mots.racine: د خ ل / س ج د
و ث ق / غ ل ظ

versets.ar: فَبِمَا نَقْضِهِم مِّيثَاقَهُمْ وَكُفْرِهِم بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَقَتْلِهِمُ الْأَنبِيَاءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَقَوْلِهِمْ قُلُوبُنَا غُلْفٌ ۚ بَلْ طَبَعَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهَا بِكُفْرِهِمْ فَلَا يُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا
versets.tr: fa-bimā naqḍihim mīthāqahum wa-kufrihim bi-āyāti llāhi wa-qatlihimu l-anbiyāʾa bi-ghayri ḥaqqin wa-qawlihim qulūbunā ghulfun bal ṭabaʿa llāhu ʿalayhā bi-kufrihim fa-lā yuʾminūna illā qalīlan
versets.trad: [They incurred Our wrath] because they broke their covenant, because they denied the signs of God, because they slew the prophets unjustly, and because they said: "Our hearts are uncircumcised!" Nay, God set a seal upon their hearts on account of their disbelief, so that they do not believe, save a few of them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the spiral of abandonment: the succession of disobediences befell them, state after state, for among the chastisements of sins is one: the forsaking that delivers one over to further transgressions. Having broken the pact without repenting, they were dragged to the denial of the signs; by the calamity of that denial, they were forsaken until they slew their prophets; then, by the calamity of that, they grew bold enough to claim understanding, saying: "our hearts are receptacles of sciences." God answered them by sealing their hearts, veiling them from the place of knowledge, and they strayed in their blindness.

mots.racine: ن ق ض / و ث ق
ق ل ب / غ ل ف
mots.def: ghulf: covered with a membrane, closed.

mots.racine: ط ب ع
mots.sens: God set a seal upon them

versets.ar: وَبِكُفْرِهِمْ وَقَوْلِهِمْ عَلَىٰ مَرْيَمَ بُهْتَانًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-bi-kufrihim wa-qawlihim ʿalā maryama buhtānan ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: [We punished them] because they disbelieved, and because they uttered against Mary a monstrous calumny,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of Mary the mirror of two opposing strayings: overstepping is straying, just as deficiency and falling short of the right are straying: one people calumniated Mary and accused her of debauchery, another overstepped all measure by deifying her, saying that her son is the son of God — and both camps fell into wandering. He extends the lesson to the saints of God: "whoever denies them perishes by abandoning their reverence, and whoever ascribes to them what they do not merit perishes by excess in their exaltation."

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ق و ل
ب ه ت / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَقَوْلِهِمْ إِنَّا قَتَلْنَا الْمَسِيحَ عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَمَا قَتَلُوهُ وَمَا صَلَبُوهُ وَلَٰكِن شُبِّهَ لَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ لَفِي شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ ۚ مَا لَهُم بِهِ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِلَّا اتِّبَاعَ الظَّنِّ ۚ وَمَا قَتَلُوهُ يَقِينًا
versets.tr: wa-qawlihim innā qatalnā l-masīḥa ʿīsā bna maryama rasūla llāhi wa-mā qatalūhu wa-mā ṣalabūhu wa-lākin shubbiha lahum wa-inna lladhīna khtalafū fīhi la-fī shakkin minhu mā lahum bihi min ʿilmin illā ttibāʿa ẓ-ẓanni wa-mā qatalūhu yaqīnan
versets.trad: and because they said: "We have surely slain the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, the Messenger of God." Yet they did not slay him, nor did they crucify him, but so it was made to seem to them. Those who differ about it are in doubt concerning it; they have no sure knowledge of it, but only follow conjecture. Assuredly, they did not slay him,
themes: the ransom of the loving soul
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports the tradition of the substitution and draws from it a parable of love: it is said that Jesus asked: "who will accept that my likeness be cast upon him, to be slain in my place? Paradise shall be his"; one of his companions consented. When he endured the trial of loss, he was not deprived of God's substitute, according to: "We do not let perish the wage of one who acts with excellence." Better still: "his companionship with Jesus being sealed by his soul, he accompanied him by his spirit: when Jesus was raised to the station of drawing-near, the spirit of the one who had ransomed himself was raised to the station of nearness."

mots.racine: ق ت ل / م س ح
ش ب ه
mots.sens: it was made to seem so to them

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ظ ن ن

versets.ar: بَل رَّفَعَهُ اللَّهُ إِلَيْهِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: bal rafaʿahu llāhu ilayhi wa-kāna llāhu ʿazīzan ḥakīman
versets.trad: rather, God raised him up to Himself. And God is mighty, wise.
versets.commentaire: The raising (rafʿ) of Jesus to God seals the foregoing parable: to the one who made himself his ransom, the spirit was raised to the station of nearness (qurba), while Jesus was raised to the station of drawing-near (zulfa). Qushayrī sees in this the law of love: whoever dies in fidelity to the beloved is raised together with him. And God remains ʿAzīz, beyond the reach of the hands that thought to seize, Ḥakīm in the ordering of this mystery.

mots.racine: ر ف ع
mots.sens: God raised him up to Himself

mots.racine: ع ز ز
ح ك م

versets.ar: وَإِن مِّنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ إِلَّا لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ بِهِ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِ ۖ وَيَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيدًا
versets.tr: wa-in min ahli l-kitābi illā la-yuʾminanna bihi qabla mawtihi wa-yawma l-qiyāmati yakūnu ʿalayhim shahīdan
versets.trad: There is none among the People of the Book but must believe in him before his death, and on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness against them.
themes: the faith of the last hour
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from belated faith a decisive teaching: since He has decreed that there is no safety for them at the time of despair, faith in that hour no longer profits them; we know, then, that what counts is being made safe by the Real, not the faith of the servant. Belief wrenched from the death-agony, when the veil is torn, does not save: only the amān granted from on high avails, not the īmān that surges too late.

mots.racine: أ م ن
mots.sens: he will surely believe in him

mots.racine: م و ت
ش ه د

versets.ar: فَبِظُلْمٍ مِّنَ الَّذِينَ هَادُوا حَرَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ طَيِّبَاتٍ أُحِلَّتْ لَهُمْ وَبِصَدِّهِمْ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ كَثِيرًا
versets.tr: fa-bi-ẓulmin mina lladhīna hādū ḥarramnā ʿalayhim ṭayyibātin uḥillat lahum wa-bi-ṣaddihim ʿan sabīli llāhi kathīran
versets.trad: Because of the iniquity of those who became Jews, and because they often turned others away from the path of God, We forbade them good things that had formerly been lawful for them;
themes: the sin that bars the lawful
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the outward sanction: "to commit the forbidden brings on the prohibition of the lawful"; thus the one who engages in a manifest transgression finds himself deprived of the lawful states he had enjoyed and of the subtle graces (alṭāf) that flowed into his inmost being. The privation of "good things" is not merely dietary: it is the withdrawal of the intimate gifts, the hidden consequence of open sin.

mots.racine: ظ ل م
ح ر م / ط ي ب
ص د د / س ب ل
mots.sens: by their turning away from the path of God

versets.ar: وَأَخْذِهِمُ الرِّبَا وَقَدْ نُهُوا عَنْهُ وَأَكْلِهِمْ أَمْوَالَ النَّاسِ بِالْبَاطِلِ ۚ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَافِرِينَ مِنْهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-akhdhihimu r-ribā wa-qad nuhū ʿanhu wa-aklihim amwāla n-nāsi bi-l-bāṭili wa-aʿtadnā li-l-kāfirīna minhum ʿadhāban alīman
versets.trad: and because they practised usury though it had been forbidden them, and because they devoured the wealth of others unjustly. For those among them who do not believe We have prepared a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links this verse to the one before: transgression draws down the prohibition of what was lawful; whoever ventures into the forbidden is stripped of the licit graces he once enjoyed, and of the lights that were dawning in his innermost secret. Usury and the unjust devouring of others' wealth are not mere economic faults: they veil the heart. The "painful punishment" reserved for the disbelievers among them is but the inscribed consequence of a heart that has preferred greed to uprightness.

mots.racine: ر ب و
mots.def: ribā: usurious interest, an unlawful increase of wealth.

mots.racine: ب ط ل
mots.def: bāṭil: the vain, the false, the illegitimate, the opposite of ḥaqq.

versets.ar: لَّٰكِنِ الرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ مِنْهُمْ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَا أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ ۚ وَالْمُقِيمِينَ الصَّلَاةَ ۚ وَالْمُؤْتُونَ الزَّكَاةَ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ أُولَٰئِكَ سَنُؤْتِيهِمْ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا
versets.tr: lākini r-rāsikhūna fī l-ʿilmi minhum wa-l-muʾminūna yuʾminūna bi-mā unzila ilayka wa-mā unzila min qablika wa-l-muqīmīna ṣ-ṣalāta wa-l-muʾtūna z-zakāta wa-l-muʾminūna bi-llāhi wa-l-yawmi l-ākhiri ulāʾika sanuʾtīhim ajran ʿaẓīman
versets.trad: But to those among them who are firmly rooted in Knowledge, who are believers, who believe in what has been revealed to you and in what was revealed before you, to those who keep up the prayer, who give the alms, who believe in God and the Last Day — to those We shall grant a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes the one "firmly rooted in Knowledge" (al-rāsikh fī l-ʿilm). The one rooted is not he who imitates in proof any more than he imitates in judgement: he sets his gaze in its place until he reaches a point where doubt finds no passage into his reason. Higher still: he is the one who rises above the mere contemplation of the argument and attains the realities of self-evidence. And he relates this saying: "Whoever acts upon what he knows, God bequeaths to him knowledge of what he did not know." Prayer is singled out among the works because it follows īmān in most passages of the Qur'an; and the "magnificent reward" is the one that exceeds any measure earned by the act.

mots.racine: ر س خ / ع ل م
mots.def: rāsikh: firmly rooted, whose knowledge does not waver.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ع ظ م
mots.def: ajr ʿaẓīm: a recompense surpassing the merit of the deed.

versets.ar: إِنَّا أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ كَمَا أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ نُوحٍ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ مِن بَعْدِهِ ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْبَاطِ وَعِيسَىٰ وَأَيُّوبَ وَيُونُسَ وَهَارُونَ وَسُلَيْمَانَ ۚ وَآتَيْنَا دَاوُودَ زَبُورًا
versets.tr: innā awḥaynā ilayka kamā awḥaynā ilā nūḥin wa-n-nabiyyīna min baʿdihi wa-awḥaynā ilā ibrāhīma wa-ismāʿīla wa-isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba wa-l-asbāṭi wa-ʿīsā wa-ayyūba wa-yūnusa wa-hārūna wa-sulaymāna wa-ātaynā dāwūda zabūran
versets.trad: We have inspired you as We inspired Noah and the prophets who came after him. We inspired Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, the Tribes, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron and Solomon, and to David We gave the Psalms.
versets.commentaire: The revelation granted to Muḥammad joins that of the prophets who preceded him: a single Source, a single Word, poured out according to the ages. Qushayrī dwells on why Noah is named apart, then Muḥammad apart: each was set alone according to the rank he deserved — one among his peers without great favours, the other among his own with a thousand favours. They meet in the singularity of election yet differ according to the measure of degree. Inspiration unites the envoys; their station sets them apart.

mots.racine: و ح ي
mots.def: awḥā: to send down a secret divine communication.

mots.racine: ز ب ر
mots.def: zabūr: the Book revealed to David.

versets.ar: وَرُسُلًا قَدْ قَصَصْنَاهُمْ عَلَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ وَرُسُلًا لَّمْ نَقْصُصْهُمْ عَلَيْكَ ۚ وَكَلَّمَ اللَّهُ مُوسَىٰ تَكْلِيمًا
versets.tr: wa-rusulan qad qaṣaṣnāhum ʿalayka min qablu wa-rusulan lam naqṣuṣhum ʿalayka wa-kallama llāhu mūsā taklīman
versets.trad: We inspired messengers whose story We have already recounted to you, and other messengers whose story We have not recounted to you. And God spoke to Moses with a direct speech.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a spiritual law from this verse: the custom of God toward His intimates is to veil some and to make others manifest; thus it is with the prophets, whose names of some He brought to light while He left the detail of others unsaid. So too with the states of His servants: God unveils one part and covers another. What He has made manifest, He demands sincerity in it; what He has veiled, it is out of jealousy over their hearts, to preserve them from contemplating themselves. And "God spoke to Moses with a true speech": the announcement of his election to hear His word without intermediary.

mots.racine: ق ص ص
mots.sens: We recounted their story to you
mots.def: qaṣṣa: to narrate, to recount the account.

mots.racine: ك ل م
mots.sens: He spoke with a true speech
mots.def: taklīm: an intensifying maṣdar, stressing the direct reality of the divine discourse.

versets.ar: رُّسُلًا مُّبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ لِئَلَّا يَكُونَ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَى اللَّهِ حُجَّةٌ بَعْدَ الرُّسُلِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: rusulan mubashshirīna wa-mundhirīna li-allā yakūna li-n-nāsi ʿalā llāhi ḥujjatun baʿda r-rusuli wa-kāna llāhu ʿazīzan ḥakīman
versets.trad: We inspired messengers bearing glad tidings and warning, so that men might have no argument against God after the coming of the messengers. God is mighty, wise.
versets.commentaire: God has set the creatures before their measure: He sent them messengers, and henceforth no objection remains. Qushayrī then raises a question that overturns the meaning: how could one who has need of God hold an argument against God? Yet God spoke to them according to the reach of their understandings. The "proof" that man believes he can invoke collapses before the evidence of his own indigence: the servant does not plead against the One on whom he depends for his very existence.

mots.racine: ب ش ر
mots.def: mubashshir: one who gladdens by the announcement of good.

mots.racine: ح ج ج
mots.def: ḥujja: a pretext or proof advanced as an excuse.

versets.ar: لَّٰكِنِ اللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ بِمَا أَنزَلَ إِلَيْكَ ۖ أَنزَلَهُ بِعِلْمِهِ ۖ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ يَشْهَدُونَ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدًا
versets.tr: lākini llāhu yashhadu bi-mā anzala ilayka anzalahu bi-ʿilmihi wa-l-malāʾikatu yashhadūna wa-kafā bi-llāhi shahīdan
versets.trad: But God bears witness to what He has revealed to you: He revealed it with His Knowledge. The angels too bear witness. And God suffices as a witness.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads this verse as a word of consolation. God soothes His envoy from the denial of the creatures by reminding him that He Himself knows his truthfulness; this is why He says: "God suffices as a witness." When the Real attests, the denial of men loses all weight. The servant who knows that his Lord is witness to his sincerity has no further need of the recognition of others: the gaze of the One stands in place of every gaze.

mots.racine: ش ه د
mots.def: shahida: to bear witness, to attest a truth.

mots.racine: ك ف ي / ش ه د
mots.def: a formula of sufficiency: God alone suffices to attest.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ قَدْ ضَلُّوا ضَلَالًا بَعِيدًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū wa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīli llāhi qad ḍallū ḍalālan baʿīdan
versets.trad: Those who have disbelieved and turned others away from the path of God have fallen into a far-reaching error.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the gravity of turning others aside: He made the act of barring believers from following the Real the equal of disbelief in God, for God has magnified the rights of His intimates as He has magnified His own right. To bar another from the way of God is no minor fault appended to unbelief: it is an assault upon what God most cherishes, and the "far" error measures the distance thus opened.

mots.racine: ص د د
mots.def: ṣadda ʿan: to prevent, to keep away from the path.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ع د
mots.def: ḍalāl baʿīd: extreme straying from guidance.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَظَلَمُوا لَمْ يَكُنِ اللَّهُ لِيَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ وَلَا لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ طَرِيقًا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū wa-ẓalamū lam yakuni llāhu li-yaghfira lahum wa-lā li-yahdiyahum ṭarīqan
versets.trad: Truly, those who have disbelieved and acted unjustly — God will not forgive them, nor will He guide them upon any path, save
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that God made their injustice the road to their disbelief and bound to the two together the perpetual chastisement. Then he qualifies: injustice, even if it is not like disbelief as to the threat of eternity, nonetheless bears a baleful omen; it is not excluded that God may abandon the unjust one until he comes to Him in disbelief. Ẓulm, by its darkness, can drag the heart toward denial: no iniquity is harmless.

mots.racine: ظ ل م
mots.def: ẓalama: to commit iniquity, to set a thing out of its place.

mots.racine: ه د ي
mots.def: hadā: to lead upon the right path.

versets.ar: إِلَّا طَرِيقَ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرًا
versets.tr: illā ṭarīqa jahannama khālidīna fīhā abadan wa-kāna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīran
versets.trad: the path of Gehenna, in which they shall abide for ever. And that is easy for God.
versets.commentaire: The only way open to one who has joined iniquity to denial is that of Gehenna, an everlasting abode. And "that is easy for God": nothing in His requital costs Him, neither grace nor justice. In Qushayrī's reading, this verse seals the triptych 167-169 like a sentence beyond appeal: he who has barred himself from the way of God has, before him, only a single path — and God expends no effort in letting him go down it.

mots.racine: خ ل د
mots.def: khālid: everlasting, that subsists without end.

mots.racine: ي س ر
mots.def: yasīr: that which demands no exertion.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَكُمُ الرَّسُولُ بِالْحَقِّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ فَآمِنُوا خَيْرًا لَّكُمْ ۚ وَإِن تَكْفُرُوا فَإِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu qad jāʾakumu r-rasūlu bi-l-ḥaqqi min rabbikum fa-āminū khayran lakum wa-in takfurū fa-inna li-llāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-kāna llāhu ʿalīman ḥakīman
versets.trad: O mankind! The Envoy has come to you with the Truth from your Lord. Believe, then — it is better for you. And if you disbelieve, [what of it, since] to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and on the earth. God is all-knowing, wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lets the self-sufficiency of God be heard: He has announced that He has no need of them; if they believe, it is their own shares they win, and if they disbelieve, it is their own calamities they draw upon themselves. The Real is too pure for any faith to increase Him or any refusal to diminish Him. And "if you disbelieve, to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth" means: even if they step out of the exercise of servanthood through their deeds, they do not step out of the reality of being His servants by creation — for "there is none in the heavens or on the earth but comes to the All-Merciful as a servant."

mots.racine: ح ق ق
mots.def: ḥaqq: the real, the true, that which is founded in right.

mots.racine: أ م ن / خ ي ر
mots.sens: believe — it is better for you
mots.def: khayran: accusative of exhortation, "it will be better."

versets.ar: يَا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ لَا تَغْلُوا فِي دِينِكُمْ وَلَا تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَّ ۚ إِنَّمَا الْمَسِيحُ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَكَلِمَتُهُ أَلْقَاهَا إِلَىٰ مَرْيَمَ وَرُوحٌ مِّنْهُ ۖ فَآمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ ۖ وَلَا تَقُولُوا ثَلَاثَةٌ ۚ انتَهُوا خَيْرًا لَّكُمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا اللَّهُ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ ۖ سُبْحَانَهُ أَن يَكُونَ لَهُ وَلَدٌ ۘ لَّهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ وَكِيلًا
versets.tr: yā ahla l-kitābi lā taghlū fī dīnikum wa-lā taqūlū ʿalā llāhi illā l-ḥaqqa innamā l-masīḥu ʿīsā bnu maryama rasūlu llāhi wa-kalimatuhu alqāhā ilā maryama wa-rūḥun minhu fa-āminū bi-llāhi wa-rusulihi wa-lā taqūlū thalāthatun intahū khayran lakum innamā llāhu ilāhun wāḥidun subḥānahu an yakūna lahu waladun lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-kafā bi-llāhi wakīlan
versets.trad: O People of the Book! Do not commit excess in your religion, and say nothing of God but the truth. The Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, is only the envoy of God, His Word which He cast into Mary, and a Spirit from Him. Believe, then, in God and in His envoys. Do not say "Three!"; cease — it will be better for you. God is but one God! Glory be to Him: how should He have a son? To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and on the earth. And God suffices as a guardian!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unmasks excess (ghuluww) as a derangement of a reason that takes itself for the measure: their immoderation in religion was to run according to their own surmises, describing their Adored One in the likeness of the creatures; then they contradicted themselves, saying that the One is three and that the three are one. And he concludes: to persist in the false adds nothing but more falsehood. The verse restores the truth laid bare: the Messiah is a servant, a Word cast into Mary, a Spirit issuing from God — and God, glory be to Him, remains the One above all sonship.

mots.racine: غ ل و
mots.def: ghalā: to overstep the measure, to be immoderate.

mots.racine: ك ل م
mots.def: kalima: here, the creative Word "Be" cast into Mary.

mots.racine: و ك ل
mots.def: wakīl: the One to whom one entrusts oneself entirely.

versets.ar: لَّن يَسْتَنكِفَ الْمَسِيحُ أَن يَكُونَ عَبْدًا لِّلَّهِ وَلَا الْمَلَائِكَةُ الْمُقَرَّبُونَ ۚ وَمَن يَسْتَنكِفْ عَنْ عِبَادَتِهِ وَيَسْتَكْبِرْ فَسَيَحْشُرُهُمْ إِلَيْهِ جَمِيعًا
versets.tr: lan yastankifa l-masīḥu an yakūna ʿabdan li-llāhi wa-lā l-malāʾikatu l-muqarrabūna wa-man yastankif ʿan ʿibādatihi wa-yastakbir fa-sayaḥshuruhum ilayhi jamīʿan
versets.trad: The Messiah does not disdain to be a servant of God, nor do the angels who are brought near to Him. Those who disdain to serve Him and grow arrogant — God will soon gather them all before Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of servanthood a blazon, not a shame: how should he disdain His servitude, when it is by it that he is honoured? How should he take pride in lowering himself, when in his pride lies his ruin? This is why the Messiah, as soon as he spoke, said: "I am the servant of God"; and it becomes servants well to abase themselves before their Masters. As for the mention of the angels brought near, Qushayrī notes that it does not indicate that they are superior to the Messiah: God was addressing the people according to their beliefs, for they held the angels to be higher than the sons of Adam.

mots.racine: ن ك ف
mots.sens: he disdains, refuses out of pride
mots.def: istankafa: to hold beneath oneself, to refuse with disdain.

mots.racine: ق ر ب
mots.def: muqarrab: admitted into the divine proximity.

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ وَيَزِيدُهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِ ۖ وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ اسْتَنكَفُوا وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا فَيُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا وَلَا يَجِدُونَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا
versets.tr: fa-ammā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti fa-yuwaffīhim ujūrahum wa-yazīduhum min faḍlihi wa-ammā lladhīna stankafū wa-stakbarū fa-yuʿadhdhibuhum ʿadhāban alīman wa-lā yajidūna lahum min dūni llāhi waliyyan wa-lā naṣīran
versets.trad: As for those who have believed and done righteous deeds, He will pay them their reward in full and add to them of His grace. And as for those who disdained [to serve] and grew arrogant, He will inflict on them a painful punishment, and they will find, apart from God, neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the "painful punishment" a subtle depth, beyond the fire: the most searing torment is that they never reach unto Him, after they have known His majesty; when their knowledge becomes inescapable, they will know that they have remained far from Him — and their regrets, then, will be the harshest of chastisements. The true pain is not suffering but the definitive distance from the One once glimpsed. For the believers, conversely, the full reward is still overflowed by the "surplus" of pure grace.

mots.racine: و ف ي
mots.def: waffā: to give one's due in its entirety.

mots.racine: ز ي د / ف ض ل
mots.sens: He adds to them of His grace
mots.def: faḍl: a gratuitous bounty exceeding the merit.

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ جَاءَكُم بُرْهَانٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَأَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكُمْ نُورًا مُّبِينًا
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā n-nāsu qad jāʾakum burhānun min rabbikum wa-anzalnā ilaykum nūran mubīnan
versets.trad: O mankind! A proof has already come to you from your Lord, and We have sent down upon you a manifest light.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorises the "proof": the burhān is that which shines forth in their innermost secrets among the witnesses of the Real. It is not merely an outward argument, but a clarity that rises within the heart. And the "manifest light": it is His address, in the meditation of which their inner sight is realised. The Qur'an does not illumine from without like a lamp set before the eyes; it opens the inner eye by which one sees. Proof and light are less received than unveiled.

mots.racine: ب ر ه ن
mots.def: burhān: a luminous demonstration, an irrefutable evidence.

mots.racine: ن و ر / ب ي ن
mots.def: nūr mubīn: a clarity that shows itself and makes one see.

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَاعْتَصَمُوا بِهِ فَسَيُدْخِلُهُمْ فِي رَحْمَةٍ مِّنْهُ وَفَضْلٍ وَيَهْدِيهِمْ إِلَيْهِ صِرَاطًا مُّسْتَقِيمًا
versets.tr: fa-ammā lladhīna āmanū bi-llāhi wa-ʿtaṣamū bihi fa-sayudkhiluhum fī raḥmatin minhu wa-faḍlin wa-yahdīhim ilayhi ṣirāṭan mustaqīman
versets.trad: As for those who have believed in Him and held fast to Him, God will admit them into a Mercy from Him and a Grace, and He will guide them toward Himself upon a straight path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the "soon" (the letter sīn) of "He will admit them": it announces the future, that is, He will preserve their faith at the end, at the instant of death, just as He honoured them with knowledge and faith in the present. Then, on "He will guide them toward Himself upon a straight path," he unveils the pure gratuity: this guidance is the honour of letting them know that it comes from God in sheer favour, not because they earned it by their seeking and their striving, nor by their toil and their weariness.

mots.racine: ع ص م
mots.def: iʿtaṣama: to cling, to seek refuge and protection.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م
mots.def: ṣirāṭ mustaqīm: the straight road leading to God.

versets.ar: يَسْتَفْتُونَكَ قُلِ اللَّهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِي الْكَلَالَةِ ۚ إِنِ امْرُؤٌ هَلَكَ لَيْسَ لَهُ وَلَدٌ وَلَهُ أُخْتٌ فَلَهَا نِصْفُ مَا تَرَكَ ۚ وَهُوَ يَرِثُهَا إِن لَّمْ يَكُن لَّهَا وَلَدٌ ۚ فَإِن كَانَتَا اثْنَتَيْنِ فَلَهُمَا الثُّلُثَانِ مِمَّا تَرَكَ ۚ وَإِن كَانُوا إِخْوَةً رِّجَالًا وَنِسَاءً فَلِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۗ يُبَيِّنُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ أَن تَضِلُّوا ۗ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: yastaftūnaka quli llāhu yuftīkum fī l-kalālati ini mruʾun halaka laysa lahu waladun wa-lahu ukhtun fa-lahā niṣfu mā taraka wa-huwa yarithuhā in lam yakun lahā waladun fa-in kānatā thnatayni fa-lahumā th-thuluthāni mimmā taraka wa-in kānū ikhwatan rijālan wa-nisāʾan fa-li-dh-dhakari mithlu ḥaẓẓi l-unthayayni yubayyinu llāhu lakum an taḍillū wa-llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: They ask you for a ruling [concerning inheritance]. Say: "God instructs you concerning the one who leaves no direct heir: if a man dies leaving no children but a sister, half of what he leaves is hers; and he inherits from her if she has no children. If there are two sisters, two thirds of what he leaves are theirs. And if there are brothers and sisters, let the male receive a share equal to that of two females." God makes it clear to you, lest you go astray. And God has knowledge of all things.
themes: the wisdom of the prescribed division
versets.commentaire: To close the sura, Qushayrī reads in the precision of the shares a wisdom that defuses covetousness: He settled the dispute between them over the division of the inheritance by what He made plain of the legal text, for wealth is dear to man and souls are kneaded with avarice; had He not fixed the measures of what is due, rivalries would have flared up in interpretation, leading to snatching and grappling; so He cut all that off by fixing the portions, severing the quarrel at its root. He adds two subtleties: the granting of a share to women, even though they do not defend the clan, shows God's regard for their weakness; and the double share of the men answers to the burden of expenses that falls upon them, to the effort of acquiring wealth and of providing for the women.

mots.racine: ف ت ي
mots.def: istaftā: to ask for a legal opinion (fatwā).

mots.racine: ك ل ل
mots.def: kalāla: the deceased with neither ascendant nor direct descendant.

mots.racine: ح ظ ظ / أ ن ث
mots.sens: a share equal to that of two females
mots.def: ḥaẓẓ: the portion assigned in the inheritance.
